visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Sunday: 9 AM to 7 PM
editorial_summary:
Master Care Centre (MCC) is a multi-branch private nursing care group headquartered in Johor Bahru with over a decade of operation. The branch listed here — MCC@Yahya at Jalan Hassan Al-Attas, Bandar Johor Bahru — serves as the group's original base, focused on nursing care, post-surgery recovery, and stroke rehabilitation for elderly residents. MCC positions itself as a clinically oriented care provider rather than a simple old folks home, and its service mix reflects that: skilled nursing, physiotherapy-linked rehabilitation, long-term residential care, and memory care support for residents with dementia or Alzheimer's are all offered across the group's locations.

With more than ten years in the market and a Google rating of 4.8 from its reviewer base, MCC@Yahya carries a strong track record in elder care circles. The group runs three JB branches — Yahya (Bandar JB), Abad (Taman Abad), and Daya (Taman Daya) — sharing the same management team, clinical standards, and contact number (+60 11-1080 1805), which allows families to enquire about placement across all sites simultaneously. The clinical emphasis on post-surgical recovery and stroke rehab makes this branch particularly relevant for elders transitioning out of hospital.

Bed count and pricing are not published online, so prospective families will need to call or visit to assess current availability and monthly fees. The shared phone number across all three branches means enquiries are handled centrally — it is worth specifying which location you are enquiring about. Questions to ask include the nurse-to-resident ratio on night shifts, whether the facility can manage PEG feeding or oxygen dependency, and what the process is for residents whose care needs escalate after admission.
address: 8, Jalan Hassan Al-Attas, Bandar Johor Bahru, 80100 Johor Bahru, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

editorial_summary:
Loving Mansion Care Centre occupies a cluster of shoplots on Jalan Kota 2/3 in Kawasan Perindustrial Kota Puteri, Masai, about 15 minutes from JB CIQ. Registered as CT0113053-M, the operator reports more than 20 years in elderly care, with day-to-day operations led by a manager named Alan who is repeatedly mentioned by name in family reviews. The home is bilingual in practice (English and Mandarin) and serves a predominantly Chinese-Malaysian resident base.

Services listed include assisted living, full nursing care, palliative care, dementia and memory care, and stroke rehabilitation. Two complementary therapies are offered on-site that are unusual for the segment: acupuncture and physiotherapy, presented as part of the routine care plan rather than paid add-ons. Residents receive 24-hour nursing coverage, home-cooked meals and a daily activities programme. Google reviews (5★ across 17 ratings) consistently flag the cleanliness of the premises and professionalism of the nursing team.

Loving Mansion is well-suited to families wanting nursing-grade care with built-in rehab and TCM-style therapy in one place — stroke recovery and chronic-pain elderly residents in particular benefit from the acupuncture/physio combination. The operator does not publish bed count, pricing, RN-to-resident ratio, or licence type on the website, so confirm capacity, monthly rates by room type, and whether acupuncture is delivered by a registered TCM practitioner before committing.

title: FCC Family Care Centre Johor Bahru
pricing_display: Day Care: RM 200/day | Basic from RM 3,500 | Intermediate from RM 3,800 | Advanced from RM 4,300 — published on operator website
care_types: Nursing Home + Day Care + Assisted Living + Rehabilitation
services: meals, laundry, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, transport, activities, dementia_care, palliative
visiting_hours: Monday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: 11 AM to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
FCC Family Care Centre is a JKM-licensed senior care facility in Ulu Tiram, Johor Bahru, operating from a campus at 17 Jalan Resam, Taman Bukit Tiram. The centre runs two distinct residential units: the CHS Home (36 beds, named after its first resident Mdm. Chia Hui Siam), which accommodates residents from independent to fully bed-bound, and the dedicated MemoryCare Home (16 beds) for residents diagnosed with dementia. Together the two homes provide 52 licensed beds. FCC also offers day care, post-operative recovery stays, and a home-based mobile care service for residents who prefer to remain in their own homes.

The operator's website lists the following care categories with published monthly fees: Basic Care (standard nursing, ADL assistance, medication management) from RM 3,500; Intermediate Care (adds dementia care and fall management) from RM 3,800; Advanced Care (includes palliative care for cancer, Parkinson's, and coma conditions, plus physiotherapy and occupational therapy) from RM 4,300; Home-Based Care from RM 4,300; and Day Care at RM 200 per day. Meals are provided five times daily (breakfast, lunch, high tea, dinner, supper) and are included in residential packages. The published staff-to-resident ratio is 1:6, with nurses on duty 24 hours and a bi-monthly doctor visit schedule plus an on-call emergency doctor. The campus deploys SmartPeep, a vision AI monitoring system that provides 24/7 fall detection and staff alerts using privacy-blurred imaging rather than live video recording.

The natural setting is a genuine differentiator: the compound includes an organic farm and edible garden whose produce goes to resident meals, outdoor areas for walking and birdwatching, a greenhouse, gazebo, and farm animals used in animal-assisted therapy sessions. FCC's Instagram feed (@fccfamilycarecentre, nearly 500 posts) shows regular resident activities — ketupat weaving, table-tennis coordination games, garden harvests, and school-community visits. Reviews on Google Maps are consistently positive about cleanliness, outdoor space, and attentive front-of-house staff; one older review flagged that viewing inside rooms requires a prior appointment and that walk-ins may only see the compound. Families should schedule a formal visit in advance via WhatsApp to see both homes and confirm room availability. On visit: confirm the room type and care tier that fits your relative's current clinical needs, as the pricing tiers differ meaningfully across the three care levels.

**Licence & verification**
- JKM Registered — licence number: J/PJBWT066/2024 (valid to 04 December 2029).

**What to ask on your visit**
- Which home (CHS or MemoryCare) suits my relative's current needs, and can I see both?
- What does the 1:6 staffing ratio look like on a night shift versus a day shift?
- How are care tier upgrades (e.g. Basic to Advanced) handled if needs change?
- Is the SmartPeep system active in all rooms or only common areas?
- When was the last JKM inspection, and can I see the result?
address: 17, Jalan Resam, Taman Bukit Tiram, 81800 Ulu Tiram, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

editorial_summary:
Multicare Nursing Home opened its Johor Bahru branch in August 2025 — the chain's first expansion outside the Klang Valley. The parent home in Petaling Jaya has roughly 32 years of operating history, which makes Multicare one of the longer-established private nursing groups in Malaysia. The JB site sits in the western Johor Bahru metro (Skudai/Iskandar Puteri area); the operator's published material describes a fully residential nursing facility rather than a light assisted-living setup, with day and night caregiver coverage and an on-call doctor for emergencies.

The chain's published service scope covers assisted living, elderly care, nursing care, stroke rehabilitation, post-operative care, palliative care, dementia care and respite care, with 24-hour registered nurses and caregivers, medication administration, physiotherapy, occupational therapy activities, and oxygen and ventilator support where required. Multicare publishes a full pricing tier list — uncommon among Malaysian nursing homes — at RM 3,500/month for a VIP single room, RM 3,000 for a single, RM 2,800 for 2-bed sharing, RM 2,500 for 3-bed, RM 2,300 for 4-bed, RM 2,000 for 5-bed and RM 1,800 for 6-bed. All tiers are described as AC rooms with TV and Wifi, an attached bathroom, meals delivered to the room and a personal caregiver included. Treat those as starting rates — the actual monthly fee will scale with dependency level, PEG/oxygen needs, wound care and any extras, and chain-wide rates are always worth re-confirming on the call for the specific branch.

Useful things to confirm before placing: the on-duty nurse-to-resident ratio for both day and night, broken out between RNs and caregivers; how many current residents are bed-bound or PEG-fed (those skew workload heavily); halal and dietary handling; language coverage among caregivers; the JKM licence number for the JB premises; and the exact street address and neighbourhood, since the operator hasn't yet published the JB branch address on its main site.
address: 13, Jalan Harimau Akar, Taman Abad, 80250 Johor Bahru, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

Anzaletta Living Care Centre is a licensed private nursing home at 9, Jalan Gangsa 1, Taman Kolam Air, Johor Bahru, operating for close to two decades. Anzaletta is categorised as offering nursing home, assisted living, and rehabilitation services. Rehabilitation programmes are offered specifically for stroke patients and for those undergoing dialysis — both of which indicate a willingness to manage residents with moderate to high care needs. Capacity and monthly fees are not published on the facility's website, so families will need to contact the team directly (016-681 1765 or 010-664 2170) to discuss availability and pricing. With roughly two decades in operation and a Google rating of 4.7, the centre has an established presence in the JB private care market. services: meals, nursing_care, physio, activities, dementia_care, palliative
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Genesis Life Care JB is the Johor Bahru branch of the Genesis Life Care chain, which operates several centres around peninsular Malaysia. The JB centre opened in April 2025 and sits on Level 6 of Holiday Plaza Tower, Jalan Dato Sulaiman in Taman Century — a vertical, building-based footprint rather than a landed bungalow conversion. The operator advertises 120-plus beds at this address and a 20-plus-strong care team; both figures are operator claims and have not been independently verified.

The website lists a broad service mix — 24/7 nursing, dementia and memory care, post-stroke rehab, palliative and end-of-life care, post-operative recovery, respite, and a senior daycare option. The operator also states weekly on-site doctor consultations and a psychologist-led dementia programme; treat both as marketing claims pending verification at the visit. Packages are advertised from RM 2,500 per month with halal dining available. Google reviews stand at 4.4 across 14 entries, against a wider chain rating closer to 4.8.

A Level-6 mall-tower address running a stated 120-bed footprint is unusual for the JB segment — most homes of that size are in landed buildings or purpose-built blocks, not retail-tower floors. That alone makes an in-person walkthrough essential before any deposit. On the visit, ask which JKM licence number applies to this specific tower-floor address, count the beds and rooms yourself rather than relying on the brochure, and ask the day-shift charge nurse for the actual nurse-to-resident ratio across day and night. Get the visiting doctor's full name and the days they round, ask the same of the psychologist, and pin down what the RM 2,500 floor rate excludes — diapers, feeds, physio sessions, and consumables are commonly billed on top.
address: Level 6, Holiday Plaza Tower, Jalan Dato Sulaiman, Taman Century, 80250 Johor Bahru, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

services: meals, laundry, nursing_care, doctor_visit, transport, activities, dementia_care, palliative
visiting_hours: Monday: 9:30 AM to 8 PM; Tuesday: 9:30 AM to 8 PM; Wednesday: 9:30 AM to 8 PM; Thursday: 9:30 AM to 8 PM; Friday: 9:30 AM to 8:30 PM; Saturday: 9:30 AM to 8:30 PM; Sunday: 9:30 AM to 8 PM
editorial_summary:
Evergreen ElderCare Centre has been operating from Taman Johor in JB since 2014 and is one of the few homes in the state that publicly anchors its dementia care in a Montessori-for-Dementia framework — a structured approach that uses meaningful daily activities to maintain function rather than relying on sedation or pure custodial care. The centre also runs a programme it calls Greening Dementia. Communication on the site is in English and Mandarin.

The model includes 24-hour nursing, respite stays, and short-term rehabilitation. The operator advertises daily on-site physician presence — verify what "daily" means in practice, because the term covers everything from a full-day staff doctor to a brief ward-round to an on-call arrangement labelled "daily". A genuine staff doctor at a small JB home is unusual and a real differentiator if it holds up; a daily ward-round is still meaningful; an on-call arrangement is closer to the JB norm. The operators describe an integrated approach mixing eastern and western medical practice. Specific capacity is not published. Google reviews sit at 4.8 across 16 entries.

For families dealing with moderate-to-advanced dementia who want more than custodial care — particularly Mandarin-speaking families in JB — Evergreen is a strong shortlist option. Visit during daytime hours and ask to observe a Montessori session in progress rather than just being shown the materials, then check the night-shift staffing, because structured-activity programmes only work if there are enough hands during the day to deliver them. Pin down the doctor question with specifics: name, hours on site, days per week, and whether they review charts or just see escalations. Also ask how the home handles resistive behaviours and whether antipsychotic use is openly discussed at family meetings.
address: PTD 4648 A Jalan Banjaran 10/5 Kg Kempas Baru skudai, Kempas Banjaran, 81200 Johor Bahru, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

editorial_summary:
EHA Elder Care Home Kluang is the sole non-Johor Bahru branch of the EHA Eldercare Group (1478860-U), a multi-branch award-winning eldercare operator. Set in Taman Dato' Amar Diraja at No. 3, Jalan Sheikh Kadir Ahmad, Kluang, it is the closest professionally managed private care option for families in the Kluang district who need a residential, day care, or short-stay solution. EHA markets itself as 24-hour senior care managed by a team of doctors, nurses, and physiotherapists — an unusually high professional density for a town-level centre.

The Kluang branch carries the same service menu as the JB group homes: 24-hour assisted living, respite care from one to six months, day care (11 hours, five days a week), dementia care, post-hospital rehabilitation, and physiotherapy. Pricing published on the EHA website starts at RM 2,300 per month for assisted living, RM 80 per day for respite, and RM 1,300 per month for day care — making it meaningfully more affordable than comparable EHA branches in JB. The centre operates Monday to Sunday, 9am to 6pm for enquiries, while residential care runs around the clock. Contact is via phone +6019-713 3697 or email ask.ehagroup@gmail.com, and the official website ehaeldercare.com.my has full package details in both English and Chinese.

Languages spoken include English and Mandarin Chinese. The Facebook page (ehakluang, 661 followers) notes the Kluang branch by its Chinese name 'Warm Home' (温馨老家). The facility's JKM licensing claim appears in both the Google listing and the facility name itself; families should request the specific JKM licence number on-site to verify. Reviewers on Google have praised the management team by name, and KKM reportedly rates EHA Kluang among the best in the district. Email enquiries can be directed to ask.ehagroup@gmail.com.

**What to ask on visit**
- Can you show us the current JKM licence certificate for this branch?
- What is the current all-in monthly rate for a bed-bound resident?
- How many registered nurses are on duty overnight?
- Is physiotherapy in-house daily or on a scheduled weekly basis?
- What is the emergency transfer protocol to Kluang Hospital?
- What languages do the daily care staff speak for residents who do not speak Mandarin?
address: 3, Jalan Sheikh Kadir Ahmad, Taman Dato’ Amar Diraja, 86000 Kluang, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

title: Noble Care Centre
services: meals, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, activities, dementia_care, palliative
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd. is one of the larger private nursing home chains in Malaysia, operating 16-plus centres across Klang Valley, Penang, Ipoh, Seremban and Johor Bahru. The JB branch this listing refers to is at 17 Jalan Tan Hack Kiat, off Jalan Tampoi, with enquiry lines on +60 16-278 6993 and +60 16-778 6795. The chain is registered as Noble Care (M) Sdn Bhd (registration 201001020128).

The published service scope is broad — respite care, palliative and end-of-life care, dementia care, stroke and cancer support, handicapped adult and child care, plus home nursing on the side. The chain also advertises specific clinical capabilities not always published by smaller homes: bedsore dressing, NG tube changes, colostomy bag changes, Foley's catheter care, tracheostomy care, and post-operative care, alongside physiotherapy. Read this list as advertised capability rather than confirmed delivery: which procedures are performed routinely on-site versus referred out varies by branch, and tracheostomy care in particular is a high-skill task that should not be assumed at the same level as bag and catheter changes (which most caregivers can be trained to do). At the Tampoi branch, ask explicitly which of these are done in-house, by whom, and how often. A separate luxury retirement living arm sits above the standard nursing footprint.

The chain footprint suits families who want operational scale, broader clinical scope on paper, and the option to move a relative between branches if circumstances change. Quality at the Tampoi branch should still be assessed on its own — chain-level marketing does not equal branch-level practice. On the visit, ask for the specific JKM licence for the Tampoi address, request a real-time look at the day and night nurse-to-resident ratio (not the brochure number), confirm the all-in monthly rate including consumables, and if tracheostomy or NG care is part of why you are looking here, ask to meet the named nurse who would actually perform it.
address: 17, Jalan Tan Hack Kiat, New Village, Jalan Tampoi, Taman Tampoi, 81200 Johor Bahru, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

editorial_summary:
Green Acres Elderly Care Centre, operated by Green Acres Residence Care (Group) Sdn. Bhd., has been running since 1998 and is one of the longer-established private nursing homes in Johor Bahru. The main branch sits at 50, Jalan Bukit Kempas 4/3 in Taman Bukit Kempas — a quiet residential neighbourhood about 20 minutes from the city centre via the EDL highway — with a secondary location at 2A-2B, Jalan Intan, Kim Teng Park. The bungalow-style setting is deliberately low-rise and domestic in feel, catering to aged and physically disabled residents who benefit from a calm, less clinical atmosphere.

The centre's stated care model rests on a multidisciplinary team: on-call doctors, a physiotherapist, registered nurses, and nursing aides, all supervised by a resident Home Manager. Physiotherapy and rehabilitation programmes are offered alongside standard nursing care, placing the facility in the nursing home plus rehabilitation category. The proximity to Hospital Permail (roughly 10 minutes away) is a meaningful safety net for residents who may need acute hospital backup. The group operates two JB addresses, suggesting the capacity to serve families from different parts of the city. Contact is via +60 11-5991 9916 or gacarecentre50@gmail.com.

Because the website does not publish bed counts, pricing, or shift-specific nurse ratios, families should go into a site visit with those questions prepared. It is also worth confirming which care conditions — dementia, PEG feeding, tracheostomy — the team is equipped to manage, and whether the rehabilitation programme follows a structured timetable or is delivered on request. Visiting hours are listed on the website as 11 am to 6 pm daily.
address: 50 & 52, 50, Jalan Bukit Kempas 4/3, Taman Bukit Kempas, 81200 Johor Bahru, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

title: Lee Nursing
visiting_hours: 9am–12pm and 4pm–7pm daily
editorial_summary:
Lee Nursing Home (李护理疗养院) is a small, Chinese family-run facility in Taman Nesa, Skudai, operating since 2011. Run by the How family — Mr Richard How handles administration while Mdm Lee (a nurse) leads clinical care — it positions itself as a round-the-clock nursing home rather than a simple old-folk shelter. Services are meaningfully clinical: Ryle's tube feeding, tracheal suctioning, catheter changes, wound care, vital-sign monitoring, and blood-glucose checks are all offered, alongside meals, personal hygiene, laundry, and transport to clinic or hospital appointments. The same management has since opened a second site — Hibiscus Serenity Care Centre in Larkin, JB — suggesting growing operational confidence.

The facility's 3-star Google rating from just two reviews is a meaningful caution flag. Two reviews is too thin a sample to be statistically reliable, but a midpoint score indicates at least one reviewer had a poor or neutral experience. Neither the website (leenursinghome.com) nor any public directory publishes pricing; families must call or WhatsApp for a quote. No JKM licence number or Ministry of Health registration code has been publicly confirmed.

Address inconsistencies across directories (No. 7, 11, and 26 appear against different street names in Taman Nesa) warrant direct verification before visiting. Visiting hours are 9am–12pm and 4pm–7pm daily. Families considering Lee Nursing Home should visit in person, ask for the JKM or MOH licence, confirm the current address, and weigh the below-average Google score against the clinical depth the home claims — including the same management team's track record at Hibiscus Serenity Care Centre.
address: 7, Jalan Joget 8, Taman Nesa, 81300 Skudai, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

title: Lotus Ville Care Centre
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: Closed
editorial_summary:
Lotus Ville Care Centre Sdn Bhd is a private nursing home registered under Malaysia's Private Healthcare Facilities and Services Act (Act 586), operating from Taman Johor Jaya in Johor Bahru. Incorporated in April 2022, it is a relatively young facility that has earned membership in AGECOPE — the Association for Residential Aged Care Operators of Malaysia — signalling commitment to industry standards in a sector where many operators remain unaffiliated. Despite the .org.my domain, the company is registered as a private limited company (Sdn Bhd). AGECOPE confirms services include assisted living, palliative care, and dementia care, with staff described as trained in geriatrics, physiotherapy, and nursing care.

A Facebook presence notes a Dr Thurgadevi as a contact, suggesting at least periodic doctor involvement. The 4-star rating from 8 Google reviews reflects steady community satisfaction, reasonable for a facility less than three years old. Two addresses appear in aggregator data — 67, Jalan Anggerik 20, Taman Johor Jaya (the more frequently cited, likely main site) and No. 1, Jalan Camar 3, Taman Perling — which may indicate a second branch or a data discrepancy that families should verify directly before visiting.

No pricing has been published publicly — quote-on-request model consistent with the broader Malaysian nursing home market. No religious affiliation has been identified; the lotus branding appears to be aesthetic rather than Buddhist or denominational. Families should call +60 17-737 1233 to confirm the current address, pricing, RN night coverage, and halal meal availability before making a placement decision.
address: 67, Jalan Anggerik 20, Taman Johor Jaya Johor Jaya, Taman Johor Jaya, 81800 Johor Bahru, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

editorial_summary:
Yong An Old Folks Care Centre (荣安养老院) sits on Jalan Berlian 4 in Taman Gunung Emas, Ulu Tiram, on the eastern edge of Johor Bahru. The home has been operating since 2013 under registration JM0650487-K and runs primarily in Mandarin and English, reflecting a largely Chinese-speaking resident base. Its location is elevated, which the operator notes helps with airflow and odour control — a small but practical detail that matters in long-term residential care.

The core offering is full-board residential care: four home-cooked meals daily, twice-daily showers, daily linen changes, medication support, and basic monitoring of temperature and blood pressure roughly three times a week. Wound care, post-hospital recovery support, on-site doctor consultations and physiotherapy are listed, alongside structured activities and pet therapy sessions. Pricing is publicly stated — RM 2,400/month for ambulatory residents and RM 2,700/month for those needing diapers — which is unusually transparent for a Johor home in this segment.

Yong An reads as a mid-sized Chinese-Malaysian residential home suited to families wanting straightforward elderly care with predictable monthly costs and no language barrier in Mandarin. Diet appears flexible (pork is on the menu, which rules it out for halal-only families). The website does not publish bed count, registered nurse coverage at night, or whether the on-site doctor is salaried or visiting — useful things to confirm by phone before placement, especially for residents with active medical needs.
address: 5, Jalan Berlian 4, Taman Gunung Emas, 81800 Ulu Tiram, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

title: Blissful Care Centre
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Blissful Senior Care Centre is one of Kluang's licensed residential care options for older adults, operating in a town of 323,000 people in central Johor — roughly 100 km north of Johor Bahru and positioned as a more affordable alternative to the saturated urban eldercare market. The facility's emphasis on government licensing and certification, carried prominently in its name and description, signals a deliberate positioning against the unlicensed care homes that continue to operate across peninsular Malaysia's smaller towns. With two well-equipped hospitals in Kluang itself — the government Hospital Enche' Besar Hajjah Khalsom (MSQH-accredited) and the private KPJ Kluang Specialist Hospital (JCI and MSQH accredited) — residents requiring acute care have reliable access without lengthy transfers.

The 4.8-star rating drawn from 22 Google reviews is the facility's most telling public signal. For a care home in a secondary Malaysian town, 22 reviews represents genuine community engagement, and a 4.8 average at that sample size almost certainly reflects authentic positive family experiences. Malaysian eldercare review patterns consistently reward facilities where staff warmth, cleanliness, food quality, and responsive family communication converge — traits that small, owner-operated care homes in towns like Kluang sometimes deliver more consistently than larger corporatised urban facilities.

What limits this profile is entirely a data gap, not a performance concern. Blissful operates without any public web presence beyond its Google Business listing — no website, no indexed Facebook page, no published pricing. Pricing, clinical capabilities, room configurations, and staffing levels all require a direct call to the facility. Once pricing and clinical scope are confirmed, this facility has the hallmarks of a credible, community-trusted home in an underserved market.

editorial_summary:
JR Segamat Care Centre is the Johor branch of Johnson Residence, a Malaysian eldercare chain operating six locations: JR Pro (Kuala Lumpur), Kee Ann (Melaka), Turf View (Penang), JR Kuantan (Pahang), JR Segamat (Johor) and JR My Place (Ipoh, listed as forthcoming). The Segamat outlet is the chain's only Johor presence and serves a town and surrounding district where residential nursing options are limited compared to JB or the Klang Valley.

Group-wide, Johnson Residence positions itself on three pillars — affordable, professional, caring — and offers 24-hour care, personal hygiene support, laundry, customised meals, and a choice of private or shared rooms. Staff training is described as ongoing and explicitly includes vital-signs monitoring, body repositioning, hand hygiene and basic life support; the operator says it accommodates five distinct categories of residents but does not detail those categories publicly. Branch-level detail (capacity, RN coverage, pricing) for Segamat specifically is not on the website.

JR Segamat is a reasonable option for families based in central or northern Johor who need residential care close to home rather than commuting to JB. The chain network is a real advantage if a resident later needs to relocate to KL, Melaka or Penang under the same operator. Specific things to confirm by phone: bed count, monthly pricing for shared vs private rooms, language coverage among caregivers, whether a doctor visits routinely, and how Segamat handles palliative or end-of-life cases given its smaller-town setting.
address: Wisma Muhibbah, Bandar Putra, 85000 Segamat, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

title: Cozzi Confinement Center (Yong Peng)
services: meals, laundry, nursing_care, transport, activities
visiting_hours: Monday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM
editorial_summary:
Cozzi Confinement Center at Yong Peng is a postnatal care branch of the Cozzi group, located at 2 Jalan Kota Impian 4 in Yong Peng township, Batu Pahat District. Yong Peng sits at the convergence of the North–South Expressway with routes to Muar and Batu Pahat town, giving the branch reasonable accessibility for families from across central Johor. Like all Cozzi branches, it operates exclusively as a Chinese-style confinement centre for new mothers and newborns — no elderly care, nursing home, or assisted living services are offered.

The Yong Peng branch follows the Cozzi group standard: 28-day residential packages (RM 17,800 Deluxe or RM 20,800 Premium) covering private air-conditioned accommodation with en-suite bathroom, 24-hour nursing and nanny coverage, five daily TCM-formulated meals, postnatal massage and body-wrap therapies, and structured educational activities for new parents. A TCM practitioner and dietician are involved in meal planning; medical emergencies rely on on-call arrangements. Babies and mothers share monitored rooms during the confinement period.

Families searching for elder care in Yong Peng or surrounding Batu Pahat districts should note that Cozzi does not serve that need at this branch. For new-mother families, the Yong Peng location is the newest addition to Cozzi's Batu Pahat cluster and is best contacted directly at +60 12-724 1013 to confirm room availability and any seasonal promotions, as package pricing and add-ons are updated periodically by the group.

title: Haywood Senior Living Medini Johor Bahru
care_types: Independent Living + Assisted Living + Long-term Nursing + Rehabilitation + Day Care + Palliative
services: meals, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, transport, activities, dementia_care, palliative
visiting_hours: Monday: 10 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 10 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 10 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 10 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 10 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: Closed; Sunday: Closed
editorial_summary:
Haywood Senior Living Medini is a premium elderly care and senior living centre situated inside Ramada by Wyndham Medini Johor Bahru, within the Iskandar Puteri corridor — approximately three minutes from Gleneagles Hospital Medini. Part of the Mintygreen Healthcare Group, the facility spans two branches in Peninsular Malaysia (Bangsar, KL and Medini, JB), positioning itself at the intersection of hotel hospitality and licensed senior care. It caters to families who want their parents to live comfortably rather than simply being managed, and to Singaporean families for whom Medini is a familiar 20-kilometre crossing.

Haywood covers the full spectrum from independent and assisted living through to long-term nursing care, day care, post-hospitalisation rehabilitation, and palliative care. The in-house team includes nurses, physiotherapists, and psychological counsellors — the centre has publicly demonstrated therapist-led sessions including Jenga-based emotional processing. In April 2026 the centre was named a Selected Senior Care and Rehabilitation Partner by Prudential Insurance Malaysia, a credential worth noting when comparing facilities with similar marketing claims. Residents receive four meals daily (including a weekly hotel breakfast buffet), access to the Ramada pool for aqua aerobics, an outdoor gym, reflexology walks, monthly outings to Emerald Lake JB and Singapore Botanic Gardens, and AI-monitored in-room CCTV and call-bell response. Traditional Chinese Medicine practitioners (acupuncture, Tui Na) are available as add-ons.

Haywood operates 24/7 with caregivers on duty around the clock; enquiry hours are Monday to Friday 9 am to 6 pm. Studio apartments include a private balcony, wall-mounted bath chair, and grab bars; shared suite options are available. Pricing is not published online — a written quote is required during a visit or enquiry call. Languages supported: English, Bahasa Malaysia, and Mandarin. Confirm JKM/MOH licence before committing. The closest hospital, Gleneagles Medini, is explicitly cited as three minutes away.

**What to ask on visit**
- Can you show us the JKM or MOH licence certificate for this branch?
- What is the all-in monthly fee for a studio versus a shared suite at my parent's care level?
- How does the Prudential partnership work in practice — does it affect billing or care protocols?
- Is the on-site doctor available every day, or on-call only?

The Tangkak branch operates alongside sister homes in Muar, Melaka and Batu Pahat; group-wide the chain reports more than 200 residents.

In a Tangkak catchment, the practical implication is that resident profiles skew towards lower-acuity stable elders and end-of-life care close to family, rather than the heavier post-discharge rehab cases more common at the chain's larger-town branches. The published trilingual operation (English, Mandarin and Malay) genuinely matters here — Tangkak's Chinese, Malay and Indian populations live more mixed than in metropolitan JB, and a family member visiting parents at the home shouldn't have to bring a translator. Branch-level features to confirm at Tangkak specifically: how many beds are on site (the chain figure is not the branch figure), whether the named physiotherapist is full-time at Tangkak or rotating from Muar, whether the speech-therapy and OT services advertised at chain level are actually delivered at Tangkak or only at the larger branches, and what local hospital arrangement applies — Hospital Tangkak is small, so escalation may mean a transfer to Muar or Segamat.

The Tangkak branch suits families wanting a licensed, multilingual chain home for an older relative who would otherwise be cared for at home in town or kampung — the JKM 4-star rating, the chain backing, and the in-town location are the genuine differentiators, particularly for families who want a parent to remain near siblings rather than be moved to JB. Pricing is not published; call ahead for a branch-specific quote that breaks out room rate, consumables and any therapy add-ons.

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan Tangkak
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: 9 AM to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan Tangkak is a welfare-model residential care home operated by Persatuan Kebajikan Nur Ehsan Negeri Johor, a charitable association founded in 2009 to provide shelter and care for abandoned elderly persons and individuals without family support. The Tangkak branch — located in Parit Bunga, Ledang District, near the Tangkak town cluster — is one of four Nur Ehsan homes spread across Johor, with the others in Johor Bahru (Kempas Baru), Bukit Indah, and Simpang Renggam. Collectively the four branches house approximately 167 residents comprising senior citizens alongside adults with physical and mental disabilities.

The Tangkak facility was the site of a 2024 clinical community programme by UiTM medical students that provided a useful snapshot of residents' clinical profile: all 21 elderly residents showed signs of frailty, six were assessed as high-risk for dementia, and residents ranged widely in their independence with daily activities. The programme introduced structured physical exercise, cognitive training, cooking sessions, and music therapy to supplement the home's existing programme of morning exercise and recreational outings. Regular health monitoring and physiotherapy are part of the standard care offer, and the home operates with 24-hour security and CCTV. The UiTM engagement also noted that limited staffing constrains the range of leisure activities that can be run on a day-to-day basis.

Because Nur Ehsan operates as a charity with no fixed resident fees, it functions primarily as a safety-net placement for elderly persons who lack family or financial resources — not as a fee-for-service nursing home. Families considering this route should contact the Johor association directly at 010-764 1379 or 07-232 6415 to understand the intake process and whether the Tangkak branch has capacity. It is also worth asking about medical referral arrangements for residents who require hospitalisation, the staff-to-resident ratio at the Tangkak site specifically, and whether residents with moderate dementia can be supported given the frailty profile of the current resident group.
address: LC 382, Jalan Sentosa 6, Taman Sentosa, 84900 Tangkak, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

title: Pusat jagaan kebajikan Manna Sinar Cahaya
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Kebajikan Manna Sinar Cahaya sits in a distinct segment of Johor Bahru's elder-care landscape — the welfare home sector — that serves clients who cannot access or afford the city's growing private nursing home industry. The name itself signals its positioning: 'Manna' is a Biblical reference found almost exclusively in Christian charity branding in Malaysia, while 'Kebajikan' (welfare) signals a social-care rather than commercial orientation. Families considering this facility should understand they are not comparing it against private nursing homes charging RM 2,000–5,000 per month; they are comparing it against other welfare homes where care is donation-subsidised, JKM-referred, and focused on shelter and basic daily support rather than clinical nursing.

The facility has a minimal digital footprint — no website, no social media presence, and no listing in the major NGO or JKM directories accessible online — which is not unusual for small Christian welfare homes in Malaysia but does mean families must make direct contact and conduct in-person visits to assess quality. Its Google rating of 3.9 stars from just 7 reviews provides a weak signal; with so few reviewers, the score could reflect one or two dissatisfied family members rather than a systemic pattern.

For families seeking an affordable long-term placement for an elderly relative who is financially disadvantaged, ambulatory or minimally dependent, and comfortable in a Christian environment, this facility warrants investigation through JKM Johor Bahru's referral process. Families should request to see the facility's JKM registration certificate, visit in person, and ask specifically about the monthly fee, staff roster, and emergency medical arrangements before committing. For high-dependency care — ventilator, PEG, dementia unit, post-surgical recovery — this type of welfare home is not the right fit.
address: No37, Jalan Danau 7, Taman Desa Jaya, 81100 Johor Bahru, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

The site describes nursing care for older adults with experienced nurses and caregivers, communicated in English and Mandarin. Specific capacity, room types, doctor visit frequency, and detailed clinical capabilities (PEG feeding, wound care complexity, dementia unit) are not yet published. Pricing is not listed online; the published enquiry line is +60 12-777 6673.

North-JB families wanting a smaller, family-run home with continuity to a longer-running operation should consider a visit. The proximity to Hospital Sultanah Aminah is a genuine practical advantage for residents likely to need acute escalation — transfers from Larkin to HSA are short, and the family can usually get to either site quickly. Because the centre is new in its own right, ask harder questions on the visit: what is the current resident count and intended capacity, how many caregivers are rostered per shift in practice (not on paper), who is the visiting doctor and how often they actually round, whether the home is JKM-licensed in its own name or only under the older Lee Nursing Home licence, and what the published rate covers versus what is billed extra each month. Worth asking also whether residents from Lee Nursing Home have transferred across — if they have, the staff continuity is a real signal of the operation's depth.

editorial_summary:
Sunrise Care Centre Sdn Bhd (Co. 567396-U) is one of two residential care centres operated by the Rebina Sunrise Elderly Care group, a family-owned operator that has been running nursing care in Johor Bahru since 2003. The facility sits at No. 48 Jalan Keranji in Taman Kebun Teh — a quiet residential neighbourhood — directly across from the group's founding home, Rebina House, at Nos. 15 & 17 on the same road. The group's stated origin story is practical: at founding, fewer than 30 care centres served a Johor Bahru population of nearly half a million, and the founders set out to fill that gap with reliable, no-frills care.

The clinical list documented on the group's website is unusually detailed for a residential home of this size. Confirmed services include Continuous Ambulatory Peritoneal Dialysis (CAPD), Ryle's tube feeding and replacement, phlegm suction, nebuliser therapy, oxygen supply, balloon catheter management, post-operative wound dressing, acupuncture, physiotherapy, and daily monitoring of blood pressure and glucose. Palliative and hospice care is also offered. Staff document individualised care plans with hourly schedules — a practice more commonly associated with step-down hospital wards than small community homes. The Instagram account shows Klinik Kesihatan Larkin doctors and nurses visiting the centres to conduct routine dental check-ups for residents, indicating an active public health partnership.

Pricing is not published on the rebinasunrise.com website; families should call +60 7-333 7715 or email care@rebinasunrise.com to request a quote. Gallery photos and social media posts show a busy activities programme — games sessions, seasonal celebrations spanning Chinese New Year, Deepavali, Eid, and Christmas — and regular community visits, giving the home a well-used, lived-in character. The facility accepts residents of all religions and backgrounds.

**What to ask on visit:**
- What is the monthly fee for the level of care needed, and what is included?
- Is the JKM or MOH registration certificate available to view?
- Which specific clinical services (CAPD, PEG feeding) are available at this branch versus Rebina House?
- What is the nursing staff ratio during day and night shifts?
- Are beds currently available, and what is the typical waiting time?
address: 48 Jalan Keranji, Taman Kebun Teh, 80250 Johor Bahru, Johor

Its active social media presence, featuring group exercise sessions and community events, suggests a genuine commitment to resident wellbeing beyond basic physical care.

The facility's key limitation for families conducting online research is a near-total absence of digital transparency. Its website domain is unreachable, its three Facebook pages create confusion about the canonical contact channel, and no pricing, staffing ratios, clinical capabilities, or JKM licence number have been disclosed publicly. Alda Homes does not appear in the AGECOPE Johor member directory, and has accumulated only 11 Google reviews in nearly a decade of operation.

For a low-to-moderate dependency resident who needs a warm, central JB placement rather than high-acuity nursing, Alda Homes is worth a site visit. Pricing is unconfirmed but expected to be broadly mid-market for JB central. Prospective families should verify the JKM licence on-site, confirm RN coverage for both day and night shifts, and establish a clear communication protocol with the operator before committing to placement.

title: Pusat Perjagaan Orang Tua Ren Ai
editorial_summary:
Pusat Perjagaan Orang Tua Ren Ai (仁爱安老院) is a community-founded care home in Senai, within Kulai District, Johor — a township that has grown rapidly alongside the Senai International Airport corridor. The facility was established in 2010 by Chang Kok Wei and operates on a charitable model: care is provided free of charge to elderly residents who have no dependents or means of support. This positions Ren Ai firmly outside the commercial private nursing home market — it is a subsidised residential charity, not a fee-paying facility. The Facebook page (RenAiSenai, 107 followers) reflects its faith-informed, community-service ethos with the phrase: 'at the end of human capacity, life begins.'

Address confirmed from the Facebook listing is No. 204, Jalan Tenggiri, Saleng, Senai, 81400. Phone on record is +60 12-730 0944, and email tiokokwee5911@gmail.com is listed. The Facebook page classifies the home as a Community Service organisation, consistent with its charitable mission. Care types are not formally published, but as a residential home for non-self-funding elderly, the facility provides basic nursing and assisted-living support — the exact clinical depth is unconfirmed and should be verified on visit. Phone +60 7-599 1944 is also on record as the facility landline.

Families considering Ren Ai should understand that this is a welfare facility — places are likely prioritised by need and welfare-department referral rather than open public admission. JKM registration has not been independently confirmed in online directories for this listing, though community welfare homes of this type typically hold registration with the Kulai District JKM office. Online presence is minimal: no website, no Google Maps business listing, only a Facebook page. Phone or email is the only reliable contact channel.

**What to ask on visit**
- What is the admission criteria — is it referral-only through JKM, or can families apply directly?
- What care services are available on-site (nursing, physiotherapy, wound care)?
- Is there a JKM registration certificate for this facility?
- What languages do care staff speak day-to-day?
- Is there a waiting list, and how long is it typically?
- What is the process if a resident needs emergency hospital care?
address: 204, Jalan Tenggiri, Taman Sri Saleng, 81400 Kulai, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

As an establishment incorporated in 2023, Fu Ka is still building its public track record. Families should request the JKM licence number, verify halal meal arrangements (not confirmed), and ask about pricing by room type. WhatsApp contact is not publicly listed — initial contact via phone is recommended. title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan
visiting_hours: Monday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: 8 AM to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan is a welfare-model nursing home operated by Persatuan Kebajikan Nur Ehsan Negeri Johor (PKNENJ), a registered Johor welfare society, from its Kempas Baru branch at Lot 3353, MLD 1034, No. 3, Jalan Denai 1, 81200 Johor Bahru. Established in November 2008 and formally incorporated in June 2009, it is one of the older Muslim-focused elderly care institutions in southern Johor. The organisation operates four branches statewide — Johor Bahru, Ledang, Bukit Indah, and Simpang Renggam — with the Kempas Baru branch having a stated capacity of 56 beds.

Admission is structured around welfare need, not market rates: the home serves elderly Muslims without close heirs, those in destitution, converts (muallaf), and persons with disabilities. Referrals typically come via JKM officers, hospital social workers, and Islamic bodies MAIJ and JAIJ. Families with means who seek to place a relative here are asked to contribute modestly — exact amounts should be confirmed with the manager, Encik Saiful, at 013-794 0350. The fee model is donation-based rather than a fixed monthly fee. Clinical services confirmed include physiotherapy and regular medical examinations; 24-hour CCTV security is in place. Visiting hours are 10am to 6pm daily. Phone: +60 7-232 6415.

The Facebook page for this branch is privacy-restricted and not publicly viewable. The facility does not maintain a public website. The main contact route is via phone to the manager or the branch landline. Because this is a welfare institution with a Muslim focus, families from non-Muslim backgrounds or those seeking standard private nursing home terms should contact first to confirm eligibility and arrangement terms.

**What to ask on visit**
- What is the monthly contribution expected for family-placed residents?
- Is the current intake open or is there a waiting list?
- Can you show the JKM registration certificate for this branch?
- What are the on-site nursing care capabilities (wound care, tube feeding, catheter)?
- Is there a doctor who visits regularly?

title: Agape Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Agape Care Centre sits quietly on Jalan Lurah 18 in Kempas Baru — a residential street that has become one of Johor Bahru's most recognisable care home clusters, home also to Woon Ho Family Care Centre and Spring Valley Homecare. At No. 62, this small facility operates as both a nursing home and a retirement home, catering to elderly who need daily assistance rather than intensive clinical intervention. With a 4.6-star Google rating from five reviewers, it carries an excellent word-of-mouth reputation within what appears to be a tight-knit referral circle.

The name 'Agape' — Greek for unconditional love, central to Christian theology — strongly suggests faith-inspired ownership or management, placing Agape Care Centre in the tradition of church-affiliated elderly care. For Christian residents or those whose families are embedded in a Christian community, this setting may offer something most commercial nursing homes do not: a genuine sense of belonging, pastoral companionship, and a value system that treats care as vocation. Whether this translates to organised chapel sessions, church volunteer visits, or informal devotional culture is something families should ask directly.

Agape Care Centre has no confirmed website, no publicly available pricing, no JKM licence number in any accessible registry, and no detailed clinical services list. This level of opacity is not unusual for a small referral-based care home in Malaysia, but it means families cannot evaluate this facility without a phone call and a site visit. Before placement, the JKM licence must be verified, clinical capabilities assessed against the resident's medical needs, and the nature of any religious programming clarified for non-Christian families.

Cozzi Confinement Center Muar (娘家精致月子中心) is one of several branches operated by the Cozzi group across Johor and Kuala Lumpur, and sits at 1A, Taman Jaya Baru, 84000 Muar. As the Chinese-style name makes explicit, this is a postnatal confinement centre for new mothers rather than a nursing home for elderly residents — it provides care during the roughly 28-30 days following childbirth, a practice deeply rooted in Chinese postpartum tradition known as zuo yue zi. The Muar branch was established as part of the group's Johor expansion and serves families in Muar and surrounding Johor districts who prefer a structured confinement facility over home-based care.

Cozzi's group-wide model — which applies at the Muar location — centres on 24-hour professional nursing and nanny care for newborns, midwife-guided support for mothers, daily meal plans developed by Chinese medicine practitioners and dietitians (five meals per day plus beverages), postnatal massages, herbal baths, exercise programmes, and regular paediatrician visits. Rooms are air-conditioned with attached bathrooms and WiFi. Cozzi's two standard packages across its network are Deluxe (RM 17,800 for 28 days) and Premium (RM 20,800 for 28 days), though branch-specific pricing should be confirmed directly.

Families planning to use the Muar branch should confirm availability well in advance, as confinement centres frequently fill up months before due dates. Specific questions worth asking: whether a lactation consultant is on roster, what the neonatal escalation pathway is if the baby needs hospital review, and whether the room rate includes all meals and supplements or whether some items carry surcharges. The centre can be reached at +60 12-712 6705.

The Sri Jaya branch forms part of Cozzi's cluster of Batu Pahat locations, which also includes centres in Parit Besar, Tanah Merah, and Yong Peng, giving the group a strong footprint across this district of Johor.

The Sri Jaya centre follows Cozzi's group-wide care model: 24-hour nursing and nanny care for newborns, weekly visits from a Chinese medicine practitioner, postnatal massage and herbal bath programmes for mothers, a structured six-meal-per-day dietary plan incorporating tonic soups and confinement-appropriate nutrition, and regular paediatrician visits. Operating hours for enquiries are listed as 09:30–18:00 daily, and the centre is contactable at +60 12-756 6705. Group-wide packages run approximately RM 17,800 (Deluxe) to RM 20,800 (Premium) for 28 days.

Families in Batu Pahat considering the Sri Jaya centre should book well in advance given how quickly confinement centres fill around due dates. It is worth confirming on contact whether the specific Sri Jaya branch pricing aligns with the group's standard packages, whether a lactation consultant is available on-site, and what the neonatal emergency escalation pathway looks like. As with the Muar branch, this facility is classified in the sheet as a nursing home but is operationally a postnatal confinement centre — families seeking long-term elderly care should look elsewhere.

editorial_summary:
Cozzi Confinement Center at Tanah Merah is one of several Batu Pahat–area branches operated by the Cozzi group, a multi-location postnatal care chain active across Johor since 2017. The Tanah Merah branch sits at 12 Jalan Matahari in the Tanah Merah township of Batu Pahat District, offering residential postpartum packages that blend traditional Chinese confinement practices with modern nursing support. The centre is Chinese-run and operates primarily in Mandarin and English, and it is positioned as a premium option for new mothers rather than a facility for elderly or medically complex residents.

Cozzi's standard offering is a 28-day confinement package (priced chain-wide at RM 17,800–RM 20,800 depending on tier) covering private accommodation, 24-hour nursing and nanny care for mother and newborn, five TCM-guided meals daily, postpartum massage and body-wrap treatments, and structured activities. The Tanah Merah branch shares this template with sibling sites in Yong Peng, Sri Jaya, Parit Besar, and Muar. Medical oversight is by on-call arrangement rather than on-site physicians, consistent with the confinement-centre model across Malaysia.

Families researching elder care should note that Cozzi does not offer nursing home, assisted living, or elderly day care services — the facility is exclusively for postpartum mothers and newborns. If your search led here while looking for an elder care bed in Batu Pahat, you will need to look at other providers in the district. For families seeking postnatal confinement care, the Tanah Merah branch is worth a direct call to confirm room availability and current package inclusions, as promotional add-ons change seasonally.

The 3.5-star Google Maps rating — below the JB average for licensed care homes — is a flag that warrants honest disclosure. What is known is that this is a small, home-based setup in a kampung residential address, relying heavily on donor support for basics such as diapers and cleaning supplies. Pusat Jagaan Teduhan Zafrah is a private care home in Taman Desa Sepayung, Muar, specialising in care for elderly men (Warga Emas) and male persons with disabilities (OKU). Founded on 9 September 2013 and run by a husband-and-wife team — Pn. Nurfarah and En. Ariffin — the facility was built on a personal mission to care for seniors and disabled residents as one would care for family. The male-only admission policy (reflected in the full name, which includes "LELAKI") means the home is purpose-structured for that cohort, allowing a focused care environment.

Teduhan Zafrah operates under dual oversight by JKM and the Ministry of Health, and nursing staff are registered with Lembaga Juruawat Malaysia. The facility offers geriatric and palliative care alongside daily structured programmes: religious classes, group health exercises, physiotherapy and rehabilitation sessions, and vocational training activities. The holistic model addresses physical, mental, emotional, and social needs in a single setting. Pricing starts from RM1,300 per month for residential care, with a RM200 registration fee and day-care options from RM100 per day; final fees are set after an individual needs assessment. The home partly supplements operating costs through public donations and grants from Baitulmal and JKM.

Given the facility's community-driven funding model and modest resident numbers (recorded at more than ten), prospective families should confirm current capacity and vacancy at the time of enquiry. The primary contacts are Pn. Nurfarah (019-685 5649) and En. Ariffin (013-311 9462). Questions worth raising include the current number of residents, the scope of palliative care available for end-of-life situations, and the process for managing residents whose medical needs escalate beyond what the home can support.

editorial_summary:
Amitabha Foundation (Amitabha Malaysia) is a Buddhist-affiliated charity headquartered at Taman Tasek in Johor Bahru, established in June 1998 under the motto "Help the Needy and Relieve the Distressed." Its elder care arm is one of four programme pillars alongside a children's home, a haemodialysis centre, and a financial assistance scheme. The head office at 59B Jalan Glasiar doubles as the administrative hub for elder care operations, with the foundation explicitly stating that its elder homes accept chronically ill, abandoned, and destitute elderly persons — including those discharged from hospital with nowhere to go. All ethnic and religious backgrounds are welcomed, and the foundation emphasises that contributions are fully channelled to care rather than administration.

The foundation's elder care capacity is spread across two residential homes — one in Country Heights and one in Kuchai Lama, Kuala Lumpur — while the Johor Bahru base manages outreach, referrals, and day-care coordination. The facility is recorded as a day-care service in Johor Bahru, likely serving seniors in the Taman Tasek catchment area through structured daytime programmes that complement rather than replace residential care. The foundation also runs a haemodialysis clinic at the same Jalan Glasiar address, making it a genuinely multi-service welfare hub. Operational funding comes from recycling proceeds and corporate sponsors, keeping the charitable elder care model financially independent from residents' families.

Families considering Amitabha Malaysia for a day-care placement or residential referral should contact the head office directly at +60 7-213 2448 to understand current availability and the intake process for each service tier. Because the organisation operates on a welfare rather than commercial model, admission typically involves a social welfare assessment rather than a simple fee schedule. It is worth asking specifically which services are available at the Johor Bahru site versus the residential homes in KL, whether transport assistance exists for day-care attendees, and how medical needs above basic health monitoring are managed.

title: EHA Parkview ElderCare
care_types: Assisted, Long-term, Respite, Day care, Post-hospitalisation
editorial_summary:
EHA Parkview Eldercare is the Perling branch of EHA Eldercare Group, in western Johor Bahru on the Skudai corridor. With 85 Google reviews at a 4.9-star average, Parkview has the largest review base of any branch in the EHA network — a meaningful signal of sustained operating history rather than a newly-opened facility. The parent group, EHA Eldercare (1478860-U), evolved from the Justlife ElderCare Home brand founded in 2009 and now runs eight residential locations across Malaysia with a combined 100-plus caregivers and 500-plus residents — making it one of the more established private eldercare chains across Johor and the Klang Valley.

The group's website lists eight published service categories that apply across branches: medical support, nutrition and diet, rehabilitation by physiotherapy, social activities, caregiver training and support, skilled nurses, special nursing procedures, and a traditional Chinese medicine approach (acupuncture and herbal). Pricing is published on EHA's website (ehaeldercare.com.my/our-packages/) and is quoted as a starting point — actual fees vary with the resident's care needs. For the Johor Bahru and Klang Valley branches (which include this one), assisted senior living starts at RM 3,200/month for 24-hour residential care across able-bodied, semi-able-bodied, and bed-bound residents; respite stays of one to six months start at RM 120/day; and day care, available 11 hours a day across five days a week with flexible timing, starts at RM 1,500/month. The Kluang branch is priced lower (assisted from RM 2,300/month, respite from RM 80/day, day care from RM 1,300/month) — useful context if the family can travel and cost is a major factor. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

Perling sits well-connected to the JB-Skudai highway and the wider Iskandar Puteri area, which suits families visiting from Bukit Indah, Tampoi, or further west. With Parkview's longer track record, the useful viewing questions are less about whether the facility runs and more about fit: ask how many residents are in each room, whether RN cover is on-site overnight (versus on-call), how often a physiotherapist attends in person, and what specific clinical procedures the home performs in-house versus refers out (PEG feeds, suction, oxygen, wound dressings, urinary catheter changes). Confirm that the "starting from" rate matches the actual care plan since add-ons can shift the monthly figure. Hospital Sultanah Aminah is the nearest government tertiary centre; KPJ Puteri Specialist Hospital is the nearest private option.

title: FCC Family Care Centre
care_types: Assisted, Dementia/Memory care, Long-term, Respite, Day care, Independent living
editorial_summary:
FCC Family Care Centre bills itself as Malaysia's first Integrated Senior Living and Wellness Village — and the description is not marketing overstatement. The campus in Ulu Tiram, Johor Bahru is built around a philosophy of natural living: an organic farm where residents plant and harvest, free-roaming animals throughout the grounds, healing gardens, and open gazebo spaces designed for community gathering rather than passive observation. This is a fundamentally different model from the clinical-ward nursing home, and it shows in the profile of residents who thrive here.

For families navigating dementia, FCC operates a dedicated MemoryCare Home — a separate, purpose-designed unit with the structured routines, sensory-safe environments, and specialist staffing that memory care requires. The facility is also one of the first in Malaysia to deploy SmartPeep AI monitoring: a system that uses computer vision to detect falls, irregular movement, and distress without invasive physical checks, alerting staff in real time. For families who cannot visit frequently, this technology provides meaningful reassurance.

Pricing tiers run from approximately RM 3,500 to RM 4,300 per month depending on room type — note that these figures are from third-party sources and should be confirmed directly with FCC, as rates may have been updated. Foreign residents (including those relocating from Singapore) are typically required to pay a three-month deposit. The MemoryCare Home pricing may differ from general nursing care rates; ask specifically when enquiring.
address: 17, Jalan Resam, Taman Bukit Tiram, 81800 Ulu Tiram, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

title: Jeta Care
care_types: Assisted Living, Long-term, High-dependency, Palliative, Physiotherapy/Rehab
editorial_summary:
Jeta Care Aged Care Centre in Kulai holds a genuinely distinctive position in the Malaysian elderly care landscape: it is Malaysia's first residential aged care facility built on Australian-model care principles, combined with Confucianist values — a hybrid designed to bridge Western clinical standards with Southeast Asian family expectations. Set at 1, 2 & 3 Jalan Susur Kulai 1, Taman Seraya, Kulai Besar, 81000 Kulai, the facility spans 2,150 m2 across three floors and holds 62 beds. It is licensed by both JKM and the Ministry of Health under the CKAPS framework, with the MOH licence valid to February 2028 — one of the strongest regulatory credentials a private nursing home in Malaysia can hold.

The clinical offering is among the most comprehensive available in Johor outside Johor Bahru: skilled nursing (wound care, PEG tube management, colostomy/ileostomy, tracheostomy, catheter), dementia care, stroke and post-surgical rehabilitation, palliative and end-of-life care, respite care, day care, and transitional care from hospital discharge. Medical oversight is provided by named medical director Dr. Goh Yi Xiong. The centre has served over 2,000 seniors in 15 years of operation. A rooftop garden, 24-hour nurse call system, halal cafe on-premises, and surau (prayer room) make this one of the most fully-featured care homes in Johor. Languages spoken: English, Chinese dialects, and Bahasa Malaysia. Contact: +60 7-663 6888 or WhatsApp +60 18-769 5688; email enquiry@jetacare.com; website jetacare.com.

Pricing is not published on the website. Historical rates from 2020 ranged from approximately RM 3,000 to RM 5,500 per month depending on room type — these figures are several years out of date and should be confirmed directly with the centre. The Facebook page (jetacare, 1,092 followers, 375 check-ins) reflects the facility's standing as the most established and visited of the Kulai-area care homes.

**What to ask on visit**
- What is the current all-in monthly rate by room type?
- Is there a current waiting list and what is the expected lead time?
- For high-dependency patients (PEG, trach): walk us through the after-hours emergency protocol.
- Can we meet Dr. Goh Yi Xiong or the clinical deputy during the tour?
- Is the dementia care a physically separate wing, or integrated with the general ward?
- What is the staffing ratio for night shifts?
- Is respite care available for stays of one to four weeks?
address: 1,2,3 TINGKAT BAWAH, JALAN SUSUR 1, TAMAN SERAYA, KULAI BESAR, 81000 KULAI JOHOR

title: Rumah penjagaan oku. (Asyiq)
longitude: Rumah penjagaan oku. (Asyiq) is an assisted living centre located in Taman Sri Rampai, Kuala Lumpur.
editorial_summary:
Rumah Penjagaan OKU (Asyiq) is listed as an assisted living facility in Taman Sri Rampai, an established Wangsa Maju neighbourhood in north-east Kuala Lumpur. The name signals a focus on Orang Kurang Upaya (OKU) residents, meaning persons with disabilities, which suggests it caters to younger adults with physical or developmental needs alongside any older residents. Beyond the Google Maps listing, no website, Facebook page, directory entry, or news mention surfaces in public sources. The operator, year of establishment, and JKM registration status are not on the public record we can verify.

With no public information on staffing, room configuration, medical capabilities, or pricing, families should treat this listing as a starting point only. Call ahead to confirm the home is still operating, ask whether residents are primarily OKU adults or include elderly cases, and verify the facility's licence under the Care Centres Act 1993. Bring a checklist covering meal arrangements, medication administration, and visiting hours, and ask for a tour before committing.

title: Serim Home Nursing
editorial_summary:
Serim Home Nursing is a home-nursing and short-stay care service operating since 2008, headquartered at No. 81, Jalan Sri Selayang 1, Taman Sri Selayang in Batu Caves rather than central Kuala Lumpur. It is registered as a local medical service rather than a residential nursing home, and the contact channel is via Ms. Lim and Mr. Tan, with no founder or company-name disclosure on the website. The service draws on registered nurses and trained caregivers and markets itself across Selangor and the Klang Valley.

Verified service offerings include 24-hour stay-in nursing, hospital-side nursing, physiotherapy, medical-equipment rental and sale, laboratory blood draws, and caregiver training. The clinical focus areas listed are bedridden patients, stroke recovery, brain injury, post-operative care, pressure-injury prevention, and chronic wound management. The site also references a small residential care arrangement for short or long-term stays, though capacity, room types, and bed numbers are not published. Google reviews show a 5-star rating from nine reviewers, a small but consistently positive sample.

This is a fit for families needing skilled home nursing for medically complex elders, especially after a hospital discharge, or those wanting a tube-fed or wound-care patient managed at home. It is less obviously suited if you are looking for a permanent residential home with structured group activities. Ask specifically about RN-to-patient ratios on overnight shifts, the daily and monthly rates for stay-in care, whether the residential bed option is currently available, and how medication errors and emergencies are escalated. Confirm the JKM or KKM registration that applies to whichever service you book.

title: Wellness Carer & Nursing Services Malaysia
editorial_summary:
Wellness Carer & Nursing Services Malaysia is registered as a Registered General Nurse service in the Bangsar area of Kuala Lumpur. Public information is limited to a Google Business listing carrying a 5-star rating from 13 reviewers and brief third-party directory mentions. The operator does not appear to maintain a website or active Facebook page, and no founder name, company registration, or specific Bangsar address is on the public record. Third-party directories describe it as offering long-term home care rather than visit-based services, with caregiver matching, daily hygiene, tube feeding, wound care, injections, and basic physiotherapy.

This profile fits a domiciliary nursing agency rather than a residential nursing home, so families considering it are typically looking for a nurse or trained caregiver to come into their own home, not a bed. Reviews suggest the team takes detailed clinical handovers, sometimes before hospital discharge, which is useful for stroke, post-operative, or palliative cases. Capacity, pricing structure, language coverage, and supervisor qualifications are not published.

For families calling, ask for the Lembaga Jururawat Malaysia registration of the nurses who would be assigned, the daily and monthly rates for live-in versus shift work, the back-up arrangement when a caregiver falls ill, and whether they handle PEG feeding, tracheostomy care, or palliative symptom control. Confirm the supervising nurse's contact details and how often they review the care plan.
address: Jalan Pudina, Bangsar, 59100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Place Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Place Care Centre is listed as a nursing home in the Wilayah Persekutuan (Federal Territory) of Kuala Lumpur, with a contact line on +60 3-2095 9735. The 03-2095 prefix points to the older central KL exchange covering areas such as Bangsar, Brickfields, and the KL city centre, but the precise street address is not published on Google Maps or in any directory we could verify. There is no website, Facebook page, news mention, or review record that we could confirm under this exact name, and the operator and year of establishment are not on the public record.

With only a name, a phone number, and a generic nursing-home category to work with, families need to do the due-diligence work themselves. Call to confirm the centre is still operating at this number, ask for the exact address, and request the Pusat Jagaan registration number issued under the Care Centres Act 1993. On a site visit, check for a registered nurse on duty, the staff-to-resident ratio across day and night shifts, the procedure for medication storage and administration, fire-safety arrangements, and whether the home accepts bedridden, dementia, or PEG-fed residents. Ask for written pricing and a sample care contract before committing.
address: 275, Jalan Maarof, Wilayah Persekutuan, 59100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Emzis Care Old Folks and Nursing Home in Bangsar
editorial_summary:
Emzis Care, also marketed as Emzis Home and Emzis Age Care, is a private nursing home positioned in Bangsar, Kuala Lumpur. The business was co-founded by Murad Popatiya, an alumnus of SEGi College Kuala Lumpur, who has been profiled in the college's alumni features for his move into eldercare. The Google listing places it in the Bangsar postcode area, but the exact street address is not published on the operator's website (emzishome.com), and no JKM registration number is publicly disclosed.

Verified material from the operator's own marketing describes a residential model centred on engagement, with residents encouraged to take part in group exercises, mental-stimulation activities, and outings to local destinations rather than staying confined to the home. The business presents itself as a small boutique operation rather than a large multi-bed centre, but capacity, room types, monthly rates, staffing ratios, and clinical capabilities such as PEG feeding, dementia management, or palliative care are not published. There is no Google review record we could verify under this slug, which is unusual for an active home and worth asking about directly.

This is a possible fit for families looking for a smaller, activity-oriented assisted-living setting in central KL for an ambulant elder who can join programmed activities. It is less clearly suited for fully bedridden, advanced-dementia, or end-of-life cases until you have confirmed clinical capability in writing. Ask for the Care Centres Act licence, the registered nurse roster, the doctor visit arrangement, the meal plan, and how the home handles a resident who deteriorates and needs more nursing-intensive care.

title: H2hCare@Bangsar
care_types: Assisted living + Home care + Nursing home
editorial_summary:
H2hCare@Bangsar (Hospital To Home Care) is a hybrid service in Bukit Persekutuan, Bangsar, that combines private home nursing with a small assisted-living and nursing-home arm. The brand maintains a website at h2hcare.com.my and a Facebook page (Hospital To Home Care | Bangsar). The team positions itself as a discharge-to-home bridge, organising hospital-to-home transfers, recording pre-visit vital signs, liaising with treating hospitals, and providing companionship alongside clinical tasks.

Publicly documented services include nursing procedures, post-hospital care, remote patient monitoring, and physiotherapy. Visit-based procedures are advertised from RM 120, with care-specialist hours stated as roughly 7 am to 7.30 pm. The team notes Mandarin-speaking care specialists are available, which suggests at least bilingual coverage in English and Mandarin. The residential element is light on public detail: bed count, monthly rates, room configuration, doctor visit arrangements, and JKM registration are not published, and Google reviews under this exact slug are not visible.

The service is best understood as a home-nursing operator that can also accommodate short residential stays in Bangsar, useful for families managing a post-stroke or post-surgical elder who is too medically complex for home alone but does not yet need a long-stay nursing home. Families considering the residential option should ask about overnight RN cover, the on-call doctor, what clinical procedures (suction, PEG, oxygen, IV) the home is licensed to do on-site, and how transfers back to hospital are handled when a resident deteriorates.
address: 31, Jalan Abdullah, Bukit Persekutuan, 59000 Bangsar, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Eden retirement village
editorial_summary:
Eden Retirement Village, also listed publicly as Eden Village Retirement Home, is a Kuala Lumpur-based retirement and assisted-living facility. The Google Maps listing places it in the Bangsar area, while the operator's Facebook page (facebook.com/edenvillagekl) and a publicly listed contact number (+60 12-283 8190) and email (edenvillagekl@gmail.com) are the main verified channels. The Facebook page describes the home as a retirement and assisted-living facility offering home-health-care services and nursing-home accommodation. The operator, founding year, and JKM licence number are not publicly disclosed.
