No room configuration, capacity, monthly rates, or staffing details are published. Reviews under this exact name are not visible in standard search results, and there is no separate website. This is a thin profile for a public-facing facility, which suggests most enquiries are handled by phone or via the Facebook page rather than through a marketing site.

Families considering Eden are best advised to call before driving over to confirm the precise address, whether the home accepts new admissions, and what level of dependency it is licensed to accept. Ask whether a registered nurse is rostered overnight, the doctor visit cadence, monthly fees and what they include (diapers, milk feeds, physiotherapy), and whether the home holds a current Pusat Jagaan registration under the Care Centres Act 1993. Request a tour and meet the duty supervisor before committing.
address: Jalan Ara, Bangsar, 59100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Capella Senior Living
editorial_summary:
Capella Senior Living is a small assisted-living home in Bangsar, Kuala Lumpur, operating from a single bungalow and marketing itself at capellasl.com. Contact runs through 012-696 1932 or 016-206 3307. The operator's name and founding year are not disclosed on the website, and no JKM registration number is published. The model is explicitly boutique: the home advertises a deliberately small resident population so caregivers can give individual attention, rather than a larger ward-style nursing-home format.

Verified services from the operator's own pages include daily personal care (bathing, grooming, mobility assistance), medication administration, three meals a day plus snacks freshly prepared on-site, housekeeping, and supervision through the day. Care plans are described as individualised, with one published testimonial referencing a resident with dementia, though the home does not formally advertise a dedicated dementia unit. Specific staffing ratios, qualifications of the lead caregiver, doctor visit schedule, and monthly rates are not published. Google reviews show a 4-star rating from a small base of two reviewers.

This is a fit for families wanting a domestic-scale setting for a parent who needs help with activities of daily living but is not heavily medically dependent. It is less obviously suited for fully bedridden cases, PEG feeding, or advanced dementia until you confirm in writing that the home can manage those needs. Ask about overnight staffing (one caregiver awake versus on-call), what happens when the lead caregiver is on leave, and the contingency plan if a resident's condition worsens beyond the home's licence.
address: 124, Lrg Maarof, Bangsar, 59000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Harapan Senior Care
editorial_summary:
Harapan Senior Care is listed as a retirement home in the Bangsar area of Kuala Lumpur, with a Google contact line on +60 10-913 9813. Beyond the basic Google Maps entry there is no verifiable web footprint: no website, no Facebook page, no directory profile, and no Google review record we could confirm under this name. The operator, founding year, address detail, and JKM Pusat Jagaan registration are not on the public record.

The "retirement home" classification on Google Maps is broad in Malaysia and can cover anything from an independent-living arrangement to a full nursing home, so families cannot assume the level of clinical support without checking. Pricing, capacity, room types, and staffing are all undisclosed.

Before visiting, call the listed number to confirm the home is still operating, get the full street address, and ask whether it caters to ambulant retirees only or accepts dependent and bedridden cases. On a site visit, ask for the licence under the Care Centres Act 1993, check whether a registered nurse is on the staff roster, see the kitchen and a typical room, and ask for the monthly fee schedule in writing. If the operator cannot produce a valid licence, treat that as a serious red flag.
address: 107, Jalan Cenderai, Bangsar, 59100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Serenity LifeCare
editorial_summary:
Serenity LifeCare is a small nursing home at No. 16, Lorong Travers, off Jalan Bangsar, Bukit Persekutuan, 50480 Bangsar, Kuala Lumpur. It is listed on the MDAC business directory as a real-estate-and-nursing-home operation, with Ms Sara recorded as the person in charge and 017-773 8809 as the contact line. The operator does not maintain a public website, and no founder, company name, or JKM registration number is published. Google reviews are positive but on a small base of six reviewers giving a 5-star average, which is encouraging but limited.

The Lorong Travers address sits inside a quiet residential pocket of Bukit Persekutuan close to Jalan Maarof, suggesting a converted bungalow or terraced-house setting rather than a purpose-built ward. Capacity, room configuration, monthly rates, doctor visit arrangements, staffing ratios, and clinical capability for bedridden or dementia residents are not on the public record. A directory blurb describes the home as a community-oriented care setting, but no specifics about activities, meals, or licence status are published.

This is potentially suitable for families seeking a smaller, residential-scale home in central KL with a Bangsar address, particularly for an elder who values being in a familiar urban neighbourhood. Before committing, ask Ms Sara for the Care Centres Act 1993 licence, the registered-nurse roster including overnight cover, the standard monthly fee with a clear list of inclusions and extras, the doctor and physiotherapy arrangement, and whether the home can manage a resident who becomes bedridden or develops advanced dementia.
address: 16, Lorong Travers, off Jalan Bangsar, Bukit Persekutuan, 50480 Bangsar, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Meridian Care Living Centre
care_types: Aged care, Adult day care center, Home health care service
editorial_summary:
Meridian Care Living Centre operates from a residential property at 31 Jalan Abdullah, Bangsar, positioning itself as an enhanced assisted-living and rehabilitation centre rather than a conventional nursing home. The operator markets the site to families looking for post-hospital and post-surgical recovery placements as well as longer-term elder care, and also runs a daycare programme. Care staff are described as a mix of Malaysian and foreign nurses qualified to BSN level and registered with the Ministry of Health, though the company has not published a founding year or operator name on its public site.

The centre advertises in-house physiotherapy with dedicated equipment, en-suite bedrooms, a sensory garden and reflexology pathway, and reading and leisure areas. Care types listed cover post-surgery rehab, post-hospital step-down, daycare and elder care; specific clinical capabilities such as PEG feeding, tracheostomy suctioning, dialysis support or dementia-secure provision are not detailed publicly. Bed count, staff-to-resident ratio and pricing are also not disclosed. Google reviews (4.6 across 26 ratings as of writing) are broadly positive but the sample is small.

This address suits families who want a Bangsar location with a rehabilitation slant and have a relative needing a structured recovery period or assisted living rather than complex skilled nursing. Ask directly about: total beds, single versus shared room rates, whether a doctor visits routinely or only on call, the centre's experience with PEG, catheter and tracheostomy care, dementia wandering safeguards, and whether the daycare and residential programmes share the same building. Confirm the JKM registration number before signing.
address: 31, Jalan Abdullah, Bangsar, 59000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Haywood Senior Living @ Bangsar
editorial_summary:
Haywood Senior Living at 136 Jalan Ara, Bangsar, is the KL flagship of the Haywood Living group, which markets itself as a hotel-style premium senior-living operator and also runs a sister site in Medini, Johor Bahru. The Bangsar location is a relatively new addition to the Bangsar care landscape and pitches itself at the upper end of the market, with shared and en-suite single rooms, twin-sharing options and studio units rather than the dormitory layout common in budget homes.

The operator publishes an unusually broad service list for a Malaysian home: in-house doctors, registered nurses, Traditional Chinese Medicine practitioners, physiotherapy, occupational therapy, speech therapy and mental-health counselling, plus a gym, swimming pool, salon, KTV and multipurpose rooms. It accepts short-term rehabilitation, long-term residential care for semi-dependent and fully bedridden residents, daycare and post-hospitalisation step-down. Capacity, doctor-visit frequency and rates are not stated on the website; Google rating sits at 4.9 across 45 reviews.

This is a fit for families prioritising a hotel-style environment in central KL and willing to pay a premium for amenities and integrated allied health rather than a clinical, hospital-feel home. Practical questions to put to the centre: licensed JKM registration number, monthly room rates by type, whether allied-health sessions are included or charged per visit, ratio of trained nurses on the night shift, and how the team manages dementia residents given the open-plan amenity areas.
address: Block C, Lower Ground 136, Jalan Ara, Bangsar, 59100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: The Senior Care
care_types: Nursing home + Assisted Living + Retirement home
editorial_summary:
The Senior Care operates two licensed branches in the heart of Petaling Jaya: a 21-bed flagship at No. 2, Jalan 12/13, Section 12, and a 16-bed quieter facility at No. 35a, Jalan Bukit 11/2, Section 11. Both are purpose-built with step-free access and toilets throughout — a practical detail that matters for residents with limited mobility or fall risk. The Section 12 site is described as vibrant and socially oriented, while Section 11 is positioned as a calmer, more intimate setting approximately 700 metres from University of Malaya Medical Centre (PPUM), which also happens to be close to Assunta, Columbia Asia, and Beacon hospitals.

The Senior Care's website lists a considered clinical offer: long-term nursing care, assisted living, dementia care, respite, rehabilitation, day care, wound care management, and physiotherapy — delivered through qualified nurses and physiotherapists described as companions as much as clinicians. Six freshly cooked meals daily, CCTV coverage in rooms and common areas, in-house doctor, weekly physiotherapist visits, a landscaped garden at the Section 11 branch, and a Koi pond and patio suggest an operator that has thought about resident experience. Section 11 has WiFi and 24-hour surveillance. The facility is registered with AGECOPE, the industry association for residential aged care operators.

With a 4.9 Google rating from 10 reviews, both branches appear well-regarded, though the review count is small. Pricing is not published; contact is via +60 12-667 6125. Key questions for families: what is the Section 12 outdoor access arrangement (urban shophouse areas can limit this), how are dementia residents managed relative to the general population, and is the in-house doctor a GP or a geriatrician? The combined 37 beds across two branches means waiting lists are plausible — confirm availability and expected timelines early.
address: 2, Jalan 12/13, Section 12, 46200 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Sunshine Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Sunshine Nursing Home occupies a double-storey corner house at 1 Jalan 52/1, Desa Jaya, Kepong, on the northern edge of Kuala Lumpur. It is a small, owner-operated home rather than a chain branch, with directory listings indicating six bedrooms and four bathrooms, which suggests a capacity in the low-to-mid teens. The operator has no website or active social-media presence; phone enquiries go to 03-6276 5867 or 012-241 5380.

The home advertises 24-hour nursing care and explicitly takes residents with higher clinical needs than many small homes will accept: stroke recovery, nasogastric and PEG tube feeding, colostomy care, and tracheostomy with suctioning. Day-to-day support covers bathing, diapering, supervised exercise, medication administration and monitoring of vital signs and blood glucose. The home also offers private nurses for hospital sitting and home visits, suggesting a flexible single-team model rather than a strict residential-only setup. Google rating is 4.8 across 10 reviews, a small sample.

The likely fit is a family wanting a quieter, residential-feel placement in Kepong for an elderly relative with stroke or tube-feeding needs, who would prefer a small home over a larger institution. Because the operator runs a low-profile setup, ask directly for the JKM registration certificate, the registered nurse versus caregiver ratio across day and night shifts, doctor visit arrangements, fire-safety clearance for a converted residential building, and whether room rates are quoted per bed in shared rooms or per room.
address: 1, Jalan 52/1, Desa Jaya, 52100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan, Malaysia

title: Cality Care Malaysia
care_types: Home health care service, Nursing agency, Physical therapist
editorial_summary:
Cality Care Malaysia is a home health care agency and staffing service headquartered in the KL Traders Square building along Jalan Gombak, with its Google Maps presence tagged to the Gombak area of Selangor. The company is the Malaysian arm of ARC Group Australia and positions itself around delivering Australian-standard home care — a useful framing that signals structured care protocols, documented care plans, and trained rather than informally recruited staff. The care team includes Registered Nurses, Medical Assistants, Physiotherapists, and Allied Health professionals, as well as in-house trained caregivers certified through their own training programme.

Cality's service model is home-based rather than residential: they dispatch carers to clients' own homes rather than operating a nursing facility with beds. Services cover personal hygiene, dressing, meal support, rehabilitation exercises, companionship, medication management, wound care, and appointment accompaniment. A minimum daily engagement of six hours is required, with flexible arrangements and no exit fees — a client-friendly policy that distinguishes them from agencies with lock-in contracts. Google rates this provider at 5 stars across 25 reviews, which is consistent with the profile of a boutique agency where the team knows each client personally.

Cality is appropriate for families who want a professionally managed home care team rather than a nursing home placement, particularly for post-hospitalisation recovery, chronic condition management at home, or supplementing family care for a dependent elder. Call them during office hours (Monday to Friday, 9am–5pm) at 011-6226 3227 to discuss the specific care needs, confirm geographic coverage for your area of Selangor or KL, and obtain a detailed quote. Ask about minimum engagement hours for lower-dependency cases and whether overnight or 24-hour live-in arrangements are available.
address: 49-2, KL Traders Square, Block C, Jln Gombak, GOMBAK, 53000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Lecadia Primacare Centre Sdn. Bhd.
editorial_summary:
Lecadia Primacare Centre Sdn Bhd has operated from 417-425 Jalan Pudu since 2004, making it one of the longer-established mid-tier nursing homes in central Kuala Lumpur. The operator is registered with the Ministry of Health and runs the home as a long-term geriatric and chronic-illness facility rather than a short-stay rehab centre. Spread across a row of shoplots, it has more capacity and a more institutional feel than the converted-house homes common in KL.

Lecadia's published clinical model includes credentialled ward doctors, staff nurses (SRNs), qualified physiotherapists and care assistants. It accepts residents with chronic illness and disability requiring long-term nursing, and provides physiotherapy and rehabilitation on site. Room layouts include both shared rooms and rooms with attached bathrooms; specific bed numbers and rates are not listed publicly. The Google profile carries a 3.8 rating across 131 reviews, a notably larger review base than most competitors and a mixed signal: families generally praise the nursing communication and food, while lower-rated reviews are worth reading in detail before committing.

Likely fit: families seeking an established, MOH-registered home in central KL for a relative needing long-term nursing rather than premium amenity. Ask the centre for: current monthly rate range by room type, whether the ward doctor is salaried or visiting and how often, RN-to-resident ratio overnight, palliative-care experience, and how they handle complaints given the rating spread. Request the JKM licence number and most recent inspection date before signing.
address: 4TH FLOOR 417-425, JALAN PUDU, 55100 KUALA LUMPUR

title: KV Guardian Senior Care Centre (Nursing Home)
editorial_summary:
KV Guardian Senior Care Centre sits at No. 6 Jalan Persiaran Titiwangsa 3, in the Taman Tasik Titiwangsa residential pocket near the Titiwangsa lake park. The operator describes itself as a family-owned home and brands the Titiwangsa site as both a senior care centre and a nursing home. There is a published company website but founding year, ownership names and JKM licence number are not stated publicly.

The service list spans assisted living, rehabilitation, medical nursing care, retirement care, care for handicapped adults and short-term stays, with 24-hour staffing and a call-bell system advertised in each room. The home accommodates both short-stay respite and long-term residents. Capacity is not disclosed; the site emphasises personal attention and a small-home environment rather than ward-style numbers. Google rating is 4.3 over 34 reviews, a reasonable sample for a home of this size and consistent with a well-run mid-market operation.

The Titiwangsa location is convenient for families based in Setapak, Wangsa Maju or central KL, and the lake-side setting is genuinely quieter than a shoplot home. Ask directly for: bed count and current vacancies, monthly rate by room type, whether a doctor visits routinely on a set schedule, registered-nurse cover at night, dementia-wandering precautions if the building is residential, and the JKM registration certificate. Confirm whether physio and rehabilitation are included in the room rate or billed per session.
address: No 6, Persiaran Titiwangsa 3, Titiwangsa, 53200 Wilayah Persekutuan, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: EHA Grandview Titiwangsa KL (Elderly Care | Post-Surgery Recovery | Stroke Rehabilitation ) 吉隆坡湖畔护理疗养中心 #1 Nursing Home KL
care_types: Nursing home, Adult day care center, Assisted living facility
editorial_summary:
EHA Grandview Titiwangsa is the Kuala Lumpur city-centre branch of EHA Eldercare Group, sited near Lake Titiwangsa. The branch is associated in third-party listings with post-surgery recovery and stroke rehabilitation, but EHA's own website does not publish stroke-specific protocols, therapist staffing levels, or rehab equipment lists for this branch — so any rehab specialism should be verified on visit rather than assumed from the listing title. With a 5.0-star rating across 10 Google reviews, the early signal is favourable but the review base is light. The parent group, EHA Eldercare (1478860-U), evolved from the Justlife ElderCare Home brand founded in 2009 and now runs eight residential locations across Malaysia with a combined 100-plus caregivers and 500-plus residents — making it one of the more established private eldercare chains across Johor and the Klang Valley.

The group's website lists eight published service categories that apply across branches: medical support, nutrition and diet, rehabilitation by physiotherapy, social activities, caregiver training and support, skilled nurses, special nursing procedures, and a traditional Chinese medicine approach (acupuncture and herbal). Pricing is published on EHA's website (ehaeldercare.com.my/our-packages/) and is quoted as a starting point — actual fees vary with the resident's care needs. For the Johor Bahru and Klang Valley branches (which include this one), assisted senior living starts at RM 3,200/month for 24-hour residential care across able-bodied, semi-able-bodied, and bed-bound residents; respite stays of one to six months start at RM 120/day; and day care, available 11 hours a day across five days a week with flexible timing, starts at RM 1,500/month. The Kluang branch is priced lower (assisted from RM 2,300/month, respite from RM 80/day, day care from RM 1,300/month) — useful context if the family can travel and cost is a major factor. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

A Titiwangsa location gives Grandview good access to Hospital Kuala Lumpur (HKL), Institut Jantung Negara, and the National Heart Institute corridor — useful for residents who need frequent specialist follow-up. Practical viewing questions: how often a physiotherapist attends in person, who supervises exercise programmes on non-therapist days, what specific clinical procedures are handled in-house versus referred out (PEG feeds, suction, oxygen, wound dressings, urinary catheter changes), and what the home's typical resident profile looks like (post-surgery short-stay vs long-term residential vs dementia care). Confirm the "starting from" rate matches the actual care needs.
address: 11A, Jalan Kuantan, Titiwangsa, 53200 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Mona Elder Care | Nursing Home in Taman Bunga Raya Kuala Lumpur
editorial_summary:
Mona Elder Care runs a small chain of nursing homes across the Klang Valley and Perak, with publicly listed branches in Ampang, Pandan Perdana, Setapak (Taman P. Ramlee), Taiping and a Taman Bunga Raya site in Kuala Lumpur. The Taman Bunga Raya address is not detailed on the company website, but the brand markets itself as an affordable mid-tier operator rather than a premium home and centralises enquiries through a single contact line.

The group's published service list covers dementia care, stroke and coma care, palliative and terminal care, respite stays, handicapped care and home nursing. Clinical capabilities advertised at chain level include bedsore management, tube feeding and changes, colostomy care, catheter care, post-operative support and tracheostomy care, suggesting Taman Bunga Raya can in principle accept higher-needs residents — though branch-level staffing and equipment should be verified in person. Operator name, founding year, JKM licence numbers per branch and bed counts are not on the public site, and the specific Taman Bunga Raya page is not yet indexed.

This branch is likely to suit families looking for a cheaper KL placement within a small multi-branch operator, particularly for a relative needing tube-feeding, stroke recovery or end-of-life care close to home. Because the Taman Bunga Raya site has thin online disclosure, walk in before admitting and ask: exact street address, JKM registration certificate, total beds, RN-to-caregiver ratio overnight, whether the chain's full clinical service list is actually delivered at this branch, and current monthly rates.
address: 2, Jalan Malinja 3, Taman Bunga Raya, 53000 City, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: His Grace Home Care Centre(Taman P.Ramlee)
editorial_summary:
His Grace Home Care Centre operates a branch from 10, Jalan Cempaka in the Taman P. Ramlee neighbourhood of Kuala Lumpur, close to Taman Desa and the MRR2 corridor. The operator also runs a second branch in Setapak. Phone for this branch: +60 11-1162 5831.

The facility has 18 photos documented on Google Maps — a high count for a home of this size, suggesting regular photo updates by staff or family members. Care specifics, capacity and pricing are not published online.

Two reviews average 4.5 stars, describing excellent and attentive service. No website is currently available. Confirm care types, JKM licence number and monthly fees during a site visit.
address: 10, Jalan Cempaka, Taman P Ramlee, 53000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Rejuvenate (Elderly Care Centre)
editorial_summary:
Rejuvenate Elderly Care Centre (RECC) is a three-storey purpose-fitted home in Setapak Indah, Kuala Lumpur, opened in September 2023 by a team of doctors with public-health backgrounds: Dr Siva Kumar Kumarasamy, Dr Mahendraganesh Maraiah and Dr Balachendren Kailasan, alongside business partner Omar Sheriff. The founders frame the centre as a response to Malaysia's ageing-population pressures rather than a purely commercial venture, and have publicly stated they operate fees on a case-by-case basis with reduced charges for families unable to meet standard rates.

The centre takes up to 45 residents, which places it in the small-to-mid-size band. The clinical and care team is reported as four physiotherapists, two clinical staff and four caregivers, with services spanning medical care, physiotherapy, mental-health support through a partnered consultancy, and basic personal care. Pre-admission assessments drive individualised care plans, and the centre runs structured recreational programmes alongside flexible admission options including daycare. Founders have explicitly said they do not want to be a place where families simply drop off elderly parents without engagement. Google rating sits at 5.0 across 9 reviews.

This is a good fit for families wanting a doctor-led, recently established home with a stated charitable ethos and capacity for daycare or trial stays before committing to long-term residency. Practical questions to confirm: registered-nurse cover overnight (the published staff list is light on RNs), the exact JKM licence number, capacity for PEG-fed or tracheostomy residents, the dementia-care setup within the three-storey layout, and how the case-by-case fee model is documented in writing.
address: 7, jalan 2a/6 taman setapak indah, Setapak indah, 53300 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: His Grace Home Care Centre
editorial_summary:
His Grace Home Care Centre is a residential care facility at 4, Jalan Perusahaan 1, off Jalan Genting Kelang, Setapak, Kuala Lumpur — a light commercial street in the Setapak area, accessible from both Jalan Genting Kelang and the Kerinchi Link. The operator also runs a second branch in the Taman P. Ramlee neighbourhood. Phone: +60 16-671 5732.

Two Google Maps photos show the facility interior. Care specifics, capacity and pricing are not published online.

Two reviews average 5.0 stars. Confirm care types accepted, current bed availability, JKM licence number and monthly fees when you visit.
address: 4, Jalan Perusahaan 1, off Jalan Genting Kelang, Setapak, 53300 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Mona Elder Care | Nursing Home in Taman P Ramlee, Setapak
editorial_summary:
Mona Elder Care runs a nursing home at 57, Jalan Teratai 2, Taman P. Ramlee, Setapak, on a quiet residential road inside Kuala Lumpur's northern suburbs. The Setapak branch is one of several Mona Elder Care houses in Peninsular Malaysia (others include Pandan Perdana and Taiping). The operator presents itself as a 24-hour eldercare service and accepts both long-stay residents and shorter respite bookings. Google reviews are sparse but positive at 4.8 across 9 ratings as of mid-2026, so prospective families should treat the public sample as small.

The Setapak house lists clinical nursing as its main work rather than purely custodial care. Mona Elder Care advertises support for stroke and coma recovery, post-operative recovery, palliative and terminal care, dementia, and care for adults with disabilities. On the procedure side the company says its nurses handle wound and pressure-sore management, nasogastric (NG) tube changes, urinary catheter care, colostomy stoma care, and tracheostomy maintenance. Day care and short-term respite stays are offered alongside long-term residence. Bed count, room types, nurse-to-resident ratios, doctor visit schedules, and pricing tiers are not published online for this branch.

The Setapak location is a fit for families looking for nurse-led care close to Kuala Lumpur city centre, particularly for residents discharged from hospital with tubes, wounds, or recent strokes. Columbia Asia Setapak and KPJ Tawakkal are nearby for escalation. Because the company runs multiple houses under one brand, ask specifically about the Taman P. Ramlee unit when calling: how many beds, room layouts, the registered nurse roster overnight, monthly fees by room type, what is included versus charged extra (diapers, milk feeds, physio, doctor visits), and the JKM Pusat Jagaan registration number. Photos and a tour are worth requesting before committing.
address: 57, jalan Teratai 2 Taman P.Ramlee, w.p KL, Setapak, 53300 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: EHA Grandview Eldercare - #1 Nursing Home KL (Kepong / Desa Parkcity / Jalan Ipoh)
care_types: Nursing home, Adult day care center, Assisted living facility
editorial_summary:
EHA Grandview Eldercare in Kepong is the north-Kuala Lumpur branch of EHA Eldercare Group, serving the Kepong, Desa Parkcity, and Jalan Ipoh corridor. With a 5.0-star rating across 27 Google reviews, it carries a stronger early track record than the Titiwangsa branch and is one of the better-reviewed KL eldercare options on Google. The parent group, EHA Eldercare (1478860-U), evolved from the Justlife ElderCare Home brand founded in 2009 and now runs eight residential locations across Malaysia with a combined 100-plus caregivers and 500-plus residents — making it one of the more established private eldercare chains across Johor and the Klang Valley.

The group's website lists eight published service categories that apply across branches: medical support, nutrition and diet, rehabilitation by physiotherapy, social activities, caregiver training and support, skilled nurses, special nursing procedures, and a traditional Chinese medicine approach (acupuncture and herbal). Pricing is published on EHA's website (ehaeldercare.com.my/our-packages/) and is quoted as a starting point — actual fees vary with the resident's care needs. For the Johor Bahru and Klang Valley branches (which include this one), assisted senior living starts at RM 3,200/month for 24-hour residential care across able-bodied, semi-able-bodied, and bed-bound residents; respite stays of one to six months start at RM 120/day; and day care, available 11 hours a day across five days a week with flexible timing, starts at RM 1,500/month. The Kluang branch is priced lower (assisted from RM 2,300/month, respite from RM 80/day, day care from RM 1,300/month) — useful context if the family can travel and cost is a major factor. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

Kepong gives the branch good access to Hospital Sungai Buloh and the Damansara hospital cluster (Sunway, KPJ Damansara, ParkCity Medical Centre), and proximity to the relatively quiet residential neighbourhoods of Desa Parkcity and Bandar Menjalara. Practical viewing questions: how many residents are currently in the home, how many caregivers cover each shift, whether RN cover is on-site overnight or on-call, and what clinical procedures are handled in-house versus referred out. Confirm the "starting from" rate matches the actual care plan since add-ons can shift the monthly figure.
address: 6, Lengkungan Vethavanam, Taman Kaya, 51100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: 2e Nursing & Healthcare
care_types: Nursing home, Service establishment, Health consultant
editorial_summary:
2E Nursing & Healthcare appears on Google Maps as a Kuala Lumpur nursing home with strong but very limited reviews (5.0 across 20 ratings as of mid-2026). It is also listed under categories "service establishment" and "health consultant", which suggests the operator may combine residential elder care with home-nursing and consultancy. There is no live website, public Facebook page, or directory listing on the major Malaysian eldercare aggregators (hati.my, ielder.asia, Mintygreen's KL roundups), so almost no public detail is verifiable.

Families considering this home should call before visiting and ask: exact street address and area, JKM Pusat Jagaan registration number, total beds and current vacancy, room types and monthly fees, whether registered nurses are rostered overnight, doctor visit arrangement, halal kitchen status, languages spoken by carers, and whether they take residents on NG/PEG feeds, with wounds, or with dementia who wander. Request a site visit and photographs of bedrooms, bathrooms, dining and outdoor areas before any deposit.

title: Eldercare Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Eldercare Nursing Home is located in Taman Putra Perdana, a residential township in Puchong, Selangor. The home is listed under the care type of nursing home and can be reached at +60 13-280 6969. Taman Putra Perdana sits in a broadly accessible part of southern Selangor, within reasonable driving distance of Puchong and the Putrajaya–Cyberjaya corridor, making it potentially convenient for families based in those areas. The home maintains a low online profile, with no website or Facebook presence surfaced through research, so most service detail is best gathered directly on a call.",

      "The Google Maps listing confirms the home's presence in the area, though granular operational data — including bed count, staffing levels, and specific care programmes — is not publicly documented. As a nursing home listing, families can expect residential care with nursing support as a baseline, but the range of clinical services (wound care, tube feeding, dementia management, physiotherapy) and the level of medical oversight available should be verified directly. Similarly, pricing, room configurations, and visitor policy are not published online.",

      "When calling, it is worth asking about current bed availability, the day and overnight nurse-to-resident ratio, which doctor or clinic provides medical oversight, and whether the home accepts residents with specific conditions such as dementia, post-stroke dependency, or PEG feeding. Ask also about subsidy eligibility and whether the home is registered with JKM, and request to see the licence and any recent JKM inspection report during a site visit. Language of care is unconfirmed — ask whether Malay, English, Mandarin, or Tamil-speaking staff are available to match your family's communication preferences.
address: No. 1&, 3, Jalan Aman Putra 1/4, Taman Putra Perdana, 47100 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Golden Years Senior Residence Kinrara Puchong
editorial_summary:
Golden Years Senior Residence operates two Selangor branches — its primary site in Kota Kemuning, Shah Alam, and a second location at 52, Jalan BK 4/3, Bandar Kinrara, Puchong. The Kinrara branch serves families in the southern Klang Valley who want professional nursing care without travelling into Shah Alam. The group positions itself as a biophilic-design nursing home, with the premises renovated to incorporate natural materials and green spaces, aiming for a less institutional environment. It carries a strong 4.7-star Google rating from 14 reviews.

The website indicates a multi-disciplinary care team that includes family medicine and internal medicine doctors, a head nurse with over 35 years of experience, registered nurses, physiotherapists, and care managers. Day-care (Monday to Friday, 8:30am–6:00pm) and residential stay-in care are both offered. The website publishes a fee structure: live-in care is listed from RM 2,950/month for low dependency, RM 3,450 for medium, and RM 3,950 for high dependency. Short-term stays are listed at RM 220–300 per day. Individual physiotherapy sessions are listed at RM 100, and occupational therapy at RM 200 per session. Activities include morning group exercises, mahjong, art classes, bakery classes, and festival celebrations.

The pricing structure is clear and published, which is a positive signal for transparency, though families should confirm the Kinrara branch uses the same fee schedule as Kota Kemuning. It is also worth asking whether the full medical team — including doctors and the experienced head nurse — is shared across both branches or based full-time at Kota Kemuning only. Visiting hours are listed as 10:00am–7:30pm daily. A refundable booking deposit of RM 3,500 is required for stay-in placement.
address: 52, Jalan BK 4/3, Bandar Kinrara, 47180 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Guardian Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Guardian Care Centre is listed on Google Maps as a nursing home in Bandar Kinrara, Puchong, Selangor, contactable at +60 11-6191 0145. It holds a 5.0-star rating from 11 Google reviews — a strong score, though based on a small review pool. The Bandar Kinrara location places it in a well-established residential township near Puchong, with access to KPJ Ambulatory Care Centre Kinrara and major road links. No website, Facebook page, or any third-party directory listing was found during research.

With no public-facing digital presence, the facility's capacity, care types handled, staffing model, pricing, or any other operational detail cannot be verified from external sources. The name 'Guardian Care Centre' does not appear in any of the major nursing home directories or aggregator listings for Puchong or Bandar Kinrara. This is consistent with a small, owner-operated neighbourhood home that relies on word-of-mouth referrals rather than digital marketing.

The strong Google rating, despite its limited base, is worth weighing positively — it suggests residents or families who have engaged with the facility have had good experiences. However, any family considering this home should conduct an in-person visit as the primary due-diligence step, since no information can be cross-checked online. Key questions to ask include: How many beds are in operation? What dependency levels do you accept? Are qualified nurses on duty overnight? How are medical emergencies handled? Is the facility JKM-registered and what is the licence number?
address: 30, Jalan BK 1/3, Bandar Kinrara, 47180 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Harvestars Care Centre (Puchong, D'Island)
editorial_summary:
Harvestars Care Centre at D'Island Puchong opened in April 2023 as the brand's flagship facility and is marketed as Malaysia's first nursing care home built in a stand-alone condo style. Located at 9 Jalan Barat 5 in the D'Island Residence township, the home occupies a triple-storey superlink building with a lift installed for resident transfers. The facility is listed as having approximately 100 beds and sits within an established residential area in Puchong, roughly 20 kilometres south-west of Kuala Lumpur.

Harvestars specialises in assisted living, post-hospitalisation recovery, dementia and Alzheimer's care, and palliative support. The care team includes registered nurses, caregivers, doctors, physiotherapists, and practitioners of traditional Chinese medicine, providing 24-hour coverage. Four meals per day are prepared by an experienced chef, with menus designed for special dietary needs. Resident amenities include karaoke, mahjong, massage rooms, and a cafeteria — a more activity-focused offering than is typical for this price bracket. The website lists nursing home fees starting from RM 4,899 per month and day care from RM 2,899 per month.

As a facility that opened in 2023, Harvestars D'Island is relatively new and its track record is still accumulating. Families should ask about nurse-to-resident ratios on night shifts, the extent of the TCM practitioners' role (supplementary wellness or clinical?), and the precise clinical capabilities available in-house versus those requiring hospital transfer. The condo-style layout may affect ventilation and outdoor space compared with landed equivalents — a site visit during the day is worthwhile. Verify the current JKM registration and enquire about whether the listed starting price covers all nursing needs or applies only to lower-dependency residents.
address: Residence, 9, Jalan Barat 5, D'island, 47130 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Master Care Nursing homes(Puteri Wangsa)
editorial_summary:
This listing for Master Care Nursing Homes (Puteri Wangsa) carries a single Google review (5.0) and a Taman Puteri Wangsa address. Taman Puteri Wangsa is in Ulu Tiram, Johor, not Kuala Lumpur, so the state assignment in the directory should be checked. There is a separate Johor-headquartered brand called Master Care Centre (masternursingcare.com) with branches in Bandar JB, Taman Abad and Taman Daya, but its public site does not list a Puteri Wangsa branch, so it is not clear whether this is the same operator or an unrelated home reusing the words "master care".

Because the public footprint is one Google review and no website, families should treat this as unverified. Useful questions on a call: who runs the home and is it part of the Master Care Centre group, JKM Pusat Jagaan licence number, total beds, monthly fees, RN coverage overnight, dementia and PEG capability, halal kitchen, and which hospital they would use in an emergency. Ask for the full address before visiting and verify against the JKM Pusat Jagaan directory.
address: 20, Jalan Beladau 10, Taman Puteri Wangsa, 81800 Ulu Tiram, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

title: Pristine Nursing Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Pristine Nursing Care Centre is located in Bandar Puchong Jaya, Puchong — a mature township south of Puchong town proper with shophouse rows and landed residential estates. A Facebook presence exists under 'Pristine Senior Care Centre', describing the facility as providing care and support for seniors and disabled individuals in a warm and supportive environment. The facility carries a 4.3 Google rating across 19 reviews. The care type is listed as nursing home.

Beyond the basic listing and Facebook presence, public information is minimal. No website is currently operational, and no detailed service, pricing, or capacity information is available from online sources. Based on the Bandar Puchong Jaya location and the general pattern of care homes in this part of Puchong, this is most likely a small-to-medium residential nursing home operating from a converted shophouse or linked terrace unit. The name 'Pristine' and the self-description suggest a private, for-profit operation aiming at a mid-range market.

Families interested in this facility should call +60 19-397 6211 and arrange a visit to assess the physical environment and staffing directly, as online information is insufficient to draw further conclusions. Key questions to bring: How many beds does the facility have, and what is the current occupancy? Is there a registered nurse on site overnight? What medical conditions can the home manage — including dementia, PEG feeding, or wound care? Does the facility hold a current JKM operating licence? A personal visit is the most reliable way to evaluate Pristine given the scarcity of publicly available information.
address: 11, Jalan Serindit 1a, Bandar Puchong Jaya, 47170 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Vida Senior Care
care_types: Assisted living facility, Nursing home, Aged care
editorial_summary:
Vida Senior Care occupies a shophouse at 59, Jalan Wawasan 3/5, Pusat Bandar Puchong, 47100 Puchong — in the commercial hub of Puchong town, with easy access from the LDP and Puchong-Kinrara road. The facility describes itself as an aged care, assisted living, and nursing home operation, with Google listing those three categories. The facility holds a 4.8 Google rating across 20 reviews.

The website and Google Business profile indicate Vida Senior Care employs a combination of on-duty doctors, certified nurses, and a full-time physiotherapist, providing medical attention around the clock. The model appears to target rehabilitation and post-acute cases — coma patients and those recovering from stroke are specifically referenced in reviewer accounts alongside general elderly residential care. Services described include bedside nursing and medical dressing, chronic kidney disease management, care for immobilised patients, and 24-hour monitoring. The facility is wheelchair accessible with accessible parking and restrooms. Reviewers highlight prompt doctor responsiveness and family communication as strengths, with specific praise for the physiotherapy team.

Bed count and pricing are not published online — call +60 10-258 0101 to get current availability and rates. Because Vida presents itself partly as a rehabilitation-oriented centre, families should clarify on a visit whether long-term residential placement is available or whether the primary model is a shorter post-acute stay. The Pusat Bandar Puchong location in a busy commercial row means the environment is urban rather than tranquil — not a concern for clinically focused families but worth factoring in for residents who value a quieter setting.
address: 59, Jalan Wawasan 3/5, Pusat Bandar Puchong, 47100 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Jia Yuan Retirement Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Jia Yuan Retirement Care Centre is located at Jalan PP1/6, Taman Putra Prima, Puchong — a residential township in the southwest Klang Valley that sits roughly equidistant between Puchong town and Putrajaya. The facility is listed in Google Maps as a retirement home and has a registered company number of 202401017567, suggesting incorporation in 2024. With a 4.1-star rating from 17 Google reviewers, it is one of the smaller-volume reviewed facilities in this batch, and publicly available information about its operations, staffing, and clinical scope is very limited.

Based on its listing category — retirement home rather than nursing home — Jia Yuan likely caters primarily to ambulant or semi-ambulant seniors who need social care and a supervised living environment rather than intensive medical nursing. Taman Putra Prima is a landed residential area, which typically means care homes operating there are shophouse or bungalow conversions rather than purpose-built facilities. No website, Facebook page, or brochure material was identified during research, so there is no way to verify care types, bed count, staffing credentials, or pricing independently from a third-party source.

Families considering Jia Yuan should contact the listed number (+60 11-5455 2599) and ask to visit in person before any decision. Key questions include whether the facility holds a current JKM licence, how many beds are available, what medical oversight exists (on-call doctor or nurse on duty), what happens if a resident's condition deteriorates, and whether the location is equipped for wheelchair or bedridden residents given the likely residential building format. The 2024 registration date means the facility is relatively new, which is worth factoring into your evaluation.
address: No. 2, Jalan PP1/6, Taman Putra Prima, 47130 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Elderlove Living Care Centre (Puchong)
editorial_summary:
Elderlove Living Care Centre's Puchong branch occupies ground-floor shophouse units (SS-G-23, SS-G-23A, and SS-G-25) in Skypod Square on Persiaran Puchong Jaya Selatan, Bandar Puchong Jaya — a commercial podium in one of Puchong's newer mixed-use developments with easy carpark access and proximity to major roads. This is one of several Elderlove branches across the Klang Valley (others include Sungai Long and Kajang), and unusually for the sector, the group was co-founded by two medical specialists — a physician and a neurologist — supported by a team that includes a geriatrician, nephrologist, physiotherapists, occupational therapists, and a dietitian. That clinical founding team is reflected in the care scope and in the dementia and neurological care programme specifically.

Elderlovemissi.com documents pricing for Puchong as ranging from approximately RM 3,400 to RM 4,500 per month for long-term care depending on room occupancy, with single beds at the top and five-to-six-bedded rooms at the lower end; day care runs at RM 110–135 per day. The package includes three main meals, whole-day snacks, laundry, weekly physiotherapy, monthly specialist review, yearly blood test, monthly grooming, and a monthly vitamin infusion — a notably inclusive bundle compared to facilities that charge many of these as extras. Rooms come in two-bedded, four-bedded, and six-bedded configurations with air-conditioning, safety handrails, ramps, non-slip flooring, and emergency call systems.

All nursing staff are described as registered Malaysian nurses with SKM Level 3 certification and CPR certification; an AI fitness centre, cooking and baking sessions, crafts, board games, and health talks round out the activities programme. Families considering this branch for a cognitively impaired relative should ask specifically whether the dementia programme run by the Geriatrician and Neurologist is active at the Puchong site, or whether residents need to attend another branch for specialist sessions. No religious affiliation is stated. The 5-star Google rating from 15 reviews is a small sample — ask for family references.
address: Unit SS-G-23, SS-G-23A & SS-G-25, Ground Floor, Skypod Square, Persiaran Puchong Jaya Selatan, Bandar Puchong Jaya, 47100 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: JJ Blessing Care Centre Puchong
editorial_summary:
JJ Blessing Care Centre (互爱安老疗养中心) operates a branch at 12 Jalan Prima 6/3 in Taman Puchong Prima, Puchong — part of a small multi-site operator whose other known locations include Taman OUG in Kuala Lumpur and a branch in Ipoh. The Puchong Prima address is a residential-zone shophouse cluster, conveniently placed in an established neighbourhood with good road access to the Puchong Perdana and IOI City areas. Google Maps lists it under aged care with a rating of 4.6 from 44 reviews, which points to a reasonably well-regarded day-to-day operation even if the centre maintains a low public profile.

Public information on specific services is sparse. The facility's Chinese name translates roughly as 'Mutual Love Elder Care and Nursing Centre', though no detailed service menu is publicly available. Operating hours listed on directory sites run from approximately 10:30am to 6:30pm, which is consistent with a centre that offers day care as its primary service, though whether 24-hour residential nursing is also available is not confirmed in any source reviewed.

Families considering JJ Blessing Puchong should ask directly about what care levels are accepted (ambulant-only versus bedridden), whether there is overnight nursing coverage, and the ratio of trained nurses to caregivers. No pricing, bed count, or JKM licence number is publicly stated. Given the limited online footprint, a site visit before committing is especially advisable to assess actual clinical capacity and environment.
address: 12, Jalan Prima 6/3, Taman Puchong Prima, 47100 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Genesis Life Care Centre Puchong
editorial_summary:
Genesis Life Care Centre Puchong sits in Taman Perindustrian Puchong, with highway access via the LDP and ELITE corridors. It is one of five Genesis Life Care branches in the Klang Valley and Johor — the others being Petaling Jaya, Klang, Kajang and Johor Bahru — operating under a chain that the operator describes as Ministry-approved and JKM-registered. The Puchong centre opened in 2022 and is the chain's largest single site, with the operator stating capacity for 120+ residents.

The published service mix covers six categories: Nursing Home Care, Dementia & Memory Care, Stroke Rehabilitation, Palliative Care, Post-Operative Recovery, and Senior Day Care. The dementia programme is described as psychologist-led, with structured cognitive stimulation activities. Stroke rehabilitation is delivered through dedicated physiotherapy and occupational therapy. Across all care levels the operator lists 24/7 nursing supervision, weekly doctor consultations, dietician-supervised meals, medication management and personalised care plans. Senior Day Care typically runs 8am–5pm on weekdays and is useful for families not yet ready for full residential placement.

Pricing is not posted on the Genesis Life Care website for any branch — final monthly fees depend on care level and room type, so request a written quote during your visit. Because the Puchong site is large and stroke rehab and dementia care are headline services, two questions are worth asking on viewing: what the night-shift nurse-to-resident ratio looks like at full occupancy, and whether residents with advanced dementia share common areas or are managed in a dedicated wing. Day care availability also makes the centre worth considering as a staged option before full admission.
address: 16, Jln TPP 1/1, Taman Perindustrian Puchong, 47100 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Unique Family Care Center
editorial_summary:
Unique Family Care Center is located at 2, Jalan PU 1/10A, Taman Puchong Utama, 47140 Puchong — a landed residential estate in the northeastern part of Puchong, roughly midway between Puchong Jaya and the Bukit Jalil corridor. Google Maps lists it as a nursing home and senior citizen centre, and it carries a 4.9-star rating from 15 reviewers. The 'family' emphasis in the name and the residential address are consistent with a home-like, smaller-scale facility operating out of a converted house or linked-terrace rather than a purpose-built care complex. No website was identified, and the facility's nursinghome.my listing explicitly notes it does not use a computer system.

Publicly available information about the scope of care, staffing, bed count, and pricing is essentially absent. The two contact numbers on record — +60 14-332 7562 and +60 16-202 6751 — are the only confirmed reach points. The senior citizen centre classification alongside nursing home suggests the facility may serve a mix of ambulant day-care residents and those requiring full residential nursing care, which is a common arrangement in smaller community homes in Puchong. The high rating despite the thin online footprint suggests it may rely on word-of-mouth referrals within a close-knit community.

Any family considering Unique Family Care Center should arrange an in-person visit before making any decision. Ask about the total number of beds, the ratio of nursing-trained staff to residents, what conditions are accepted (particularly whether stroke, dementia, tube-feeding, or bedridden cases are managed), what happens medically in an emergency, and whether the facility holds a current JKM licence. The residential setting and small online presence mean independent verification is limited — a face-to-face assessment is especially important here.
address: 2, Jalan PU 1/10A, Taman Puchong Utama, 47140 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: KS Care Centre Shah Alam
editorial_summary:
KS Care Centre (凯心安老院, meaning "Caring Heart Senior Home") is a professionally run, Chinese-managed nursing home with two Selangor branches — one in Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang, and one at Jalan Pulau Angsa U10/32F in Shah Alam, which is the branch listed here in the Bandar Nusa Rhu area. The Shah Alam branch operates seven days a week from 8:30 am to 10 pm, suggesting an active extended-day model rather than purely overnight residential care, though it markets itself as a live-in facility offering round-the-clock support.

KS Care Centre's published service list covers nursing home (live-in) care, dementia care, stroke and post-cancer recovery, handicapped support, and outreach home nursing. The Shah Alam branch advertises pricing starting from approximately RM 2,950 per month for low-dependency residents, RM 3,450 for medium, and RM 3,950 for high dependency — positioning it at the mid-to-upper end of the Selangor market. Notably, the founding nurses are described as holding 25 years of Assunta Hospital experience, which, if verifiable, indicates a clinically trained core team. Recreational activities and nutritional meal planning are stated programme components.

Bed count for the Shah Alam branch specifically is not published, so families should confirm capacity and current availability during enquiry. Ask whether the dementia care programme includes secured access to prevent wandering, what the overnight nurse ratio is, and whether short-term or respite admissions are available. The facility has only one published Google review, so independent family testimonials are limited — a site visit before committing is strongly advisable.
address: 58, Jalan Pulau Angsa U10/32f, Bandar Nusa Rhu, 40170 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Woodrose Senior Residences 1 Seksyen 11 (Female - Syariah Complaint)
editorial_summary:
Woodrose Senior Residences 1 is the female wing of the Woodrose Healthcare campus in Seksyen 11, Shah Alam — located on Jalan Silat, a short distance from the male Residence 2 on Jalan Gamelan. Operated by Woodrose Senior Residences Sdn Bhd, this is the earlier of the two Seksyen 11 homes and features 2-bedded and 4-bedded rooms along with a swimming pool, which can be used for hydrotherapy and light aquatic exercise. The facility caters to Muslim female elderly residents who require nursing home, dementia, palliative, respite, or day care services in a Shariah-compliant environment.

The Shariah compliance programme at Woodrose — shared across both Seksyen 11 residences — was developed with an in-house Ustaz to govern caregiving SOPs, dietary preparation (halal food), spiritual programming, and the management of personal care in a gender-appropriate manner. Female residents benefit from female-only caregiving staff, Islamic daily routines, and a care environment designed with their religious and emotional comfort in mind. The broader Woodrose service offer includes dementia and Alzheimer's management, stroke rehabilitation, physiotherapy, and palliative care, all available to residents of Residence 1. The Google rating for Residence 1 (3.9 stars, 26 reviews) is notably lower than Residence 2 (4.2 stars), which is worth exploring further with the operator.

Pricing is not published and should be obtained directly by calling the shared line (+60 12-709 8102). Ask specifically about current capacity at Residence 1, staff-to-resident ratios, and how high-dependency or bed-bound residents are managed. The lower Google rating relative to sibling homes warrants a candid conversation and, ideally, an in-person visit before placement. Confirm JKM registration status and the frequency of physician oversight.
address: No. 28, Jalan Silat 11/3, Seksyen 11, 40100 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Caring Hearts Elder Care Centre
care_types: Nursing home, Senior citizen center, Aged care
editorial_summary:
Caring Hearts Elder Care Centre is situated in Taman Bukit Cantik, a residential area in Selangor, and lists its care types as nursing home, senior citizen centre, and aged care — a combination that suggests the facility may accommodate residents across a range of dependency levels, from those needing companionship and light support through to residents requiring nursing care. The centre can be reached at +60 12-671 1360. A Facebook page exists for the centre under the Kajang listing on the platform, indicating a Selangor presence, though a dedicated website has not been surfaced through research.

The breadth of the listed care categories — nursing home, senior citizen centre, and aged care — indicates that Caring Hearts may serve residents who are relatively independent alongside those with higher dependency needs. This can work well for families planning ahead, as a resident can potentially age in place rather than moving facilities as needs change. Specific operational details such as bed count, staffing ratios, clinical services available, and programme schedules are not publicly documented, so they should be confirmed directly with the home.

Visiting the home in person is strongly recommended before making a commitment, particularly to assess the physical environment, observe interactions between staff and residents, and understand how care is escalated when a resident's condition changes. On a call, ask about the nurse-to-resident ratio on day and night shifts, which doctor provides medical oversight, what happens when a resident requires hospital transfer, and what the pricing structure covers versus what attracts additional charges. Ask also about language of care and whether staff are comfortable communicating in your family member's preferred language.
address: 2, Jalan Tiga, Taman Bukit Cantik, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Selcare Nursing
editorial_summary:
Selcare Nursing operates under Selgate Corporation — a wholly owned subsidiary of Perbadanan Kemajuan Negeri Selangor (PKNS), the state's development agency — and is based at No. 15, Jalan Pembangunan, Seksyen 14, Shah Alam. Selgate runs an integrated healthcare network under the Selcare brand that includes clinics, dental, pharmacy, diagnostics, and nursing. The nursing arm is listed alongside these services as a distinct offering rather than as a standalone residential nursing home. It carries a perfect 5.0-star Google rating from 12 reviews and is categorised on Google Maps as a nursing home and physical therapist, suggesting physiotherapy is part of its service model.

Publicly available information about Selcare Nursing's specific residential or nursing home operations is limited. The Selcare website indicates a nursing service that is bookable through their digital platform, which suggests a home nursing deployment model is at least part of what they offer — dispatching nurses to patients at home or in clinical settings. The PKNS parentage suggests a degree of institutional accountability, and the co-location with clinics and diagnostics in Seksyen 14 means residents or clients may have ready access to integrated medical services under one umbrella.

Families should contact Selcare Nursing directly to clarify whether this operates as a residential care facility with on-site beds or primarily as a home nursing deployment service. If residential beds exist, ask about capacity, staffing, dependency levels accepted, and whether the physiotherapy service is provided in-house or on referral. The State-linked ownership structure may be reassuring in terms of regulatory compliance, but the limited public information makes direct enquiry essential before drawing any conclusions about suitability.
address: No. 1-03A, Level 1, Fateh Group Of Companies (Plaza Azalea), 14/6, Jalan Pembangunan, Seksyen 14, 40000 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Utopia Care Center
editorial_summary:
Utopia Care Center is listed at an address in Taman Makmur, a residential area in the Ampang district of Selangor, accessible via the Ampang corridor with links to public hospitals serving the east Klang Valley. The Google listing categorises it as a nursing home and shows a contact of 011-3323 0611 with a 5-star rating from a small but consistently positive group of reviewers.

The home keeps a low online profile — no published website, Facebook page, or third-party directory entry was found during research — so most service detail is best gathered directly. Bed count, staffing levels, care scope, pricing, language of operations, and JKM registration are best confirmed with the operator on a call. The Taman Makmur area is predominantly residential, and many smaller care homes in such settings operate from converted terrace houses with a limited bed count.

Families should call 011-3323 0611 to establish whether the home is currently taking residents and what care level is available. Useful questions: how many beds are there, is there a registered nurse on site, can they handle bedridden or post-stroke residents, what is the monthly cost, and what is the current JKM licence number. A site visit before placement is the most reliable way to assess fit, especially for smaller community-style homes.
address: 23, Jalan Makmur 1, Taman Makmur, 43200 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Sunshine Nursing Home Care
editorial_summary:
Sunshine Nursing Home Care is a residential nursing facility at No. 33, Jalan Teluki 2, Bukit Sentosa, 44300 Rawang, Selangor — a township on the northern fringe of the Klang Valley, approximately 35km from central KL. Phone: +60 12-474 6615.

One photo is available on Google Maps. Care details, capacity and pricing are not published online. No website is available.

Two reviews present contrasting experiences: one 5-star review and one 1-star review citing a dispute over deposit refund following a resident's passing. Before admission, families should clarify the deposit and refund policy in writing and ask to see the JKM licence.
address: No 33, Jalan Teluki 2, Bukit Sentosa, 44300 Rawang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Angel Best Centre Nursing Home - Rawang Area
editorial_summary:
Angel Best Centre Nursing Home — known in Chinese as 天使之家养老院 (Angel's Home Nursing Centre) — is located at No. 34 Jalan Setia 2, Taman Setia, Rawang, Selangor. The centre serves the Rawang area (万挠区) and describes itself as a nursing home focused on senior care services. Rawang sits in northern Selangor, roughly 35 kilometres from central Kuala Lumpur via the North-South Expressway, making it accessible for families in Rawang, Kepong, and the northern corridor. The facility's bilingual branding — and its contact number of +60 16-251 5662 — suggests a primarily Chinese-speaking operation, which may be relevant for families seeking Mandarin or Cantonese-comfortable caregiving.",

      "The home is listed as a nursing home on Google Maps and has an active Facebook presence, though a dedicated website has not been surfaced. Detailed operational data including bed count, staffing levels, care programmes, and pricing are not publicly documented online. As the Chinese name and branding lean toward community-level residential care for seniors, the home likely provides personal care, meals, and nursing support as a foundation, but the availability of specific clinical services — dementia care, wound management, physiotherapy, PEG feeding — should be confirmed directly with the team.",

      "A phone enquiry to +60 16-251 5662 is the practical first step, and a site visit to Taman Setia will give the clearest picture of the home's environment and care culture. On the call, ask about current bed availability, the nurse-to-resident ratio across day and night shifts, what medical oversight is in place, and whether the home can support residents with dementia, post-stroke conditions, or incontinence. Ask too about language — whether Cantonese, Mandarin, Malay, or English-speaking staff are on the floor — and whether the home holds a current JKM licence. Pricing and what is included in the monthly rate should be confirmed in writing.
address: 34, Jalan Setia 2, Taman Setia, 48000 Rawang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Well Living Care Centre Sdn. Bhd.
editorial_summary:
Well Living Care Centre Sdn. Bhd. is a licensed, 80-bed nursing home and retirement home located at Lot 4832, Lorong 7, Kampung Sungai Terentang, in the Rawang area of northern Selangor — a hillside rural-residential setting that its AGECOPE listing describes simply as 'a home on the hill.' The Rawang location, while further from central KL than most facilities in this batch, offers quieter surroundings and typically lower pricing compared to urban Klang Valley homes. It is registered with AGECOPE, the industry association for residential care operators, and is classified as a non-NGO commercial facility. Contact is through a mobile number (+60 17-577 5730), consistent with the Kampung Kenanga area listed on the Google profile.

At 80 beds, Well Living is among the larger independent homes in the Rawang corridor. A Facebook page exists under 'Well Living' in Rawang, indicating some community presence, though the page itself could not be accessed to extract programme details. No clinical capability breakdown, pricing, staffing ratios, or programme details are publicly available beyond the basic directory listing. The facility carries a 4.6 Google rating from 10 reviews, consistent with adequate care without standout commendation.

For families considering Rawang, the key practical considerations are proximity to medical services — Hospital Selayang is the nearest tertiary government hospital, approximately 25–30 minutes away, while private options in the area are limited — and transport access for family visits. The capacity of 80 beds suggests the operator has some infrastructure, but the rural setting and sparse online presence mean an in-person visit is especially important. Ask about nursing coverage overnight, which conditions the home can manage versus refer, and what the transfer protocol is in an emergency.
address: 4832, Lorong 7 Kampung Sungai Terentang, Jalan Kampung Baru, Kampung Kenanga, 48000 Rawang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Noble Care | Nursing Home & Old Folks Home Rawang
editorial_summary:
Noble Care Rawang serves as the headquarters for the Noble Care Malaysia network, one of the larger private nursing home chains operating in Peninsular Malaysia with over 15 branches. The Rawang facility is located at D-10 Jalan LKS-4, Lee Kim Sai, 48000 Rawang — a residential area in northern Selangor roughly 35 kilometres from Kuala Lumpur. Noble Care describes itself as having over 16 years of experience in elderly care and positions Rawang as its founding and anchor location.

The Rawang branch provides a broad clinical menu: dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative and respite care, stroke and cancer support, physiotherapy, and skilled nursing procedures including Foley's catheter management, nasogastric tube care, tracheostomy care, colostomy bag changes, bedsore dressing, and post-operative recovery. This is a substantive clinical capability set that makes the home suitable for higher-dependency residents. Round-the-clock nursing cover is listed on the website, with qualified nurses and experienced caregivers providing personalised attention.

Noble Care does not publish pricing on its website, so families will need to call to get a current fee schedule. As with any chain operator, service consistency can vary between branches — the Rawang HQ is likely to have stronger oversight and staffing given its flagship status, but confirming bed availability and current nursing ratios at the time of enquiry is advisable. Ask specifically about the medical review schedule (how often a doctor visits), the palliative care pathway, and whether dementia residents are housed in a separate unit or integrated with the general population. The facility shares a phone number (+60 16-278 6993) with the Ampang branch, so confirm you are speaking with Rawang staff when making enquiries.
address: D10, Jalan Lks 4, Kampung Dato Lee Kim Sai, 48000 Rawang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Elderlove Living Care Centre (Sg.Long)
care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living facility, Day care center
editorial_summary:
Elderlove Living Care Centre (Sg Long) is located at No. 11 Jalan Budiman, Budiman Business Park, Bandar Sungai Long — a light-industrial business park setting in the Kajang-Cheras corridor, accessible from the SILK Highway. The centre is the Sungai Long branch of a multi-site operator founded by Dr CS Lim, a consultant nephrologist, whose specialist background shaped the organisation's clinical positioning. Elderlove received the Malaysia Health and Wellness Brand Award in 2018 — cited as the first nursing home in Malaysia to do so — and runs five branches across the Klang Valley and Ipoh.

The service range is well-documented: 24/7 nursing with registered Malaysian nurses and SKM-3 certified caregivers, physiotherapy and rehabilitation, structured dementia care with trained caregivers and safe environmental adaptations, an AI Fitness Centre, and a day care programme running 9am to 6pm. An in-house specialist doctor conducts monthly reviews for residential patients; daily health monitoring is handled by nursing staff. Pricing is published on the website: long-term monthly rates run from RM3,500 (6-bedded room) to RM4,250 (2-bedded room); day care is RM125 per day for standard dependency, RM135 for higher dependency.

The business park location means there is no residential streetscape feel — outdoor space and natural light are worth asking about specifically, as they vary considerably between shopblock nursing homes. The monthly specialist review cycle (rather than weekly GP visits) suits stable long-term residents but may be insufficient for those with active medical complexity; confirm what the escalation pathway is for urgent clinical needs. Day care may suit families in the Kajang-Cheras corridor who want structured programming and nursing oversight without full residential placement.
address: Budiman Business Park, 11, Jln Budiman, Bandar Sungai Long, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: KS Care Centre Kajang
editorial_summary:
KS Care Centre (凯心安老院) sits at No. 28, Jalan Ikhlas 5 in Bandar Sungai Long, a residential township on the Kajang–Cheras fringe of Selangor. The Chinese name — meaning roughly "benevolent heart elder home" — signals its primary audience: Mandarin-speaking families looking for a home-like environment rather than a clinical institution. The centre operates 365 days a year with stated 24-hour caregiving coverage, positioning itself as a full residential option rather than a day-centre. A second branch exists in Shah Alam, suggesting modest chain ambitions.

The service menu spans six named categories: Senior Citizen Care, Stroke and Cancer Care, Dementia Care, Handicapped Care, Retirement Resort, and Home Nursing. Staff are described as having up to 25 years of hospital experience, though exact nurse-to-resident ratios and clinical qualifications are not publicly disclosed. Pricing found online lists four-bed rooms at RM 2,000–2,300/month, three-bed at RM 2,500, and two-bed at RM 2,700, with a first-three-months promotional rate from RM 1,700 — placing it in the budget-to-mid range for Kajang. Hours of 8:30am to 10pm suggest visiting is structured rather than open-ended.

Before committing, families should ask specifically what the dementia care offering entails — whether it involves a dedicated wing or just higher supervision. It is also worth clarifying staffing levels overnight, given the 8:30am–10pm posted hours versus the claimed 24-hour care. The Shah Alam branch may have different standards to the Kajang original, so visiting the specific site your relative would enter is essential. Bed count, licence number, and physiotherapy availability are all gaps to close during enquiry.
address: 28, Jalan Ikhlas 5, Bandar Sungai Long, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Sincere Care Home
editorial_summary:
Sincere Care Home, operating under the registered name Pertubuhan Ikhlas Malaysia, is a charitable welfare home established in 2002 by Madam Danapakiyam. It is located at Lot 18367, Jalan Kelapa, Kajang, Selangor — off Jalan Semenyih — and has a lengthy track record of housing vulnerable elderly people who have been abandoned, discharged from hospital without family support, or who are unable to provide for themselves. The home serves a mixed population: elderly residents with varying levels of dependency alongside a small number of orphans, and it explicitly accepts individuals regardless of gender. Hospital Kajang and KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital are both within a short drive, which provides a useful safety net for acute medical events.",

      "Based on charity documentation and third-party write-ups, the home was caring for approximately 84 elderly residents and 6 orphans at the time of reporting, with many residents described as having mental health conditions or being semi-independent. The home's priority is shelter, nutrition, and basic medical access — residents who fall ill are taken to clinic or hospital by the team. Monthly running costs have been cited around RM 40,000, and the organisation has sought public donations to fund renovations and expansion. Given its welfare-home character, Sincere Care Home operates differently from fee-based nursing homes — families considering placement here should understand it as a charitable residential setting rather than a clinical nursing facility.",

      "Because Sincere Care Home is a registered welfare organisation rather than a private nursing operator, families should call +60 3-8734 8242 or speak with the management team to understand current capacity, the resident profile, and what care provision is available in-house versus what relies on outside clinics. Questions worth raising include the nursing qualification of staff on duty, how medical emergencies are managed, what daily structure residents follow, and whether the home is registered with JKM. For families exploring placement for a low-income or socially vulnerable elder, this home's mission-driven model and long community track record make it a meaningful option — and a call or visit will clarify whether it is the right fit.
address: Lot 18367, Jalan Kelapa, Bandar Kajang, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Mintygreen Senior Care Center Cheras (Sungai Long)
care_types: Retirement home, Medical Center, Nursing home
editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Senior Care Center at Sungai Long is the founding headquarters of the Mintygreen Healthcare Group, occupying six connected shophouse units at Landmark Residences 2, Jalan Sungai Long, Kajang, Selangor. The group was established in 2017 starting from a bungalow in Desa Permai and has since expanded to multiple branches across Kajang, Cheras, PJ, Kepong, and Penang, positioning the Sungai Long site as the flagship. It holds a 4.7-star rating from 13 reviews on Google. The facility is classified on Google Maps as a retirement home, medical centre, and nursing home — reflecting its broader scope beyond basic residential care.

Mintygreen markets itself as the first nursing home chain in Malaysia to formally integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) into its care model, offering acupuncture and Tui Na massage alongside conventional physiotherapy. The website indicates care ranges from semi-dependent to fully bed-ridden patients, with clinical services including NG tube feeding, stoma care, urine catheter management, insulin injections, bedsore prevention, and post-stroke and post-cancer care. Visiting doctors attend bi-weekly (GP basis). Day care and overnight care options are available alongside long-term residency. The facility is 10–15 minutes from Columbia Asia Cheras, UKM Medical Centre, Kajang Hospital, and KPJ Kajang Hospital — useful proximity for specialist follow-up.

Bed count and pricing are not published on the website, so families will need to enquire directly for current availability and fee schedules. The bi-weekly GP visit frequency is worth flagging — for residents with complex medical needs, ask whether specialist review can be arranged and how acute deteriorations are managed between visits. The TCM integration may be a positive draw for Chinese-speaking families, but confirm which therapies are included in the base fee versus charged additionally.
