Contact is through the Sungai Long person-in-charge (Ms Ru Jun, +60 18-987 3862) or the 24/7 customer service line (+60 11-5587 9788).
address: B-1-5, Landmark Residence 2 Landmark Residence, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Noble Care Retirement Resort
editorial_summary:
Noble Care Retirement Resort occupies units SCS G1–G6 within Country Heights Villas in Kajang — a gated township that lends the property an upscale residential character quite different from a typical nursing home. Noble Care is a multi-site operator with branches in Cheras, Ampang, Johor Bahru, and USJ, giving it more than two decades of cumulative experience across the group. The Country Heights location is marketed as a resort-style retirement option with a treehouse, gym, zen gardens, rooftop terrace, spa, sauna, Yoga, and Tai Chi, which positions it firmly at the premium leisure-retirement end of the spectrum rather than as a high-dependency medical facility.

That said, Noble Care's service sheet does include clinical capabilities: palliative care, dementia care and treatment, respite care, stroke and cancer care, bedsore dressing, Foley catheter management, nasogastric tube changes, colostomy bag changes, tracheostomy care, and physiotherapy. This suggests the Country Heights site can manage nursing-dependent residents beyond purely ambulant retirees, though the resort framing suggests this is not the primary market. The facility is within roughly 20 minutes of Serdang Hospital, KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital, and Hospital Kajang.

No public pricing was found — expect a call-to-enquire model typical of premium operators. With only two Google reviews (both 5-star), independent assessment is very limited. Families should clarify whether the nursing and medical capabilities are fully available on-site at Country Heights specifically, or whether those services are primarily delivered at other Noble Care branches. Also ask about nurse-to-resident ratios overnight, given the resort emphasis on lifestyle rather than clinical depth.
address: SCS G1 - G6 Country Heights Villas, Country Heights, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: EHA Bayview Eldercare (Cheras / Sungai Long / Kajang) #1 Nursing Home KL
care_types: Nursing home, Adult day care center, Assisted living facility
editorial_summary:
EHA Bayview Eldercare is the Klang Valley branch of EHA Eldercare Group, sited in Bandar Sungai Long on the Cheras–Kajang fringe — a light-industrial enclave on the eastern edge of greater Kuala Lumpur. The branch's listing title and marketing emphasise its catchment across Cheras, Sungai Long, and Kajang. With a 5.0-star rating across 22 Google reviews, the early signal is favourable, though the review base remains modest. The parent group, EHA Eldercare (1478860-U), evolved from the Justlife ElderCare Home brand founded in 2009 and now runs eight residential locations across Malaysia with a combined 100-plus caregivers and 500-plus residents — making it one of the more established private eldercare chains across Johor and the Klang Valley.

The group's website lists eight published service categories that apply across branches: medical support, nutrition and diet, rehabilitation by physiotherapy, social activities, caregiver training and support, skilled nurses, special nursing procedures, and a traditional Chinese medicine approach (acupuncture and herbal). Pricing is published on EHA's website (ehaeldercare.com.my/our-packages/) and is quoted as a starting point — actual fees vary with the resident's care needs. For the Johor Bahru and Klang Valley branches (which include this one), assisted senior living starts at RM 3,200/month for 24-hour residential care across able-bodied, semi-able-bodied, and bed-bound residents; respite stays of one to six months start at RM 120/day; and day care, available 11 hours a day across five days a week with flexible timing, starts at RM 1,500/month. The Kluang branch is priced lower (assisted from RM 2,300/month, respite from RM 80/day, day care from RM 1,300/month) — useful context if the family can travel and cost is a major factor. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

The Sungai Long location gives Bayview reasonable access to private hospitals on the Cheras–Kajang corridor (KPJ Kajang, Columbia Asia Cheras, Sunway Velocity), although the immediate neighbourhood is more industrial-fringe than residential. Practical viewing questions: how many caregivers are on each shift, whether RN cover is on-site overnight, what acute-escalation pathways the home typically uses (which hospital, how transfer is arranged), and what clinical procedures are handled in-house versus referred out. Confirm the "starting from" rate matches the actual care plan since add-ons can shift the monthly figure.
address: 7, Persiaran Sungai Long 5, Bandar Sungai Long, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Noble Care Retirement Home
editorial_summary:
Noble Care Retirement Home in Kampung Sungai Sekamat, Kajang sits at Lot 4288, Jalan Cheras BT 12½, roughly 20 minutes by road from Serdang Hospital, KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital, and Kajang Hospital — a useful proximity for residents with ongoing medical appointments. Noble Care is a multi-branch operator with facilities across the Klang Valley and beyond (Klang, Cheras, Ampang, Sungai Petani), positioning itself at the premium end of the Kajang nursing home market. The Kajang branch is described as offering a 'serene sanctuary' with an emphasis on personalised care alongside lifestyle amenities.

Nursing capabilities at Noble Care Kajang cover a broad clinical range: palliative care, respite care, dementia care, stroke and cancer care, post-operative care, catheter and colostomy management, nasogastric tube feeding, tracheostomy care, bedsore dressing, and physiotherapy. The facility claims 24/7 nursing supervision by qualified nurses and experienced caregivers. Lifestyle amenities listed include a treehouse, gym, zen garden, rooftop terrace, spa, sauna, massage services, and yoga and Tai Chi classes — signalling a resort-style positioning that goes well beyond typical nursing home offerings.

Pricing and bed count are not published on their website; contact them at +60 16-278 6993 or +60 16-778 6795 to request a consultation. The premium positioning implies rates at the higher end of the Kajang market, so budget-conscious families should clarify costs upfront. Ask specifically about the staffing ratio on night shifts, whether the dementia unit is a dedicated locked ward or integrated with general residents, and what the discharge or escalation pathway is if a resident's medical needs exceed what the facility can provide.
address: Jalan Sungai Sekamat, Kampung Sungai Sekamat, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Kajang Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Kajang Nursing Home appears at 142 Jalan Bukit in Kajang town proper — a central Kajang address that gives it reasonable proximity to Hospital Kajang (under 3 km) and KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital. A landline number (+60 3-6737 3571) is the listed contact. The home does not maintain a published website or active social media page, so most service detail is best gathered on a call or visit. The Google Maps entry carries three reviews.

The name 'Kajang Nursing Home' implies a nursing-level rather than purely social-care operation. Staffing model, capacity, clinical capability, and operating history are best confirmed directly with the home. The central Kajang Jaya township location in a residential area is typical of smaller, community-embedded homes that may serve long-term Kajang residents who prefer staying local.

Families considering this home should call ahead to establish whether it is currently accepting residents, confirm licence status with JKM Selangor, ask about nursing coverage overnight, and visit in person before committing. Useful questions when calling: the JKM licence number, registered-nurse coverage on each shift, doctor-visit frequency, and current monthly fees. A short visit will give a clearer picture of day-to-day conditions than the listing alone.
address: 142, Jalan Bukit, Taman Kajang Jaya, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Geriacare
editorial_summary:
GERIACARE is located at Jalan Desa Impian 6, Taman Desa Impian, 43000 Kajang, Selangor — a semi-suburban residential township in the Kajang corridor, approximately 30 km southeast of Kuala Lumpur. The facility's Facebook presence is titled 'Geriacare Nursing & Rehabilitation', signalling an active rehabilitation orientation alongside standard nursing home care. It carries a Google rating of 4.9 from 60 reviews — the joint-highest in this batch — and its website positions it as doctor-overseen, with full-time registered nurses on-site and a care model the company describes as family-like in character.

The website lists a broad clinical scope: stroke rehabilitation with physiotherapy and speech therapy, dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative and hospice care with pain management, post-hospitalisation recovery, and assisted daily living. Physical infrastructure includes an on-site physiotherapy gym, outdoor gardens with walking paths, a dining room with customised meal plans, a prayer/meditation room, an activity and entertainment lounge, and 24-hour CCTV security. Daily programming covers physical exercises, art and craft therapy, gardening, music therapy, and social events. Contact details include two phone numbers (+60 16-270 2844 and +60 14-905 2844), suggesting a reasonable level of accessibility. Visiting hours are listed as 11 am–8 pm daily.

Pricing is not publicly disclosed. Kajang's position relative to major hospitals is worth noting — Hospital Kajang (the nearest government hospital) is a reasonable distance away but not a tertiary centre; families with residents who might need subspecialty intervention should consider this. Confirm whether the attending doctors are resident or visiting, what frequency physiotherapy is delivered at, and whether the dementia and palliative care programmes are delivered in integrated or dedicated areas of the facility. The prayer/meditation room suggests Muslim-friendly but not necessarily Muslim-exclusive operations — worth clarifying if religious alignment matters to your family.
address: 23, Jalan Desa Impian 6, Taman Desa Impian, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Genesis Life Care Centre Kajang
editorial_summary:
Genesis Life Care Kajang is the flagship centre of one of Malaysia's largest private long-term care operators, located at 41 Jalan Ria 1 in Kawasan Perindustrian Ria — an industrial park setting off the Cheras-Kajang Highway that trades scenic surroundings for straightforward vehicle access from Cheras, Bangi, and Kajang town. The centre opened in 2021 and holds JKM (Jabatan Kebajikan Masyarakat) registration and AgeCope certification, making it one of the more formally credentialled facilities in the Kajang corridor. With 120+ beds, it is substantially larger than the typical independent nursing home in the area.

The care menu is genuinely broad: 24/7 nursing, weekly on-site GP consultations, a psychologist-led cognitive stimulation programme for dementia residents, physiotherapy and occupational therapy for stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, post-operative recovery, and a senior day care option. Dementia care uses individualised plans tied to the resident's history and stage, with art therapy, music sessions, and gentle exercise forming the daily activity spine. An in-house kitchen operates under dietician supervision. The Google rating stands at 4.9 from 37 reviews, reflecting strong family satisfaction in the early years of operation.

Pricing is not published and described only as contact-based depending on dependency level and room type; families should request a written breakdown. The 120-bed scale means the centre can feel busier than smaller homes — ask about actual staff-to-resident ratios on night shift and weekends. No overnight specialist cover is described; the weekly doctor visit model is standard for the sector but worth confirming in writing if your family member has complex medical needs.
address: 41, Jalan Ria 1, Kawasan Perindustrian Ria, 43500 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Mintygreen Nursing Home (Sungai Long & Kajang ElderCare Center)
care_types: Nursing home, Nursing agency, Physical therapist
editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Nursing Home's Sungai Long branch — branded as the Kajang ElderCare Center and operating as the group's original headquarters — is located at Landmark Residences 2, Jalan Sungai Long, Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang. The Mintygreen Healthcare Group was founded in 2017, starting in a bungalow in Desa Permai before expanding to a six-unit shoplot in Sungai Long and subsequently growing to seven branches across KL, Selangor, and Penang. The group describes itself as the first senior care chain in Malaysia to integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) as a core service.

Care ranges from semi-dependent to fully bedridden residents, with short-term and long-term placement options and day care available. Clinical services include bi-weekly visiting GP consultations, NG tube feeding and changing, stoma and catheter care, insulin administration, wound and pressure ulcer management, and oxygen therapy. TCM services — acupuncture and Tui Na — are offered as add-ons. The Sungai Long branch is within 10–15 minutes of several hospitals including Columbia Asia Cheras, UKM Medical Centre, and KPJ Kajang. Long-term stay pricing starts from RM 4,000 per month; day care is listed at RM 220 per day.

The TCM integration is uncommon in the Malaysian nursing home sector and may suit families who prefer a complementary approach alongside conventional nursing. The Sungai Long branch draws from the Kajang and Cheras catchment areas. Ask about current bed availability (the shoplot format limits total capacity), JKM registration, and whether the visiting doctor schedule can be adjusted for residents with higher medical needs.
address: B-1-03, Landmark Residences, 2, Jalan Sungai Long, Bandar Sungai Long, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Good Family Home Care
editorial_summary:
Good Family Home Care is a small residential care facility at 799, Lorong Permai 14, Kampung Tasek Permai, 68000 Ampang, Selangor — a semi-rural neighbourhood on the eastern fringe of Kuala Lumpur near the Ampang border. Nine photos are visible on Google Maps, providing a visual sense of the property exterior.

No phone number, website or published care details are currently available online. The facility appears to operate without a significant digital presence, meaning direct in-person contact is the most reliable way to assess suitability.

No reviews have been recorded on Google Maps at this time. Families considering this facility should visit in person and confirm care types offered, JKM licence status, current bed availability and monthly fees before making any decision.
address: 799, Lorong Permai 14, Kampung Tasek Permai, 68000 Ampang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Sree Sai Elder Home Care Mental Health & Old Age Residential Home
editorial_summary:
Sree Sai Elder Home Care is a residential care facility at No. 204B, off Jalan Ampang, Kuala Ampang, Kuala Lumpur — a compact property on a quiet side street in the Ampang residential belt. The full registered name indicates care coverage that extends to both elderly residential needs and mental health support, making it one of the few KL facilities that explicitly states this dual scope. Phone: +60 12-297 0597.

No website is available; care details are drawn from Google Maps data. Three photos show the facility exterior. The mental health component is worth clarifying on your first call to confirm whether the home accepts residents with behavioural or psychiatric conditions alongside standard physical care.

Six reviews average 5.0 stars. Reviewers describe a well-organised, clean home with attentive staff. Care type specifics, capacity, pricing and JKM licence number should all be confirmed directly during a site visit.
address: No. 204 B, Off, Jln Ampang, Kuala Ampang, 55000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Woodrose Senior Residences
longitude: Woodrose Senior Residences is an assisted living centre located in Taman Sri Ukay, Selangor. It holds a 4.4/5 rating on Google Maps from 8 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Woodrose Senior Residences at Taman Sri Ukay, Lorong Hillview 2D, Ampang, is the third and newest facility in the Woodrose network, described on the brand's website as the group's most premium offering. Woodrose Senior Residences Sdn Bhd was formed from the merger of Zara Aisyah Care Centre (ZACC) and MLL Sdn Bhd, and operates two further homes in Seksyen 11, Shah Alam alongside this Ampang location. The Taman Sri Ukay site is approximately 20 minutes from central Kuala Lumpur by car, set in a quieter hill-residential neighbourhood with elegant furnishings noted in the facility's own descriptions.

Woodrose is distinguished by its Shariah-compliant operating framework, described as a rarity in the Malaysian elderly care landscape. A full-time Ustaz oversees Islamic standards across food sourcing, cleanliness, daily activities, and spiritual programming — making this a particularly coherent choice for Muslim-Malay families who want a religiously aligned environment for a parent or grandparent. Care programmes span residential, nursing, memory care (dementia and Alzheimer's), palliative, respite, and day care. Published pricing for the Shah Alam homes starts at RM 3,800 per month for independent living and RM 4,500 per month for assisted living — though rates for the Ampang site should be confirmed separately as a newer and premium-positioned property.

Because specific bed counts and room-type details for the Taman Sri Ukay home are not published, families should request a site visit to assess room size, outdoor access, and the staffing model. Ask about nurse-to-resident ratios at night, how medical emergencies are handled, and what clinical procedures can be managed in-house versus what requires hospital transfer. The Shariah-compliant positioning means this home will attract a specific clientele — demand may result in a waiting list, so enquire early if placement timing is important.

title: Canaan Home
editorial_summary:
Canaan Home is listed as a retirement home in Kampung Indah Permai — a residential pocket in the broader Ampang corridor of Selangor. The name 'Canaan' (迦南) carries a Christian connotation, suggesting the home may serve, or have been founded by, a Christian community in the area. The home is contactable on +60 14-682 8622.

The home keeps a low online profile, with a Facebook page that is not publicly browsable and no published website, so most service detail is best gathered on a call. The 'retirement home' classification typically implies a focus on independent or low-dependency seniors rather than high-acuity nursing cases, but this is best confirmed directly. Kampung Indah Permai is a neighbourhood-scale residential setting, consistent with smaller community homes in the area.

Families considering Canaan Home should call ahead to establish current admission criteria, religious or cultural orientation, and what dependency levels the home accepts. Useful questions: bed count, registered-nurse availability, daily routines and activities, dietary arrangements, monthly fees, and the JKM licence status. A short visit will give the clearest sense of the home's character and whether it suits a particular resident.
address: 1307, Lorong Indah 8/2, Kampung Indah Permai, 68000 Ampang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Love and Care Centre Kajang
editorial_summary:
Love and Care Centre Kajang is at No. 1, Jalan S8, Taman Sakap, 43000 Kajang — a quiet residential area in the southern Klang Valley, roughly 30 km from KL city centre. The facility is a ministry of social concern operated under the Lutheran Church of Malaysia and was established in 2003 (female home) with a male home added in 2006. It is registered with the Selangor Social Welfare Department. The primary character of this centre is a small faith-based welfare home rather than a medically-oriented nursing facility.

The facility operates two separate homes — one for female residents, one for male — with a combined stated capacity of approximately 15 residents (10 female, 5 male) at the time of last reporting, with plans to expand the female wing by at least six additional places. The resident profile skews toward the financially vulnerable elderly and those without family support, including OKU individuals in need of care. The staffing model consists of a supervisor, two caregivers, a cook-caregiver, and a driver-caregiver, with community volunteers providing regular activities and entertainment. Transportation for medical appointments is available.

As a small welfare home with a limited staff-to-resident ratio and no indication of registered nursing staff on-site, Love and Care Kajang is best suited for ambulant or lightly-dependent elderly who primarily need shelter, meals, companionship, and basic supervision rather than clinical nursing care. Families with residents who have complex medical needs — tube feeding, wound care, catheter management, or advanced dementia — should ask explicitly whether the facility can manage those requirements. Fees are likely subsidised given the charity model; contact +60 3-8733 8075 to discuss admission criteria and current availability.
address: No.1, Jalan S8, Taman Sakap, 43000 Kajang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Blissful Family Old Folks Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Blissful Family Old Folks Nursing Home is listed in Taman Bukit Indah — a residential township in the southern Klang Valley. The Google listing categorises it under aged care. The home is contactable on +60 16-323 7258.

The home keeps a low online profile and does not maintain a published website or active social media page, so most service detail is best gathered directly. Bed count, staffing model, the scope of medical care, and JKM registration are best confirmed on a call. The Taman Bukit Indah area is a mixed residential neighbourhood that supports several smaller community-scale aged-care providers.

Families considering this home should call ahead to establish current operating status, what care types are offered, and the scale of the operation. Useful questions: bed count, registered-nurse cover overnight, doctor-visit frequency, dependency levels accepted, monthly fees, and the JKM licence number. Smaller community homes are often best understood through a short visit, where the staff-to-resident dynamic and physical environment are visible in a way listings cannot convey.
address: 13, Jalan Rasmi 6, Taman Bukit Indah, 68000 Ampang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Gam Yam Senior Home in Ampang, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
care_types: Retirement home, Assisted living facility, Home health care service
editorial_summary:
Gam Yam Senior Home operates from 327, Jalan 16, Kampung Baru Ampang — a long-established Chinese residential neighbourhood in Ampang that sits on the Selangor-KL boundary. The facility's Google listing categorises it as a retirement home, assisted living facility, and home health care service, suggesting it spans both residential and outreach care. A directory listing places physiotherapy and residential care for the elderly and disabled among its stated activities. The address is also associated with an entity listed as AIM Healthcare at the same premises — worth clarifying with the home which licence and which operator currently runs the facility.

The Google rating sits at 3.3 from 11 reviews, which is a smaller and more mixed reviewer base than some neighbouring Ampang facilities. No website or Facebook presence was found during research, so most service detail is best gathered on a call. The Kampung Baru Ampang setting is a densely built residential area with limited outdoor space, though Hospital Ampang is within a few kilometres for medical escalation.

Families considering this home will want to ask for the Care Centre Act licence number, clarify the relationship with AIM Healthcare at the same address, confirm which medical conditions are managed in-house versus referred out, and ask the home for current family references. A visit during a mealtime or activity period will give a clearer picture of day-to-day conditions than a formal tour, and is the most reliable way to assess fit.
address: 327B, Jalan 16, Kampung Baru Ampang, 68000 Ampang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Apple Nursery Home
editorial_summary:
Apple Nursery Home is located at 241, Jalan 8, Kampung Baru Ampang, 68000 Ampang — a long-established residential area straddling the Selangor–Ampang Jaya corridor, within reasonable reach of public hospitals serving the Ampang district. Its name includes 'nursery', which raises a question about whether it is primarily an elderly nursing home or has a dual children-and-elderly function; the Google listing categorises it as a nursing home and gives a phone of 03-4292 8228.

No website, Facebook page, or any substantive third-party listings were found for this facility beyond basic map directory entries. With only four Google reviews at a 4.5 rating, there is very little user feedback to assess the quality of care, staffing ratios, or services on offer. Bed count, pricing, language of care, religious affiliation, and JKM registration status are all unknown.

Before visiting, families should call 03-4292 8228 to clarify whether this is a full nursing home or a smaller residential care arrangement, and ask about current vacancies, daily care routine, nursing staff availability, and whether the facility can handle common dependency needs such as incontinence care, post-stroke rehabilitation, or tube feeding.
address: 241, Jalan 8, Kampung Baru Ampang, 68000 Ampang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Lekshmy Illam Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Lekshmy Illam Nursing Home is located at No. 89, Jalan Bersatu 4, Taman Bersatu, Rawang, Selangor, a township roughly 25 km north of Kuala Lumpur along the Rawang corridor. The name 'Lekshmy Illam' is Tamil — 'illam' meaning home. The facility's admissions policy is not publicly stated. The facility carries a 4.6 Google rating across 21 reviews.

The care type is listed as nursing home, and the facility's stated motto is 'We care our patient like our family', indicating a small, family-style operation rather than a large institutional setup. A Facebook page exists under the name Pusat Jagaan Lekshmy Illam, but detailed service information is not published online. Based on the location in a Rawang residential area and the family-run character of the operation, this is likely a small-capacity home providing basic nursing and residential care for elderly and bed-bound residents.

As very little clinical or operational detail is publicly available, families considering this home should visit in person and ask specifically about: the number of beds and current occupancy, whether a registered nurse is on duty around the clock, what happens when a resident requires hospitalisation, and whether the home can manage complex needs such as dementia or tube feeding. Confirm the primary language of caregiving when you call +60 16-448 2421.
address: No 89, Jalan Bersatu 4, Taman Bersatu, 48000 Rawang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Love and Care Village
editorial_summary:
Love and Care Village is a nursing home located in Taman Ampang Utama, a residential estate in Ampang on the Selangor–KL border. The area sits in a well-connected part of greater Ampang, close to Jalan Ampang and within reach of Ampang Puteri Specialist Hospital and Hospital Ampang. The facility is listed on Google Maps as a nursing home with a 5-star rating from seven reviews — a perfect score that, on a small sample, reflects early positive feedback but should be read cautiously rather than as a definitive quality signal.

Beyond the Maps listing, Love and Care Village has no publicly accessible website or Facebook page that surfaced in research. No capacity figures, care programme details, pricing, or staffing information have been independently published. The name suggests a residential, village-style model oriented toward warm community living rather than a purely clinical nursing approach, though this cannot be confirmed from available sources. Contact is through a mobile number (+60 12-353 9035), which is typical of smaller, owner-operated homes.

Families should call to determine whether the facility caters to ambulant elderly, nursing-dependent residents, or both; what medical cover (if any) is on site or on-call; and whether a JKM licence is held. The Ampang Utama location, while residential and relatively quiet, may also be of interest to families based in Ampang, Wangsa Maju, or Keramat who want proximity to KL without the congestion of the city centre. Given the thin public footprint, an in-person visit before committing is strongly advisable.
address: 298, Jalan 3, Taman Ampang Utama, 68000 Ampang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Ampang Old Folks Home
editorial_summary:
Ampang Old Folks Home is one of the older recorded care facilities in Kampung Baru Ampang, 68000 Ampang, Selangor — an address that has appeared in social welfare directories going back at least to the mid-2000s. The Wikimapia record and legacy directory listings confirm its physical presence in the area, and the Google listing gives a phone of 03-4291 8978 with a 4.5 rating from four reviews and a retirement home classification.

As with many long-standing community welfare homes, public documentation is sparse. No website or social media presence could be located. There is no verified data on bed count, staffing, care scope, pricing, language of operations, or JKM licensing status. The retirement home classification on Google suggests it may cater to more mobile or semi-independent residents rather than high-dependency nursing cases, but this is not confirmed.

Families should call to verify the home is still actively accepting residents — legacy listings sometimes persist after facilities have closed or significantly downsized. Ask about current capacity, the level of nursing care available for any medical needs your family member has, whether meals and laundry are included, and what the monthly fee structure looks like. A site visit is strongly recommended before committing.
address: Lot 699, 701, Jalan Merdeka, Kampung Baru Ampang, 68000 Ampang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Convee Elderly Care Center
care_types: Nursing home, Adult day care center, Home health care service
editorial_summary:
Convee Elderly Care Center is located at Jalan Bunga Tanjung 22, Taman Seraya, Ampang — a quiet, established residential neighbourhood in the Ampang-Kuala Lumpur fringe that the Google Maps listing attributes to Selangor. The centre holds a 4.8 rating from 37 reviews, an unusually high score that suggests consistently positive feedback from families using the service. Its listed care types span three distinct service lines: nursing home residential care, adult day care, and home health care — making it one of the few smaller operators in the area advertising all three under one roof.

The facility's website describes care packages ranging from active-senior day care through to 24-hour residential care for bedridden residents, with packages described as personalised to individual needs. Regular health checks by doctors and licensed medical practitioners are listed as part of the care programme. The centre is accessible by bus (route 23 is mentioned) and has external parking, with ramp access indicated — practical details that matter for families bringing in residents who use wheelchairs.

Detailed service specifications — bed count, pricing, staffing composition, JKM licence number — are not published publicly. The home health care offering is particularly worth probing: ask whether nurses are dispatched for wound care, IV administration, or purely companion-level assistance, as the scope varies widely between operators. Families who want day care only should clarify start and end times and whether transport is provided. The strong Google rating is encouraging but independent verification through a site visit remains the essential step before placing a family member.
address: 2 & 4, Jalan Bunga Tanjung 22, Taman Seraya, 68000 Ampang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Glory Living Care Home
care_types: Aged care, Assisted living facility, Retirement home
editorial_summary:
Glory Living Care Home is positioned in the Taman Sri Ukay / Taman Hillview area of Ampang — a hillside residential enclave off Jalan Ulu Kelang, about 15 minutes from the Ampang town centre. The facility describes itself as a retirement home and assisted living centre focused on seniors who value independence, friendship, and a healthy lifestyle, with an emphasis on a clean, hygienic, and home-like environment suited to those who need support but wish to remain socially engaged. Contact is via a mobile number (+60 10-962 8683), and the facility has a Facebook page but no independent website with detailed service listings.

Based on its Google listing category of aged care, assisted living, and retirement home, the facility appears to cater to relatively mobile or semi-independent residents rather than high-dependency nursing cases. The Sri Ukay hillside setting offers a quieter, greener environment compared to urban care homes, which some families find preferable for residents with mild cognitive decline or those in earlier stages of frailty. The 4.8 Google rating across 11 reviews is positive, though the small sample limits confidence. No bed count, pricing, or staffing details are publicly available.

With an online footprint this limited, families should visit in person before committing. Key questions: Is registered nursing staff present around the clock, or is this primarily caregiver-staffed? What conditions can the home manage — incontinence, tube feeding, wound care — versus what would trigger a transfer? The Ampang area has reasonable hospital access via Hospital Ampang and several private facilities along the Ampang-KL corridor, but confirm the home's emergency protocol and hospital relationships before placement.
address: 22, Lor Hillview 2D, Taman Hillview, 68000 Ampang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Mona Elder Care | Nursing Home in Jalan Sri Kemuning Lembah Jaya Selatan Ampang
editorial_summary:
Mona Elder Care's Ampang branch is situated at 2, Jalan Sri Kemuning, Lembah Jaya Selatan, 68000 Ampang — a residential pocket just south of the main Ampang town corridor, with reasonable access to Hospital Ampang and the broader Ampang-Cheras clinical network. This is one of at least four Mona Elder Care locations in the Klang Valley, sharing a common brand, clinical identity, and service menu with the Pandan Perdana branch. The group's 24/7 model and its emphasis on medically complex cases — stroke, coma, tube feeding, post-operative recovery, and tracheostomy care — position this as a nursing home that can take on cases hospitals discharge earlier than families might expect.

The services page of monaeldercare.com lists nine primary care types offered across the network: respite care, palliative care, home nursing, stroke and coma care, senior citizens care, handicapped care, physiotherapy, dementia care, and day care. Clinical procedures documented include bedsore dressing, NG tube changes, colostomy bag management, Foley catheter care, tracheostomy care, post-operative care, and cerebral palsy support. This is a more clinically detailed public disclosure than most similarly-sized Selangor nursing homes provide, which is useful for families assessing whether a specific care need can be met. The facility describes a structured 24-hour shift system across morning, afternoon, evening, and night shifts.

With a 4.7-star rating from 15 Google reviewers, this Ampang branch performs slightly better on volume than the Pandan Perdana location's identical 17-review base. As with all Mona Elder Care sites, no pricing, bed count, or staff ratio figures are publicly available — request these by contacting +60 16-616 4535. Language coverage by caregivers is not disclosed; given the Ampang location's mixed Chinese, Malay, and Indian demographic profile, ask directly about Mandarin, Cantonese, and Tamil capability. Confirm the JKM licence number and ask whether the Ampang site has its own registered licence or operates under a group licence.
address: 2, JALAN SRI KEMUNING LEMBAH JAYA SELATAN Ampang, Kampung Lembah Jaya Selatan, 68000 Kuala Lumpur, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Noble Care Malaysia Ampang
editorial_summary:
Noble Care Malaysia Ampang is one of the network's Klang Valley branches, situated at 537 Jalan 5, Taman Ampang Utama, 68000 Ampang, Selangor. Ampang Utama is a long-established residential neighbourhood with good access to hospitals — Ampang Hospital is approximately 5 kilometres away, and Gleneagles Hospital Kuala Lumpur is reachable within 15 minutes — making this a relatively well-serviced location for a nursing home. The Noble Care brand operates more than 15 branches nationally and has over 16 years in the sector, positioning itself as a professional, multi-location aged care provider.

The Ampang branch offers the group's standard clinical menu: 24-hour nursing supervision, dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative and respite support, physiotherapy, and skilled nursing procedures including Foley's catheter care, tracheostomy management, nasogastric tube care, colostomy bag changing, bedsore dressing, and post-operative care. The website also lists capability for cerebral palsy care and care for individuals with stroke and cancer diagnoses. The facility describes itself as providing a home-like environment with nutritious meals and social activities for residents.

Bed count and room-type details are not published for this branch, and no pricing is listed — families should contact Noble Care directly at +60 16-278 6993, noting that this number is shared with the Rawang HQ. Ask to speak specifically with the Ampang team to confirm current availability, local pricing, and staffing levels. As with all chain operators, quality can vary between branches, so a site visit to the Ampang location specifically is worthwhile rather than drawing conclusions from the network's overall reputation. Confirm whether dementia residents have a dedicated unit or are integrated into the general ward.
address: 537, Jalan 5, Taman Ampang Utama, 68000 Ampang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Aliz Nursing Care
care_types: Aged care, Career guidance service, Home health care service
editorial_summary:
Aliz Nursing Care is based at 12A Jalan Badminton 13/29, Tadisma Business Park, Seksyen 13, Shah Alam — a commercial shopoffice park in central Shah Alam. Unlike most facilities on this guide, Aliz presents primarily as a home-based nursing care provider rather than a residential nursing home: the website and public listings emphasise one-to-one nursing care delivered at the patient's own home, with the commercial address serving as the operational base. Google Maps categorises the service as aged care, career guidance, and home health care, consistent with a hybrid model that may include some centre-based care alongside outreach.

Aliz's stated service range covers aged and elderly care, post-surgery recovery, disability care, injury care, personal care assistance, and 24-hour in-home and hospital nursing. The company indicates it employs local registered nurses trained to assist with activities of daily living, framing one-to-one home-based care as superior to institutional placement for both patient outcomes and family peace of mind. The emphasis on home care means Aliz is likely a good fit for families managing a parent who wishes to remain at home but requires professional nursing input beyond what a domestic helper can provide.

Pricing is not published. Families should call to understand whether Aliz offers any centre-based residential placements or exclusively provides nursing staff for home deployment. Confirm the qualifications and registration of nurses supplied, the minimum engagement duration, and how continuity of care is managed if the assigned nurse is unavailable. For families in Shah Alam, Subang Jaya, or nearby Petaling Jaya, Aliz represents a potentially convenient option for post-hospitalisation home nursing or ongoing personal care management.
address: 12A, Section 13, Jalan Badminton 13/29, Tadisma Business Park, 40100 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Golden Years Senior Residence Kota Kemuning Shah Alam
editorial_summary:
Golden Years Senior Residence in Kota Kemuning is a medically oriented nursing home set within a quiet residential pocket of Shah Alam at 4, Jalan Anggerik Aranda 31/170F — a biophilic environment with gardens designed to reduce the institutional feel that families often dread. The facility was founded by healthcare professionals: the clinical leadership team includes a GP, an Internal Medicine Specialist with over 15 years of experience, a Registered Nurse Matron with 35-plus years in clinical care, and an in-house physiotherapist. A second location in Bandar Kinrara, Puchong, and a home nursing service covering surrounding areas extend the group's reach.

Services at Kota Kemuning are structured around four care modes: long-term residential care for chronic and progressive conditions, short-term post-hospital recovery, adult day care (8:30am–6pm weekdays, RM2,000–2,300/month), and home nursing visits within the local area at RM30/hour. Monthly residential fees are tiered by dependency: RM2,950 for low, RM3,450 for medium, and RM3,950 for high, with short-term stays priced at RM220–300/day. Occupational therapy is available at RM200/session, physiotherapy at RM100/session for non-package residents. A telemedical doctor standby service is listed for acute medical needs, and residents receive daily vital sign monitoring and annual health screenings.

A RM3,500 refundable deposit is required on admission for residential care (RM2,000 for day care). Families should ask about current bed availability — the Kota Kemuning facility appears to be a multi-unit operation — and clarify what "standby telemedical doctor" means in practice versus an on-site physician. Confirm whether the Internal Medicine Specialist provides regular in-person consultations and at what frequency. The Google rating (4.7 stars, 24 reviews) and the published pricing transparency make this one of the more accountable small operators in Shah Alam.
address: 4, Jalan Anggerik Aranda 31/170f, Kota Kemuning, 40460 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Care Concierge | Care Luxe Ampang
care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living facility, Occupational therapist
editorial_summary:
Care Luxe is the premium assisted living branch of Care Concierge, Malaysia's largest specialist senior care group. Occupying Level 3 of Oakwood Hotel & Residence at 222 Jalan Ampang, in the Kampung Datuk Keramat corridor near the Ampang–Kuala Lumpur boundary, it is the most hotel-forward of Care Concierge's several Klang Valley residences. The concept deliberately merges clinical nursing with hotel-grade hospitality — chef-prepared meals, concierge assistance, and housekeeping sit alongside 24-hour care support — targeting seniors who want comfort and privacy without compromising on medical oversight. Primary care covers independent retirement living, post-surgery recovery, stroke rehabilitation, diabetes management, cancer support, dementia, Parkinson's, and palliative care.

The website lists five care packages ranging from RM10,000 per month for Independent Living to RM18,000 for Personal 1-to-1 Care, with a short-stay or respite option at RM500 per night. Each resident receives a personalised care plan, weekly doctor visits, daily health monitoring, physiotherapy, and pharmacy medication reviews. Care Concierge holds B Corporation certification and the group operates a proprietary mobile app used by nursing staff for real-time vital monitoring and care tracking. The broader group employs certified nurses, therapists, and doctors across all its residences, and the Ampang location benefits from the same centralised clinical governance as the rest of the network.

Care Luxe sits at the higher end of the private elder-care market in Malaysia, so the pricing warrants a direct conversation to understand exactly which clinical procedures are included at each tier versus billed separately. Because it operates within a hotel building, ask about how medical emergencies are handled on-site and what the transfer protocol to a nearby hospital looks like. Proximity to Gleneagles KL and DEMC Specialist Hospital Ampang is a practical plus. Visiting arrangements, room sizes, and whether the hotel's residential-tower neighbours affect the care environment are worth clarifying during a site visit.
address: Level 3, Oakwood Hotel & Residence Kuala Lumpur, 222, Jln Ampang, Kampung Datuk Keramat, 50450 Ampang, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Woodrose Senior Residences 2 Seksyen 11 (Male - Syariah Compliant)
editorial_summary:
Woodrose Senior Residences 2 is the male wing of Woodrose Healthcare's Seksyen 11 campus in Shah Alam, Selangor — a pair of adjacent facilities that together form what is likely the most established Shariah-compliant nursing home operation in the Klang Valley. Woodrose Senior Residences Sdn Bhd describes itself as "the most well established nursing home in Selangor" and emerged from a merger between Zara Aisyah Care Centre and MLL Sdn Bhd. Residence 2, on Jalan Gamelan, is specifically designated for male residents and operates under Shariah oversight developed with an in-house Ustaz who shaped the facility's SOPs for food, personal care, daily activities, and spiritual guidance.

The Shariah compliance framework at Woodrose is designed to address the genuine clinical and religious tension of having non-mahram caregivers attend to opposite-gender residents — a significant concern for observant Muslim families. By separating the homes by gender, Woodrose allows for same-gender caregiving, halal-certified food, Islamic daily routines, and religious programming including observances during Ramadan. Services across the Woodrose network include dementia care, Alzheimer's care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, physiotherapy, day care, and short-term respite. The campus features indoor and outdoor activity spaces, and rooms at Residence 2 are described as spacious with good access facilities.

Woodrose is not the cheapest option in Shah Alam and is clearly positioned at the mid-to-upper end of the market. Families should ask about specific capacity at Residence 2 versus Residence 1, the current Ustaz oversight structure, waiting times for admission, and pricing tiers by dependency level. It is worth confirming whether the facility holds a valid JKM licence and whether there is an on-call or visiting physician arrangement — details not published on the website.
address: 1, Jalan Gamelan 11/2e, Seksyen 11, 40100 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

Sayang Nursing Home Care lists its address as the second floor of Anggerik Mall, Seksyen 14, 40000 Shah Alam, Selangor — a commercial mall location rather than a residential care property. This suggests the business may operate as a home care placement or coordination service rather than a live-in residential nursing facility. Phone: +60 3-5519 7501.

No website, reviews or published care details are currently available. The nature of the operation — residential care or home-based care services — is not confirmed through publicly available information.

Families should contact the Shah Alam number directly to clarify whether the service dispatches caregivers to clients' homes, operates a residential facility at a separate address, or provides both. This will help determine whether it meets your specific care requirements.
address: Lot 2-01, 2nd Floor, Anggerik Mall Shah Alam, No. 5, Jalan 14/8, Seksyen 14, 40000, Shah Alam, Selangor, Seksyen 14, 40000 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Zara Aisyah Care Centre (ZACC)
editorial_summary:
ZARA AISYAH CARE CENTRE (ZACC) was founded in 2015 by Wan Salbiah Mior Yahya and is based at Jalan Gunung Tahan U11/10A, Bukit Bandaraya Shah Alam, Selangor — a residential area in the Seksyen U11 corridor of Shah Alam. It is one of Malaysia's more prominently known Islamic-oriented elderly care providers, positioning itself as a pioneer Shariah-compliant care specialist. The company reports growth from two staff caring for two residents at founding to over 300 employees and more than 500 patients served across its network, which now includes a second site branded ZACC MYTOWN.

ZACC offers 24/7 residential nursing care, dementia care, and assisted daily living in what it describes as a luxury environment. Spiritual care is a core differentiator: daily prayers, Quran recitation, and Islamic routines are structured into the daily schedule. Medical support, physiotherapy, and counselling are included in the care model. Room types cover single with attached toilet, twin with attached toilet, and triple configurations. Monthly fees are reported in the RM 3,001–5,000 range, with a comprehensive inclusions package covering 24-hour care, accommodation, housekeeping, laundry, catering, monthly medical review, and security. Visiting hours are structured and booking-based (9 am–2 pm and 6 pm–8 pm, daily including public holidays).

ZACC's strong Islamic identity means it is an excellent fit for Muslim families who want religious observance integrated into care — but families of non-Muslim residents should clarify how the religious programme is structured and whether it is optional or universal. Ask about the nurse-to-resident ratio, the qualifications of the physiotherapy and dementia care staff, and whether the RM 3,001–5,000 range reflects the U11 branch specifically or is a network-wide figure. Given the booking-only visiting policy, also confirm the escalation pathway for families who need to reach staff urgently outside visiting hours.
address: No 17, Jalan Gunung Tahan U11/10A Bukit Bandaraya Shah Alam Bukit Bandaraya, Seksyen U11, 40170 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Woodrose Senior Residences 3 Seksyen 13 (Female - Syariah compliant)
editorial_summary:
Woodrose Senior Residences is a growing Shah Alam-based operator that emerged from the merger of Zara Aisyah Care Centre (ZACC) and MLL Sdn Bhd, positioning itself as Selangor's leading Syariah-compliant elderly care brand. The Seksyen 13 branch — listed as Woodrose Senior Residences 3, reachable at +60 12-709 8102 — is specifically designated as a female-only, Syariah-compliant residence, making it one of the few nursing facilities in the Klang Valley explicitly designed for Muslim women who require religious and gender-sensitive care. The head office sits in Seksyen 9, Shah Alam, with other branches in Seksyen 11.

Woodrose's Syariah compliance framework is documented on their website and covers four pillars: same-gender medical and caregiving teams assigned to female residents; halal-certified food, beverages, and pharmaceuticals; prayer facilities with ablution assistance and tayammum provision for bedridden residents; and management operating on Islamic ethical principles. Across their network, the operator offers residential nursing care, dementia care, palliative care, rehabilitation, physiotherapy, respite care, and senior day care. These services are understood to apply across branches, though the specific clinical capacity of the Seksyen 13 location should be confirmed directly.

For families seeking a female Muslim environment, this is likely the most structured option in Shah Alam. The facility does not publish its bed count or pricing online, so call ahead to confirm availability, room configurations, and what level of nursing dependency they can accommodate at the Seksyen 13 site specifically. Ask whether a resident can receive opposite-gender medical attention in emergencies (their stated dharurah policy allows this with a mahram present), and clarify whether the branch handles bedridden or dementia cases or refers high-dependency residents to their other locations.
address: 6, Jalan Bola Lisut 13/17, Seksyen 13, 40100 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Avicenna Nursing Centre
editorial_summary:
Avicenna Nursing Centre occupies a shophouse unit at No. 64-A, Jalan Renang 13/26 in Tadisma Business Park, Shah Alam's Seksyen 13 — a light-industrial and commercial zone about 2 km from Avisena Specialist Hospital (note the different spelling; the two are unrelated). The name references Ibn Sina, the medieval Islamic physician, which alongside the location in predominantly Malay Shah Alam may hint at a Muslim-oriented operation, though this is unconfirmed. The centre is listed simply as a nursing home with a single mobile contact number and no public website or Facebook page found.

Publicly available information about Avicenna Nursing Centre is extremely limited. No service menu, bed count, staffing details, or pricing have been published online. The shophouse unit format in a business park typically suggests a smaller facility — likely under 30 beds — operating on a practical rather than resort model. Proximity to Avisena Specialist Hospital and the broader Shah Alam Hospital catchment may be an operational advantage for residents needing frequent specialist appointments.

Families should treat this as a facility requiring a full in-person visit before any decision. Key questions include: JKM registration and licence number, total bed capacity, nurse coverage at night, whether the unit has been purpose-fitted or is a basic conversion, and what clinical capabilities (wound care, catheter management, NGT feeding) are on offer. The shophouse setting in an industrial park also warrants a look at the physical environment — ventilation, outdoor access, and amenities for residents who are ambulant.
address: NO. 64-A, JALAN RENANG 13/26 TADISMA BUSINESS PARK, Seksyen 13, 40100 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Oasis Nursing Home (Setia Alam)
editorial_summary:
Oasis Nursing Home's Setia Alam branch is located at 66 Jalan Setia Impian U13/8B in the Setia Alam township, Shah Alam — a double-storey corner house within one of Selangor's best-planned residential developments. The facility is one of three Oasis branches operating across the greater Klang Valley, alongside locations in Shah Alam U12 and Klang, giving the group operational depth that single-location homes lack. The lead physician across the Oasis network is Dr. Wendy Oon, and the Setia Alam site shares the same medical and nursing leadership as the group's other branches. A 5-star Google Maps rating from six reviews reflects early strong feedback.

The Oasis group website lists a clear clinical remit across its branches: long-term care, short-term and post-hospitalisation care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and dementia care. Named staff include a physiotherapist (Nur Ain) and nursing leads (Shaziq as Head Nurse, Siti Ramlah as Assistant Head Nurse), indicating a structured clinical hierarchy rather than an informally run home. The 24-hour care model with physician oversight makes this positioning closer to a nursing facility than an assisted-living centre. Phone contact is via mobile (+60 12-270 3166) and email at oasisnursingcare@gmail.com.

Bed count and pricing for the Setia Alam branch specifically are not published. As a corner double-storey house, physical capacity will be modest — families should call ahead to check availability. The Setia Alam township location is an advantage for residents from Shah Alam, Klang, or Subang who want a new, well-serviced suburb setting, but the nearest major hospital (Hospital Shah Alam) is approximately 10–15 minutes by car. Confirm whether the named physician conducts regular site visits at the Setia Alam branch specifically, and ask for the JKM registration number.
address: 66, Jalan Setia Impian U13/8b, Setia Alam, 40170 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Mona Elder Care | Nursing Home in Pandan Perdana
editorial_summary:
Mona Elder Care's Pandan Perdana branch is located at No. 32, Jalan Perdana 1/4, Pandan Perdana, in the part of the district that straddles the KL-Selangor boundary near Cheras. It is one of at least four Mona Elder Care locations in the Klang Valley — the others being in Ampang, Setapak, and Taiping — and the group's website presents a consistent clinical identity across all sites. The branch targets seniors who need nursing home-level care in a residential neighbourhood setting, with particular emphasis on stroke and coma cases, dementia, and palliative care alongside standard elderly nursing care for immobile or medically complex residents.

The services page lists a notably detailed clinical menu: bedsore dressing, nasogastric tube changes, colostomy bag management, Foley catheter care, tracheostomy care, and post-operative support alongside physiotherapy, respite care, day care, and handicapped care for adults with disabilities. This breadth suggests the facility can take on residents who require active nursing intervention, not just assisted daily living. A 24/7 care model is stated across the group's online presence, and the address is described as accessible to nearby clinics, hospitals, and public transport — useful for families attending regular follow-up appointments.

Bed count, specific staff ratios, and pricing are not disclosed publicly by Mona Elder Care; all of these should be asked directly when you call the Pandan Perdana number (+60 14-688 7358). There is no mention of JKM licence numbers, religious affiliation, or the primary languages spoken by caregivers on the website. The Facebook page for the group shows activity but does not distinguish between branches, so clarify which site photos and updates refer to.
address: 32, Jalan Perdana 1/4, Pandan Perdana, 55300 Kuala Lumpur, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Happy Family Nursing Home (Kepong)
editorial_summary:
Happy Family Nursing Home (Kepong) is at 335, Jalan Kuang Gunung, Taman Kepong, 52100 Kuala Lumpur. It is one of several houses under the Happy Family brand, which also operates in Taman Kepong (Jalan 2), Taman Desa Jaya, Kajang and Cheras. The official corporate website is currently down, but third-party directory pages confirm the address and phone number 018-777 7383. Google reviews are mixed at 2.8 across 47 ratings as of mid-2026 — a notably low score relative to other Kepong homes and the largest review sample on this batch, so families should weigh recent review themes carefully before booking a visit.

The operator describes itself as a long-running affordable nursing home for the elderly, with bathing, housekeeping, grooming, basic medical support and meals, and says it equips its houses with electronic medical equipment for residents with disabilities or chronic disease. Specific capabilities published online are limited: bed count, registered nurse roster, doctor visit cadence, room types and monthly pricing are not disclosed publicly for this branch, and there is no clear separation in marketing between the Kuang Gunung, Jalan 2, and Desa Jaya houses.

Kepong residents looking for a budget-tier nursing home close to home will recognise the brand, and the multi-house network may help with capacity. Given the 2.8 rating, prospective families should read the actual Google reviews before visiting, ask specifically about staffing ratios overnight, infection-control routines, hygiene and bathing schedules, and how complaints are handled. On a tour: confirm JKM Pusat Jagaan registration number, ask to see the bedrooms in use (not show rooms), check the toilet-to-resident ratio, and ask whether the home takes NG/PEG feeds, wounds and bed-bound residents. Compare against the higher-rated Jalan 2 sister house (4.4) before deciding.
address: 335, Jalan Kuang Gunung, Taman Kepong, 52100 Kepong, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Mintygreen Nursing & Retirement Home (Kepong and PJ Bandar Sri Damansara)
care_types: Nursing home, Retirement community, Retirement home
editorial_summary:
Mintygreen's Bandar Sri Damansara branch occupies a commercial block at Sphere Damansara, Jalan PJU 10/9 in Prima Damansara — a location that sits at the Selayang-KL border and draws families from both Kepong and the northern PJ corridor. Part of a chain founded in 2017 and now operating across seven sites in the Klang Valley and Penang, the centre markets itself as the first senior care operator in Malaysia to structurally integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine into its standard programme. It serves a range of dependency levels, from semi-ambulant residents who need some daily assistance through to fully bedridden patients requiring round-the-clock nursing.

The website lists a broad scope of clinical nursing care: NG tube feeding, catheter care, insulin management, and pressure ulcer prevention and management. Day care and short-term respite stays are available alongside long-term placements, and the facility specifically mentions post-stroke, post-cancer, and post-operative recovery tracks. TCM physicians provide on-site acupuncture and Tui Na as complementary therapies. A general practitioner visits bi-weekly, and the wider Mintygreen group holds an AgeCope certification (listed on the AgeCope registry under the Emerald Care Center entity). Visiting hours are listed as 10am to 6pm.

One gap to note: bed capacity is not publicly disclosed for the BSD branch specifically, and pricing is not published online — you will need to call to get a quotation. The facility is physically inside a commercial shopblock rather than a purpose-built building, which affects the feel of communal outdoor space; ask about the activity area and how often residents access it. Languages supported appear to be English and Mandarin, consistent with the chain's bilingual marketing; families who need Malay-medium communication should confirm staff language coverage before committing.
address: A03-09, Sphere Damansara, Jalan PJU 10/9, Prima Damansara, 47830 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: OpulenceCARE II
care_types: Assisted living facility, Nursing home, Senior citizen center
editorial_summary:
OpulenceCARE II (甲洞乐理护老之家) is the second house in the OpulenceCARE chain in Taman Kepong, Kuala Lumpur. The group also runs OpulenceCARE 64 nearby on Jalan Development. Listed Google categories are assisted living facility, nursing home, and senior citizen centre, indicating the home accepts a mix of dependency levels rather than only bed-bound nursing cases. Reviews stand at 3.9 across 8 ratings as of mid-2026 — a small sample, so individual visits matter more than the score. The operator is reachable on +603 6262 9778 and on WhatsApp, and the brand markets in both English and Chinese.

OpulenceCARE describes its houses as offering large rooms, exercise facilities and socialising areas, and positions itself in the cleanliness and environment tier rather than the budget tier. Public detail beyond that is light: bed count for the II house, room types and monthly fees, registered nurse staffing overnight, doctor visit arrangements, dementia handling (whether there is a locked memory unit), and clinical capabilities (PEG, wound, oxygen, dialysis transport) are not published online. The chain has at least two operating houses in Kepong, suggesting a settled local operation.

This branch may suit Kepong-area families wanting a Mandarin-speaking, mid-tier home with shared activity space rather than a hospital-style ward feel. Ask on a visit: which Pusat Jagaan licence number applies to OpulenceCARE II specifically, RN coverage overnight, monthly fees by room type and what is bundled (diapers, milk, physio, doctor), how many residents are on NG/PEG and how many are ambulant, and the home's policy on wandering dementia residents. Compare directly against OpulenceCARE 64 down the road if both have vacancies.
address: 1, Jalan 7, Taman Kepong, 52100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Kepong Nursing Home
url: /facilities/协和开心之家安心养老庭院-甲洞安老院-kepong-nursing-home
editorial_summary:
协和开心之家安心养老庭院 (Kepong Nursing Home / 甲洞安老院) sits in Kepong Baru, Kuala Lumpur, on the +60 16-251 5662 line. Families should call before visiting and clarify: is this an adult day care only or does it accept residential stays, total beds and current availability, monthly or daily pricing, JKM Pusat Jagaan registration, RN versus PRA carer staffing, hours of operation, dementia handling, transport to and from home, languages spoken by carers, and halal status of meals if relevant. Visit the premises and inspect bathrooms, bedrooms (if residential), and the activity area before committing.
address: 82, Jalan Api Api 5, Kepong Baru, 52100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Happy Family Nursing Home (Taman Kepong) (Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Happy Family)
editorial_summary:
Happy Family Nursing Home (Taman Kepong), trading also as Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Happy Family, sits at No. 38 & 36, Jalan 2, Taman Kepong, Kuala Lumpur. The business was registered as Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Taman Kepong in June 2015 (registration 002452041A), giving it roughly a decade of trading history at the time of writing. It is a sister house to Happy Family Nursing Home (Kepong) on Jalan Kuang Gunung roughly 600 metres away, and to Happy Family (Desa Jaya). The contact line for this house is 019-777 7328 — different from the Kuang Gunung branch — and Google reviews are 4.4 across 13 ratings as of mid-2026, distinctly higher than the 2.8 score on the sister house.

The Happy Family brand markets itself as an affordable nursing home for elderly residents needing 24-hour care, with bathing, grooming, housekeeping, scheduled meals, basic medical support, and electronic medical equipment for residents with disabilities or chronic conditions. The Jalan 2 location occupies two adjoining shoplots (Nos 36 and 38), which is typical of small KL nursing homes. Specific bed count, registered nurse staffing, doctor visit cadence, and pricing tiers are not published online for this branch, and the central corporate website is currently suspended.

This Jalan 2 branch suits Kepong-area families looking for a budget-tier residential home with a longer trading history and a measurably better Google score than the nearby Kuang Gunung sister house. Worth asking on a visit: which house operates under which JKM Pusat Jagaan licence (the same group can have separate licences), RN overnight, total beds and current vacancy, monthly fees by room type, what is included (diapers, feeds, doctor, physio, transport to dialysis), and whether the home takes residents on NG/PEG feeds, with wounds, or with dementia who wander. Compare in person with the Kuang Gunung house before deciding which Happy Family branch to use.
address: 38, Jalan 2, Taman Kepong, 52100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Caregiver in KL
care_types: Home health care service, Service establishment, Nursing agency
editorial_summary:
Caregiver in KL is a home health care and nursing agency serving the Klang Valley, listed in Google Maps under the categories of home health care service, service establishment, and nursing agency. The registered phone number is +60 12-382 0255. The business appears to operate as a placement or staffing service connecting trained caregivers and nurses with families who need in-home support — a model common among smaller agencies in the KL and Selangor corridor that do not operate a physical residential facility. The exact area of operations within Selangor is not specified in the listing beyond the broad geographic tag.

Agencies of this type typically provide live-in or part-time caregivers, registered nurses for home visits, and sometimes relief or respite cover for family carers. The home health care and nursing agency classification indicates the service likely covers basic nursing tasks — medication management, wound dressing, mobility assistance — in addition to companion and personal care. Because the agency keeps a low online profile, detailed information on staff credentials, screening procedures, response times, and the range of clinical procedures covered is best gathered on a direct call.

Families exploring Caregiver in KL should ask at the outset whether the agency employs its own staff or operates as a marketplace placing independent contractors, since this affects accountability and consistency of care. It is worth establishing what qualifications and background checks are carried out on caregivers, whether a registered nurse is available for clinical oversight, and what the escalation path looks like if a caregiver is unwell or the client's condition changes unexpectedly. Confirming the hourly or monthly rates, minimum engagement hours, and whether the agency is registered with a relevant authority will complete the picture before making a placement decision.

title: Lorong Hj. Othman 2, Kampung Giching Hilir
editorial_summary:
This listing corresponds to an address in Lorong Hj. Othman 2, Kampung Giching Hilir — a rural Malay kampung settlement in the Sepang district of southern Selangor, south of Salak Tinggi and some distance from the urban nursing home concentration in Shah Alam, Subang Jaya, or Petaling Jaya. The Google Maps category of assisted living facility indicates the premises function as a care home for seniors or adults with support needs, most likely operating as a small community or family-run home rather than a purpose-built commercial facility. No phone number, website, or Facebook page is attached to the listing.

Facilities in rural kampung settings like Giching Hilir tend to operate in a traditional gotong-royong or family-extended model, often accepting a small number of residents from the local community. The Malay-Muslim context of the area points toward a halal food environment and Islamic observance as the default cultural framework. Bed counts in settings like this are typically modest — single digits to low teens — with care delivered by family members or locally trained caregivers rather than credentialed nursing staff. Regulatory standing with the Department of Social Welfare (JKM) is a particularly important question given how few details are in the public domain.

Families interested in this placement should contact the home directly through Google Maps or by asking locals in Kampung Giching Hilir for the operator's details, as no direct phone number is currently findable online. On that first call, priority questions include licensed capacity, whether a registered nurse visits regularly, what arrangements are in place for medical emergencies given the relatively remote location, and how meals, activities, and personal care are structured day to day. The facility's JKM registration status is worth confirming before proceeding.
address: Lot 7242, Lorong Hj. Osman 2, Kampung Giching, 43900 Sepang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Rumah Kebajikan Calvary Land
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Rumah Kebajikan Calvary Land is part of Calvaryland, an integrated social welfare campus operated by Calvary Church of Malaysia in Sungai Pelek, Sepang District. Set on 10 acres of eco-friendly land roughly 50 km south of Kuala Lumpur, the campus houses multiple residential programmes — including a home for older adults, a children's home, and a women-in-crisis shelter — all under one roof. The facility has been registered with the Selangor Social Welfare Department since 2008 and operates as a registered non-profit charity. It is explicitly non-discriminatory in admissions, welcoming residents regardless of religion, ethnicity, or gender.

The elderly wing provides residential accommodation focused on companionship, daily routines, and community living in a quiet, semi-rural setting. The agricultural and green surroundings are cited as part of the care philosophy — offering peace and a natural environment that larger urban homes cannot replicate. As a welfare-oriented centre, the mission prioritises the poor, marginalised, and neglected, meaning residents are often those with limited family support or financial means. Registration number B/PJB P 01/2022 is on record.

Because Calvaryland functions primarily as a welfare home rather than a medically-staffed nursing facility, families should ask directly about clinical capabilities: whether trained nurses are on-site, what happens when a resident's health deteriorates, and whether the home can manage residents with complex medical needs such as dementia or PEG feeding. The rural Sungai Pelek location — while tranquil — may be a practical consideration for families in Klang Valley who want frequent visits. Pricing is likely subsidised or donation-based given its charity status, so a direct call to clarify fees and admission criteria is strongly recommended.
address: Lot.471, Jalan Ke Kampung Ulu Chuchoh Sungai Pelek, Selangor, 43950 Sepang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Sam Wound And Home Nursing Care
editorial_summary:
Sam Wound and Home Nursing Care is listed on Google Maps as a nursing agency and service establishment operating out of Selangor, with a contact number of +60 19-202 9418. The 5-star rating from 32 reviews is striking for any healthcare provider — and particularly so for a home nursing agency, where reviews typically reflect the personal relationship between nurse and family rather than institutional factors. As the name suggests, wound care appears to be the primary clinical offering, positioning the service for families managing post-operative wounds, diabetic ulcers, pressure injuries, or other complex wound dressings in a home setting.

Home nursing agencies of this type typically deploy registered or enrolled nurses to a patient's home for scheduled visits — usually one to three times per week for wound dressings — rather than providing continuous residential care. This is a fundamentally different service model from a residential nursing home: the patient stays at home, and the nurse comes to them. If correctly understood, Sam Wound and Home Nursing Care fills a specific and genuinely useful gap for families who want clinical nursing support without moving a family member into a facility.

No website, Facebook page, or formal business directory listing was found during research, which limits the ability to verify the scope of services, pricing, areas covered, or the qualifications of the nurses deployed. Families should ask directly about nurse registration status (Lembaga Jururawat Malaysia), the specific wound types handled, turnaround time for urgent callouts, and whether any non-wound nursing services (catheter care, NG tube, IV administration) are also available. A referral from a hospital discharge team or specialist nurse would add further confidence before engaging.

title: Alia Home
editorial_summary:
Alia Home is listed in Google Maps as a retirement home located in Kg Gicing Hilir (also written Kampung Giching Hilir), in the Sepang district of southern Selangor. This is a semi-rural kampung setting south of Salak Tinggi, geographically distinct from the main elder-care clusters in the northern and central parts of the state. No phone number, website, or social media page is linked to the listing. The Google Maps category of retirement home indicates the facility is intended for older residents who require supported accommodation and daily care in a residential setting, likely in a modest, community-facing format consistent with similar homes in this part of Selangor.

The Gicing Hilir location sits in a predominantly Malay-Muslim area, which suggests the home most likely observes halal food standards and an Islamic daily routine by default. Small residential retirement homes in kampung environments in Malaysia commonly operate with a close-knit, family-extended model — a handful of residents, owner-led daily care, and local community integration rather than a formal programme schedule. Clinical staffing arrangements and the extent of medical support available on-site are not documented publicly and would need to be established on a direct visit.

Families considering Alia Home should make contact through Google Maps messaging or through personal referrals from the local Kampung Giching community, as no published phone number is currently available. The most important questions for an initial call are the number of residents currently in residence and licensed capacity, how day-to-day care is staffed, what medical or nursing support is available and how frequently, and what the plan is for a resident who needs hospital care given the home's distance from major health facilities. JKM registration status should also be confirmed early in the process.
address: Plot 7, Lot 39282, Jalan Hj Marmo, Salak Tinggi, Kg Gicing Hilir, 43900 Sepang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Komuniti
editorial_summary:
PUSAT KOMUNITI is a listing at Desa Vista in Selangor, categorised in Google Maps as an assisted living facility. Desa Vista is a residential township development in the Sabak Bernam or northern Selangor corridor, and the name Pusat Komuniti — meaning Community Centre — is widely used in Malaysia for both government-supported community gathering spaces and privately run small care homes that market themselves to the local community. The listing carries no phone number, website, or Facebook page, and no business records or directory entries for this specific address appear in publicly accessible sources. No details on capacity, staffing, or care programmes are available online.

Community-based assisted living placements in settings like this typically serve a local catchment with a small resident count, a Bahasa Malaysia-primary environment, and culturally appropriate care for the demographic of the surrounding neighbourhood. If the facility is Malay-Muslim in orientation, halal food and Islamic observance would be the norm. If it is registered as an assisted living centre with JKM under the Care Centres Act 1993, that licence would be the key quality signal available to families prior to a visit.

Because the home keeps a very low online profile, the most practical first step is to use the Google Maps listing to attempt a direct message or to ask the local Desa Vista residents' association for contact details. On a first call, families should establish whether this is a JKM-licensed care centre or a community-use venue with a different primary function, what the current resident count and licensed capacity are, what daily care and medical oversight look like, and whether there is direct transport access for family visits. A site visit before any placement decision is strongly recommended given the limited information currently available.
address: Desa Vista, 43900 Sepang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Loving Elderly Care Center Sdn. Bhd., LECC
editorial_summary:
Loving Elderly Care Center (LECC) is a private, incorporated care home — registered as an Sdn Bhd — operating in Klang, Selangor, with a listed address at 2B, Jalan Bidor/KU1, off Jalan Landasan Bukit Kuda, Mukim Kapar, 41300 Klang. The Google Maps pin places it in the Taman Summit vicinity.
