With over six years of operation and a presence on Facebook (facebook.com/lovingelderly), LECC markets itself as a home-style nursing environment that combines nursing home medical support with assisted living flexibility, aiming at residents who need care but benefit from a less clinical setting.

LECC describes itself as having managed two care facilities in the Klang Valley across its operating history, which suggests accumulated experience in the sector. The Google listing categorises it as both nursing home and assisted living facility, and staff have described an ongoing commitment to upgrading their physical environment and service standards. That said, specific verified data — bed capacity, staffing ratios, whether nurses are on duty overnight, and whether dementia or palliative care is offered — has not been independently confirmed from public sources.

The 3.5 Google rating from four reviews is on the lower side and warrants attention; read the actual reviews before visiting. Contact LECC on 019-217 1108 to ask about current vacancies, the daily care schedule, nurse coverage overnight, pricing structure, and whether they hold a valid JKM care home licence. Given the mixed rating, a physical inspection of the facility and staff interactions is particularly important before making a decision.
address: 2B, Jalan Bidor/Ku1, Jalan Bukit Kuda, Taman Summit, 41300 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Aurora Home Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Aurora Home Care Centre is a small residential care facility at 166E, Jalan Sireh, off Jalan Meru, Kawasan 17, 41050 Klang, Selangor — a mixed residential area roughly midway between Klang town centre and Port Klang. The home maintains a Facebook presence at facebook.com/Aurorahomecarecentre. Phone: +60 16-690 6333.

Two Google Maps photos are available. Care details and capacity are not published beyond the Facebook page, which is the primary public-facing channel for the facility.

One review rates it 5.0 stars, praising the caregivers and specifically naming a staff member for attentive, consistent care. For a Klang-based facility with an active social media presence, this is a reasonable starting point. Confirm JKM licence, care types, current availability and monthly fees when you call.
address: 166e, Jalan Sireh, off, Jln Meru, Kawasan 17, 41050 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Home Town Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Home Town Care Centre is listed on Google Maps as a nursing home in the Kawasan 6 area of Shah Alam, Selangor, reachable at +60 16-335 4865. It carries a 3.9-star rating from 14 reviews — the lowest rating in this batch — which is worth noting even though the review count is small. No website, Facebook page, or independent directory listing was found during research, and the facility does not appear in any of the major nursing home aggregator lists for the Shah Alam or Petaling Jaya corridors.

With no accessible digital presence, nothing about the facility's capacity, staffing, care dependency range, or pricing can be verified from public sources. The Kawasan 6 location places it in a light industrial and mixed residential zone of Shah Alam, which is a common setting for smaller neighbourhood nursing homes. The Google classification as a nursing home suggests it operates residential beds rather than day-care only, but this has not been confirmed.

The below-average Google rating warrants particular caution. Before visiting, try to identify the nature of any negative reviews — whether they relate to hygiene, staffing, communication, or management. An in-person visit should assess room conditions, odour control, staff attentiveness, and the physical state of the premises. Ask directly how many beds are occupied, what nursing qualifications staff hold, how medical emergencies are managed, and whether the facility is currently licensed with JKM. This is a case where a personal visit is the only reliable way to form a view.
address: 05, Jalan Emas Putih, Kawasan 6, 41100 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: City Home Care Centre
editorial_summary:
City Home Care Centre is a nursing home at 20, Lorong Kadok, Taman Chi Liung, 41200 Klang, Selangor, with a website at dannycarecentre.wixsite.com. Phone: +60 16-612 3368. The facility has 10 photos documented on Google Maps.

Care details and bed capacity are not published on the website. No staffing qualifications or JKM licence number is listed online.

Six Google Maps reviews average 1.7 stars. Families considering this facility are strongly encouraged to read the Google Maps reviews in full before proceeding, visit the facility in person, and clarify in writing: the deposit amount and refund policy, the JKM licence number, staffing qualifications, and the escalation procedure if a resident requires urgent hospital transfer.
address: SS 9a Petaling Jaya, and, 20, Lorong Kadok, Taman Chi Liung, 41200 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: House of Glory
editorial_summary:
House of Glory is a small nursing home at 16, Jalan 23/119, Taman Taynton View, 56000 Cheras, Kuala Lumpur, registered as House Of Glory Sdn. Bhd. (1205244-A). It runs out of a single residential lot in a quiet Cheras neighbourhood, with UKM Specialist Centre and Pantai Hospital Cheras both within roughly a 12-minute drive for hospital escalation. Google reviews are 4.3 across only 3 ratings as of mid-2026, so the public review sample is too small to draw conclusions from — direct site visits matter more than the headline score for a home of this size.

House of Glory's published menu covers long-term residential stay, short-term stay, and day care, in a choice of single, double-shared, or open dormitory rooms. Care described includes scheduled medication administration, home-cooked meals, housekeeping and laundry, family gathering sessions, and a recreational programme covering physical fitness, social and learning activities, plus festival celebrations during Chinese New Year and Christmas (suggesting a primarily Chinese resident profile). The site is bilingual in English and Chinese. Specific bed count, registered nurse coverage, doctor visit arrangements, clinical capabilities (NG/PEG/wound/oxygen) and monthly pricing are not published.

The home is a fit for Cheras-area families wanting a small, home-style residential setting with mixed room tiers, particularly for relatively independent or moderate-dependency elders rather than complex post-acute cases. Worth asking on a tour: JKM Pusat Jagaan registration number, total beds and vacancy in each room type, the registered nurse roster overnight versus daytime carers only, doctor visit arrangement, what monthly fees include (diapers, feeds, doctor, physio, transport), whether the home takes residents on NG/PEG, with active wounds, or with wandering dementia, and which hospital they default to in an emergency.
address: 16, Jalan 23/119, Taman Taynton View, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Komune Care
care_types: Assisted living facility, Nursing home, Occupational therapist
editorial_summary:
Komune Care is a purpose-built assisted-living and senior daycare facility at 21, Jalan Tasik Permaisuri 2, Bandar Tun Razak, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, operated under UOA Group inside the Komune Living & Wellness mixed-use development overlooking Tasik Permaisuri urban park. It is structurally different from a typical KL shoplot nursing home: of 718 units in the Komune Living tower 141 are dedicated to Komune Care eldercare and 577 operate as serviced hotel-style units, with the design emphasising single-level floors, lift access, wheelchair-wide passageways and intergenerational living. Google reviews are 4.5 across 19 ratings as of mid-2026, and AGECOPE (the Association of Gerontologists, Care Operators and Professionals for the Elderly) lists the centre as a member.

Komune Care's published service set covers assisted living suites, dementia care with structured routines, post-hospital recovery, respite stays, and senior daycare, with capacity quoted as 150 residential beds and a daycare centre that can accommodate over 80 attendees. Residents stay in private en-suite rooms with 24-hour nursing and caregiving cover and emergency response. On-site amenities include a traditional Chinese medicine centre, medical and dental clinics, swimming pools, karaoke, game rooms and a clubhouse. UOA Academy, an HRD Corp-aligned training centre, runs accredited eldercare programmes that feed graduates directly into Komune Care, which is unusual for the local sector and suggests a structured staffing pipeline. Pricing is not published online and is positioned at the premium end of the KL market.

Komune Care suits families looking for a premium, purpose-built setting close to the city for parents who want hotel-grade hospitality with on-site clinical and dementia support, including those recovering from hospital stays. It is generally not the budget-tier shoplot option. Worth asking on a tour: which licence covers the residential beds (Pusat Jagaan or Private Aged Healthcare under Act 802), nurse-to-resident ratio overnight, current monthly rates per room tier and what is bundled versus billed (medications, doctor visits, physio, dietitian), the dementia ward layout and whether it is locked, halal kitchen status, and how the building handles fire evacuation given the tower layout.
address: M-03A, Komune Living & Wellness, 21, Jalan Tasik Permaisuri 2, Bandar Tun Razak, 56000 Cheras, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Papa & Mama Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Papa & Mama Care Centre (好爸妈安老院) is a small private nursing home at 13, Jalan Bunga Melur 1, Taman Muda, on the Cheras side of Kuala Lumpur. The home is a member of AGECOPE, the national association of registered aged-care operators, which lists it as a non-NGO nursing home with bookings handled directly by the family-run operator. Its bilingual Chinese-English Facebook presence and Mandarin name suggest a primarily Chinese-speaking household, and the residential-street setting on a cul-de-sac off Jalan Cheras is the kind of converted terrace house that dominates this part of Cheras.

Verified offering is limited to what the operator publishes: a registered nursing home placement (long-stay residential care), with enquiries channelled through phone or email rather than a website. The Google profile carries a 5-star rating but only a handful of reviews, so the rating reflects a small sample rather than a track record. AGECOPE membership is the most useful external signal here: it indicates the operator has registered with the sector body, but it does not by itself confirm a JKM (Jabatan Kebajikan Masyarakat) Act 506 licence, which is the licence families should ask to see.

This home is likely to suit families looking for a smaller, neighbourhood-scale Chinese-speaking nursing home in Taman Muda or upper Cheras, particularly those who prefer a home-like setting over an institutional one. Because almost no detail is published online, families should call ahead and ask directly: current bed availability and room types; monthly fees and what is included (diapers, milk feeds, consumables); whether the home accepts bedridden, NG-tube or PEG-fed residents; nurse-to-resident ratio day and night; whether a doctor visits and how often; and a copy of the JKM Act 506 registration certificate. A site visit during a meal or activity period is worth the trip.
address: 13, Jalan Bunga Melur 1, Taman Muda, 56100 Cheras, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Loving touch care center
editorial_summary:
Loving Touch Care Center is listed on Google Maps as a nursing home and medical billing service in the Kawasan 6 area of Shah Alam, Selangor, reachable at +60 18-358 3505. It holds a 4.9-star rating from 11 reviews, which is strong for a small facility and suggests those who have used it have been broadly satisfied. Beyond the Google listing, no website, Facebook page, or third-party directory entry was found during research. The facility does not appear in any of the nursing home aggregator lists covering Shah Alam or surrounding areas.

The Google Maps categorisation as both a nursing home and a medical billing service is an unusual pairing that may reflect how the operator has tagged their listing rather than a meaningful description of services. Nothing about the facility's capacity, staffing, clinical capabilities, pricing, or care dependency range can be verified from publicly available sources. Kawasan 6, Shah Alam, is a mixed-use zone that commonly hosts smaller neighbourhood care homes within shophouse or terrace-house premises.

Families interested in this facility should phone ahead to confirm it is still operating under this name and at this location, as listings without any web presence can sometimes reflect outdated or inactive entries. If it is operational, an in-person visit is the only way to assess the facility's physical conditions, staffing qualifications, and suitability for a specific resident's care needs. Key questions include: What dependency levels do you accept? Are nurses on duty overnight? How do you handle medical emergencies? Is the facility registered with JKM and what is the licence number?
address: 4, Jalan Besi, Kawasan 6, 41100 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: I-Care Haven - Home for the Elderly
care_types: Nursing home, Home health care service, Aged care
editorial_summary:
I-Care Haven sits at 24, Lorong Pendita 1, Taman Connaught, on a quiet residential cul-de-sac a few minutes from Taman Connaught MRT. The home is a converted double-storey bungalow rather than a purpose-built facility, and the operator markets it under a "home away from home" concept with a small garden that supplies vegetables to the kitchen. It is run as a Full Service Home for Aged Care offering both short and long-term placements, with enquiries handled by a single operator contact line.

The operator states it provides 24-hour nursing cover, daily occupational therapy and physiotherapy sessions for residents, and a monthly doctor visit for routine review. Room options run from single rooms up to five-bedded shared rooms, which is typical for a converted-bungalow nursing home in this part of Cheras. An on-call ambulance arrangement is in place for hospital transfers. Capacity, exact staffing numbers, RN cover overnight and JKM Act 506 licence number are not published; the website is dated and parts have been suspended for renewal, so any specifics need to be confirmed by phone.

This is the kind of home that suits families who want a smaller, residential-feel placement near Cheras and the MRT, especially for relatively stable elderly relatives who need 24-hour supervision and basic nursing rather than acute medical care. Before committing, ask: how many beds in total and what is currently available; whether a registered nurse is rostered overnight or only carers; the named doctor and visit frequency; monthly fees by room type and what is excluded (diapers, milk, consumables, physio sessions); whether they take PEG/NG-fed, tracheostomy or fully bedridden residents; halal kitchen arrangements if relevant; and the JKM Act 506 registration certificate. Visit at mealtime to see staffing in practice.
address: 24, Lorong Pendita 1, Taman Connaught, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Hemei Life Care Center Klang
editorial_summary:
Hemei Life Care Center's Klang outlet sits at 10, Jalan Batu Tiga Lama, Kawasan 16, Klang — part of a multi-branch chain that also operates in Cheras, Seri Kembangan, Selayang, Sri Damansara, and Johor Bahru. The group positions itself squarely around short-to-medium-term recovery and step-down care rather than purely long-term placement, with long stay, short stay, day care, and post-operative rehabilitation all listed as core offerings. That makes the Klang branch a candidate not just for families seeking permanent residential care but also for patients who need a supervised recovery environment after surgery or a hospital discharge before returning home.

A third-party aggregator (seniorliving.com.my) lists the Klang branch with a capacity of 200 private rooms and quotes care tiers as follows: independent or assisted living from RM 4,000 per month (standard RM 5,000), wheelchair or dementia care from RM 4,500 (standard RM 5,500), and bedridden or tube-feeding care from RM 5,000 (standard RM 6,000), with a RM 300 add-on for a medical bed. Chargeable extras listed include diapers, underpads, gloves, wet wipes, and wound dressing. The website emphasises hotel-style accommodation, CCTV surveillance, four daily meals, safety handrails, and activity programming to support mental health.

Operating hours shown on the contact page are 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM, which raises a practical question about after-hours access for family visits and whether night-shift nursing coverage is equivalent to daytime. The Klang branch was listed as 'coming soon' on some earlier versions of the Hemei website, so families should confirm it is now fully operational and ask for an updated resident count before committing. Specialist services such as physiotherapy and dementia care, which are available at other Hemei branches, should be verified as active at this specific location.
address: Level 1 & 2, Swing and Pillows @ I-City Shah Alam, 10, Jalan Batu Tiga Lama, Kawasan 16, 41300 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Anns Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Ann's Care Centre is a nursing home and assisted living facility at 123, Jalan Pakis 2, Taman Ferngrove, Batu 8½, Cheras 43200, Selangor — a well-established residential area in the inner Cheras belt with reasonable access to hospital facilities serving the Cheras corridor. Reachable on 03-9076 4855, the centre has been listed in multiple healthcare directories including nursinghome.my, and maintains a Facebook presence at facebook.com/AnnsCareCentre. Its 5-star rating comes from three reviews — encouraging but a small sample.

The facility openly describes its scope as covering geriatric care, medical and surgical nursing cases, bedsore management, post-stroke rehabilitation support, and major nursing care. It accepts both short-term stays (including respite and post-hospitalisation recovery) and long-term placements. This positions Ann's as a clinically-oriented nursing home rather than a basic residential home — suited to residents who need hands-on nursing attention rather than simply a supervised living environment.

Specific details not confirmed publicly include the bed count, staffing ratios (particularly nurse coverage at night), pricing, and whether specialised dementia care or palliative support is available. When calling to enquire, ask about current bed availability, nurse qualifications, how post-stroke cases are managed day-to-day, and the monthly fee for your family member's level of dependency. Confirm JKM registration and ask whether a doctor visits the facility regularly.
address: 123, Jalan Pakis 2, Batu 8 1/2, Taman Ferngrove, 43200 Cheras, Selangor, Malaysia

title: PALS Caring Home
editorial_summary:
PALS Caring Home sits at No. 19, Jalan 32, Kampung Cheras Baru — a residential neighbourhood in southern Cheras that straddles the Selangor-KL border. The facility markets itself as a nursing home providing high-quality assisted living, and its website lists a wide range of conditions it supports: stroke, dementia, Parkinson's, diabetes, cancer, palliative, post-hospitalization, post-surgery, and arthritis care. With a Google rating of 4.9 from 11 reviews, the limited review pool suggests a small, community-facing operation rather than a large corporate facility.

The website indicates that care plans are personalised to each resident and that stay-in caregivers provide overnight coverage when needed. Listed programmes include daily exercises, nutritious meals designed with seniors' digestive needs in mind, and various activities for physical and mental engagement. PALS also promotes home-care services alongside its residential offering, which suggests staffing capacity to support both settings. Dementia care and rehabilitation therapy are specifically cited as areas of focus, though the depth of clinical capability — specialist nurses, physiotherapy equipment, or dementia unit infrastructure — is not detailed on the site.

Bed count and pricing are not publicly disclosed. The Cheras Baru location puts residents reasonably close to Hospital Cheras and the range of specialist facilities along the MRR2 corridor, which matters for follow-up appointments. Families should ask directly about nurse-to-resident ratios on night shifts, what constitutes dementia care in practice (secure unit versus general supervision), and the precise scope of rehabilitation — whether a physiotherapist attends in person or exercises are caregiver-led.
address: 19, Jalan 32, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Kuala Lumpur, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Prowell Elderly Home Care Centre - Hq
care_types: Nursing Home + Dementia + Palliative + Rehab + Day Care
editorial_summary:
Prowell Elderly Home Care Centre is a family-owned operator billing itself as a fifth-generation company in the senior living space. The HQ sits at 17-1, Jalan Dato Haji Harun, Taman Taynton View, Cheras, with a second branch at 2, Persiaran Titiwangsa 2, Titiwangsa, both in Kuala Lumpur. The website is pehcc.com.my.

Clinical capability is a stated strength: the centre lists feeding tube management, urinary catheter care, wound and bedsore dressing, tracheostomy management, physiotherapy and 24/7 doctor on-call with ambulance service. Care programmes cover long-term, short-term, retirement, dementia, bedridden and day care. Operating hours are daily 9am to 7pm.

Seven Google Maps reviews of the Cheras HQ average 4.1 stars. One reviewer expressed gratitude for compassionate end-of-life care provided to their brother. Pricing, bed count and JKM licence numbers are not listed on the operator website — ask for these when you call or visit the facility.
address: 17-1, Jalan Dato Haji Harun, Taman Taynton View, 56000 Cheras, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Elderly Gardens Care Centre KG CHERAS BARU
editorial_summary:
Elderly Gardens Care Centre's Kampung Cheras Baru branch is at 21, Jalan 34B, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Cheras. The operator's main outlet is at Jalan Awan Cina, Taman Yarl, off Old Klang Road, with the Cheras Baru house listed as a second site. Elderly Gardens Care Centre (M) Sdn Bhd is registered as a Malaysian company (registration 202301003844 / 1497763-K) and the operator states the brand has more than twelve years of operating history in long-term and rehabilitation care.

The Taman Yarl site publishes a fairly comprehensive service list which the Cheras Baru branch shares: bathing, dressing, toileting and feeding for dependent residents, incontinence and continence care, medication reminders, escort to outpatient appointments, fall-risk supervision, physiotherapy, dementia and Alzheimer's care, bedridden and tube-feeding care, and wound care. Air-conditioned rooms are available. The operator also offers home-based packages from one hour up to 24/7, which is unusual - confirm whether the Cheras Baru house is a residential nursing home, a base for home-care staff, or both. Total beds, current fees, RN ratio overnight, JKM Act 506 licence number and halal status are not published.

This branch suits families in the Kg Cheras Baru / Taman Bukit Mewah / Taman Sri Bahtera catchment who want a Chinese-Malaysian-run nursing home with a clear scope around dementia and tube-feeding care, and is also worth a call from families who want a hybrid arrangement (home care now, residential later). Google reviews are mixed (3.7 average, small sample), which is reason to visit in person rather than rely on the rating. Specifically ask: how many beds at the Cheras Baru house, how many staff on duty overnight, whether a Registered Nurse is rostered or only carers, monthly fees by dependency level (mobile vs bedridden vs tube-fed), what is included vs charged extra, and a sight of the JKM Act 506 certificate.
address: 21, Jalan 34b, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Cheras, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan En Yuan PLT ( Kg Cheras Baru)
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan En Yuan PLT is at 5 / 5A, Jalan 28, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Kuala Lumpur, in the densely-packed Cheras Baru terrace-house belt that hosts a cluster of small Chinese-run nursing homes. The PLT suffix on the operator name indicates the business is registered as a Perkongsian Liabiliti Terhad (limited-liability partnership) under SSM. Listings classify it variously as an assisted-living facility and a nursing home, and its Mandarin name (En Yuan / 安养, broadly "peaceful care") is consistent with a Chinese-speaking operator.

Verified information is limited - there is no operator website. Independent listings give the address and a phone contact, and Google reviews show a 4.2 average across about 20 reviews, which is a more useful sample size than several of its neighbours. The home does not publish capacity, room types, fees, staffing model or JKM Act 506 licence details. Foursquare and other directories list it as assisted-living rather than skilled nursing, but local Cheras Baru operators usually mix dependency levels in practice; this needs to be checked directly.

This home is likely to suit families looking for a Chinese-speaking, neighbourhood-scale residential placement in the Cheras Baru / Taman Bukit Mewah area at mid-market price points. Before committing, call and ask: registered name and JKM Act 506 licence number; total beds and current vacancies; whether a Registered Nurse is on duty (and overnight specifically) or only PRA-trained carers; what dependency levels they accept (mobile only, bedridden, NG/PEG tube, tracheostomy, dementia with wandering); monthly fees by room type and what is excluded; and how medical issues are handled - named GP, nearest hospital used, ambulance arrangement. A walk-in visit is the only reliable way to assess this kind of small operator.
address: 5A, Jalan 28, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Happy Family Nursing Home (Cheras) (Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Taman Sri Bahtera)
editorial_summary:
Happy Family Nursing Home (Cheras), registered as Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Taman Sri Bahtera, sits at 49, Jalan Sekuci, Taman Sri Bahtera, 56100 Kuala Lumpur, on the eastern Cheras side near Taman Connaught and Taman Bukit Mewah. The Cheras outlet is one of several branches under the Happy Family Nursing Home operator, which also runs centres in Taman Kepong (335, Jalan Kuang Gunung), Taman Desa Jaya (Kepong) and Bandar Sungai Long (Kajang). The group operates a single contact number (019-777 7328) across branches and uses a shared corporate website at happyfamilynursinghome.com.my.

As a multi-branch operator, Happy Family is structured as a chain of converted-house nursing homes rather than a single purpose-built facility - typical for the Klang Valley mid-market. Public information about the Cheras outlet specifically is sparse: capacity, room mix, RN cover, dementia and tube-feeding scope, halal status, and JKM Act 506 licence number are not on the website. The Google profile for the Sri Bahtera branch shows only two reviews with a 3.0 average, which is too small to draw conclusions from. The chain pattern itself is a useful signal - operators running four or more outlets tend to have a more standardised admission process and clearer fee schedule than one-off houses.

This home is likely to suit families in Taman Sri Bahtera, Cheras Baru and Taman Connaught who want a Chinese-Malaysian-run residential placement at mid-market pricing, with the option to move a relative between the operator's branches if catchment changes. Before committing, ask the operator directly: which branch holds the JKM Act 506 licence and the licence number; bed capacity and current vacancy at the Sri Bahtera house specifically; whether a Registered Nurse covers nights or only carers; dependency levels accepted (bedridden, NG/PEG, dementia with wandering); monthly fees with itemised inclusions; and arrangements for medical review and hospital transfer. Visit the actual Cheras branch rather than the marketing photos on the website, which are likely a composite of all outlets.
address: 49, Jalan Sekuci, Taman Sri Bahtera, 56100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Mintygreen Care Suites - Home Care Nursing Cheras KL
editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Suites (Cheras) is the Kuala Lumpur branch of the Mintygreen Wellness group, located in Phoenix Business Park along Jalan Cheras. Reviewers describe the environment as feeling more like a hotel than a traditional nursing home — a distinction that sets this branch apart from the group's suburban locations. The Cheras branch caters primarily to KL-based families seeking nursing care with a premium ambience.

**Care services**
- Residential nursing care
- Daily activities: exercise, gardening, mahjong, creative programmes
- Personal care and hygiene assistance
- Nutritious meals
- Caregiver support

**Staffing & support**
- Caring nurses confirmed in Maps reviews
- Clean environment and activities highlighted

**Capacity & pricing**
- Pricing not published on operator website — request a written quote during your visit

**License & verification**
- To be verified — confirm JKM or MOH registration on visit.

**What to ask on visit**
- Is this Cheras branch registered with JKM?
- What is the monthly fee structure and what is included?
- What nurse-to-resident ratio is maintained, particularly at night?
- Are doctor visits arranged and at what frequency?
- How are families notified of incidents or changes in condition?
address: 100, Jln Cheras, Phoenix Business Park, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Oasis Nursing Home (Klang)
editorial_summary:
Oasis Nursing Home (安康之家) is located at No. 50, Jalan Sungai Kapar Indah 5E, Taman Sungai Kapar Indah, in the Kapar district of Klang — a suburban residential area north of Klang town proper. The facility is distinctive in that its founder and doctor-in-charge is Dr. Wendy Oon, a named physician whose involvement is documented on the facility's own website. The care team listed includes a head of nursing (Shaziq), an assistant head nurse (Siti Ramlah), and an in-house physiotherapist (Nur Ain) — a staff configuration that goes beyond what most small nursing homes publish, and indicates genuine clinical structure rather than caregiver-only operations. The facility has a Wix-hosted website and a Facebook page, and carries a perfect 5.0 Google rating from 8 reviews.

Oasis lists its care scope as: long-term care, short-term stays, post-hospitalization, stroke rehabilitation, post-operation, palliative care, and dementia care. A testimonial cited on the website describes improvement in a resident's bedsore from grade 4 to grade 1 — a clinically meaningful outcome that, if verified, suggests nursing wound care competency. The Chinese-language name (安康之家) translates literally as 'home of health and tranquility'. The care team includes both Chinese and Malay staff names, indicating a mixed-language environment. Operating hours listed are Sunday through Friday, 8am–6pm, which refers to visiting or administrative hours; residential care is 24-hour.

Bed count is not disclosed. Kapar is roughly 15–20 minutes from Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah in Klang town, the main government hospital for the area, and 10 minutes from the Kapar town centre. With pricing not published, families should contact via +60 12-270 3166 or oasisnursingcare@gmail.com. Ask about the frequency of Dr. Oon's on-site presence versus on-call arrangement, what physiotherapy sessions look like in practice, and whether palliative care extends to end-of-life support or is primarily post-acute.
address: NO 50, Jalan Sungai Kapar Indah 5e, Taman Sungai Kapar Indah, 42200 Kapar, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Acacia by Pacific Senior Living
editorial_summary:
Acacia by Pacific Senior Living is a purpose-built senior living residence in Klang, Selangor, operated by Pacific Senior Living Sdn Bhd, which is part of the Seattle-headquartered Columbia Pacific Management Group. The property was converted from the former Merrida Hotel and officially launched in November 2023. It occupies a resort-like setting in Kawasan 17, Klang, with reasonably convenient access to Kuala Lumpur. Acacia positions itself firmly at the premium end of the market, targeting independent and assisted-living residents aged 60 and above — it is not a conventional nursing home and does not take on acute medical cases.

The residence has 136 private rooms across three tiers — Superior (312 sq ft), Executive, and Premier (up to 592 sq ft) — each with an ensuite bathroom, smart TV, writing desk, and mini fridge. Assisted living units start from RM 9,200 per person per month; independent living from RM 8,500. Wellness Associates are on-site 24/7 for non-clinical support, and healthcare professional visits are scheduled rather than resident. Columbia Asia serves as the strategic healthcare partner under a memorandum of understanding signed in August 2023. On-site amenities include a senior-friendly swimming pool with jacuzzi, gymnasium, mini theatre, karaoke room, games area, F&B restaurant, and hobby workshop. The Silver Club day programme (7 am–7 pm daily) is open to non-residents.

Acacia is the right fit for relatively independent seniors or early assisted-living needs; families expecting full nursing-home-level clinical care should look elsewhere. The pricing is among the highest in Selangor and does not appear to include JKM subsidy eligibility given its private premium model. Confirm precisely what triggers an escalation from Wellness Associate support to nursing-level care, what Columbia Asia's role actually covers day-to-day (scheduled visits, not full-time on-site), and what additional fees apply for medication management, physiotherapy sessions, or transport to hospital.
address: 18A, Lebuh Enggang, Off, Persiaran Sultan, Kawasan 17, 41050 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Athena Care Centre (Klang)
editorial_summary:
Athena Care Centre is pinned to Taman Sri Andalas, a township in the southern Klang area with reasonable proximity to Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah — the main public hospital for Klang — roughly 8–10 km away. The home is contactable via the Google Maps listing and carries 5-star reviews from a small reviewer base. The home does not maintain a published website or Facebook page, so most service detail is best gathered on a call or visit.

Care types offered, JKM licence number, capacity, and on-duty nursing capability are best confirmed directly with the home. Taman Sri Andalas is a mixed residential-commercial area that supports a number of small community nursing homes, often operating from converted terrace or shop premises. The proximity to HTAR is a practical asset for families anticipating hospital escalations.

Families should call the listed number to confirm the home is currently accepting residents, establish care type and licence status, and arrange a site visit before considering placement. Useful questions on the call: the JKM licence number, the registered-nurse cover overnight, the visiting-doctor frequency, the monthly fee, and what the protocol is for medical emergencies.
address: 9, Jalan Sri Siantan 37, Taman Sri Andalas, 41200 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: RS Global Home Care ( Only For Muslim )
editorial_summary:
RS Global Home Care in Kampung Cheras Baru, Kuala Lumpur, is a residential nursing home serving Muslim residents exclusively, at 1C, Jalan 2, 56100 KL. The home emphasises Islamic programming alongside standard residential care. Phone: +60 11-3586 0025.

Google Maps reviewers consistently highlight Islamic care practices as a key differentiator: residents receive assistance with daily prayers, participate in Quran recitation sessions, and are served halal meals. The facility has over 200 photos documented on Google Maps, reflecting active engagement by staff and families. Specific capacity and pricing details are not published online.

Eighteen reviews average 4.7 stars. Malay-language reviewers describe clean rooms, well-prepared halal meals and staff who are attentive to religious obligations. For Muslim families seeking a values-aligned care environment with structured religious programming, this is one of the few facilities in the Cheras area explicitly committed to this. Confirm bed availability, fees and JKM licence directly by phone.
address: 1 C, Jalan 2, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Penyayang
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Penyayang is listed in Taman Serdang Raya, Seri Kembangan — a well-connected township southeast of Kuala Lumpur with reasonable access to Columbia Asia Hospital Cheras and several private medical clinics. The Google listing categorises it under aged care. The home is contactable on +60 12-203 9932.

The home keeps a low online profile and does not maintain a published website or active social media page, so most service detail is best gathered directly. Bed count, staffing, the scope of medical care, and JKM registration are best confirmed on a call. Taman Serdang Raya is a mature residential township with multiple smaller community-scale care homes catering to local families.

Families considering this centre should call ahead to establish current admission availability and the level of care offered. Useful questions: bed count, registered-nurse cover, doctor-visit frequency, dependency levels accepted (ambulant, partial-care, bedridden), monthly fees, and the JKM licence number. The Serdang location is a practical asset for families anticipating hospital escalations to UPM Teaching Hospital or Columbia Asia.
address: Taman Serdang Raya, 47120 Seri Kembangan, Selangor, Malaysia

title: GoldLeaf Villa Care Centre
editorial_summary:
GoldLeaf Villa Care Centre is listed at Taman Orkid Desa, Cheras, with phone enquiries on 010-238 3848. The home does not appear on any operator website, sector directory (AGECOPE, hati.my, ielder.asia, Care Concierge top-10 lists), or significant news coverage that we could verify, and the Google profile carries no public reviews or rating at the time of writing. It is registered as a nursing home ("Care Centre" in the name suggests a residential elder-care setup rather than a daycare), and Taman Orkid Desa is a residential terrace-house neighbourhood off Jalan Cheras, consistent with a converted-house operator.

Until more information is published or verified by visit, families should treat this as a home that has to be assessed entirely by phone call and site visit. Ask: registered operator name and JKM (Act 506) licence number; total beds and current vacancy; staff numbers on duty day and night, and whether a Registered Nurse is rostered overnight or only carers; what dependency levels are accepted (mobile, bedridden, NG/PEG tube, dementia with wandering, palliative); monthly fees and what is included vs charged extra; languages spoken; halal or non-halal kitchen; named doctor and hospital used for transfers; and visiting hours.
address: 1, Jalan 16/142, Taman Orkid Desa, 56000 Cheras, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Ln Home Care Center
editorial_summary:
LN HOME CARE CENTER is an elderly care facility in Taman Teluk Pulai, Selangor. The current JKM or MOH registration status should be confirmed on visit.

Google reviews stand at 5★ across 3 reviews — too few to identify recurring trends.

Pricing is not published — contact for a quote. Visiting hours are not published — confirm when booking a viewing. The operator does not maintain a website at the time of writing; contact details are in the sidebar.

**What to ask on visit:**
- What is the monthly fee, and what is included (meals, diapers, laundry, medication)?
- What is the resident-to-staff ratio overnight, and is a registered nurse on duty?
- What clinical conditions are accepted — dementia, bedridden, post-stroke, palliative?
- What is the protocol for medical emergencies and hospital transfers?
- What languages do staff speak (BM, English, Mandarin, Tamil)?
- Are religious or dietary preferences (halal, vegetarian, festival observances) accommodated?
address: Bunga ros Kawasan 6, Taman Teluk Pulai, 41100 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Suria Care Home / Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas
editorial_summary:
Suria Care Home / Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas (Mandarin: 舒利亚乐龄休养中心) is listed in Taman City, on the Jinjang / Kepong side of Kuala Lumpur (not Cheras, despite being in the KL batch), with a landline contact on 03-6241 9132. The trilingual name - Bahasa Malaysia ("Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas"), English and Mandarin - is unusual and suggests an operator catering to mixed Malaysian families rather than a single language community. Listings classify it as aged care. Note that there are at least two unrelated "Suria" elder-care brands in the Klang Valley (one in PJ, one in Titiwangsa), so families should be careful to confirm they are dialling the Taman City address specifically.

No operator website, social-media page, sector-directory listing or Google reviews could be verified for this address, and capacity, fees, services, JKM Act 506 licence, staffing and religion are all unpublished. Treat this as a home to assess entirely by phone and site visit. Ask: registered operator name and JKM Act 506 licence number; total beds and current vacancy; RN cover overnight vs carer-only; what dependency levels are accepted (mobile, bedridden, NG/PEG, dementia, palliative); monthly fees by room type and inclusions; halal or non-halal kitchen; languages spoken by carers; and how medical issues and hospital transfers are handled.
address: 133A, JALAN MATANG 3, Jln Kuching, Taman City, 51200 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Persatuan Kebajikan Warga Emas Chan Kl
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Persatuan Kebajikan Warga Emas Chan KL — also known informally as 安安老人院 (An An Old Folks Home) — is a welfare-oriented elderly care centre in the Pandan Indah / Ampang Jaya area of Selangor, operated by a Chinese community welfare association bearing the Chan family name. The Malay listing includes 'KL' in its title despite being situated in Selangor, reflecting its proximity to the Ampang border with Kuala Lumpur. It maintains a Facebook presence (pusatjagaanwargaemaschan) and is navigable via Waze. The Google listing categorises it as a 'social services organisation' rather than a formal nursing home — a meaningful distinction for families looking for nursing-grade care.

As a welfare association home, An An is likely structured around community charity rather than commercial care delivery — meaning it may accept subsidised or low-income referrals alongside self-paying residents. The 'languages: 30' data point in the source file appears to be a data entry error rather than a languages field. Bed count, the care dependency levels managed, and JKM registration status are best confirmed directly with the home.

Families should clarify upfront whether the home is JKM-registered as a residential care facility, what nursing qualifications staff hold, and what happens when a resident's medical needs escalate beyond what the home can manage. If it is primarily a social-welfare placement rather than a nursing home, it may not be suited to residents who need wound care, PEG feeding, or dementia-specific programmes. A short visit in person is the most reliable way to assess the physical environment and staffing model.
address: Kampung Tasek Permai, 43000 Ampang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat jagaan orang tua cahaya maju
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Orang Tua Cahaya Maju is a welfare-based elderly care home operated by Persatuan Kebajikan Cahaya Maju (Cahaya Maju Welfare Association) at 22 Jalan Melati 1, Taman Seri Melati, 68100 Batu Caves, Selangor. Incorporated in January 2019, the centre is classified on its registration as providing residential care and is co-listed as a shelter catering to elderly individuals aged 40 and above, including those from low-income or vulnerable backgrounds. It operates daily from approximately 08:45 to 19:00 and accepts both monetary and in-kind donations, reflecting its non-profit community welfare model.

The organisation's charitable mandate centres on providing shelter, food, basic medical access, and a degree of social support to seniors who might otherwise lack these necessities. Google Maps classifies the listing as a homeless shelter alongside an elderly care function, indicating the home serves a mixed population of older adults in need rather than functioning as a clinical nursing home. The facility's capacity and staffing levels are not published, and no nursing or medical programming details are available through public sources.

Families seeking placement for a dependent elderly relative with significant clinical needs should approach this centre with an understanding that it operates as a welfare home rather than a licensed nursing facility. Ask directly about what level of personal and nursing care is provided, whether any medically trained staff are on site, and how medical emergencies are handled. The Batu Caves location provides reasonable access to Selayang Hospital (approximately 8 kilometres). Confirm current JKM registration status before placing a resident here.
address: 22, Jalan Melati 1, Taman Seri Melati, 68100 Batu Caves, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Orang Tua Damai
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Orang Tua Damai is a small private elderly care home at No. 56, Jalan Arasakesari 2, Taman Taynton, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, in the Taman Taynton View neighbourhood of Cheras. The phone number on file is +60 10-773 1193, with a secondary contact-page listing of 016-256 8XXX. There is no website and no public Facebook page; nearest public transport is Taman Mutiara MRT/LRT, about a 14-minute walk. Online reviews are minimal (a single Google review with a low rating).

Beyond confirming the address and that it operates as an elderly care facility in a residential terrace house, almost no operational detail is published online. Care types, bed count, fee structure, dementia or palliative capability, halal/non-halal status, and JKM registration status all need to be confirmed directly with the operator.

If you are considering this home, call ahead and ask: how many residents and how many staff on each shift; is there a registered nurse on duty day and night, or only caregivers; is the centre registered with JKM under Act 506; what is the monthly fee for shared vs single rooms and what does it include (diapers, milk feeds, laundry, GP visits); can they manage tube feeds, catheters, wound care, oxygen, and dementia behaviours; what is the visiting policy; and can the family inspect the rooms, kitchen, and bathrooms before committing.
address: No. 56, Jalan Arasakesari 2, Taman Taynton View, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Merry
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Merry is a small private elderly care home at 53, Jalan Belinggai, Kepong Baru, 52100 Kuala Lumpur, on the residential side of Kepong Baru. Telephone is +60 3-6277 3595. The home has no website and no public Facebook page, and appears in Malaysian directory listings (worldorgs.com) but not on the larger curated nursing home directories such as hati.my or ielder.asia. Google reviews are positive but very limited in number (rating around 4.7 from only 3 reviewers), so the sample is too small to draw firm conclusions.

No operational detail is published online: care intensity (whether the home accepts bedbound residents, tube feeds, wound care, dementia), staffing model (RN vs caregiver ratio), bed count, fee tiers, halal status, and JKM/Act 506 registration are all unconfirmed. The Kepong Baru area has multiple small homes operating from converted terrace houses, and based on the address format this is most likely the same model.

For families shortlisting Merry, the practical step is a site visit and a structured phone call. Ask for the JKM registration number and date; total beds and current occupancy; whether a registered nurse is on duty overnight or only caregivers; monthly fee and what it includes (diapers, milk feeds, GP, transport to clinic); ability to manage NG/PEG feeds, urinary catheters, oxygen, and dementia wandering; menu and halal certification if relevant; and visiting hours and any restrictions.
address: 53, Jalan Belinggai, Kepong Baru, 52100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Kangiten
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Kangiten (PJWEK) operates from 29, Jalan SP1 in Taman Selayang Jaya, Batu Caves — a residential estate north of Kuala Lumpur that sits within easy reach of Hospital Selayang, one of the country's larger public tertiary centres. The facility markets itself explicitly as both a nursing home and a physiotherapy centre, which is a meaningful distinction: rather than treating physio as a bolt-on, it appears to be a core service pillar. The team description references doctors, nurses, and physiotherapists on staff, though headcounts and shift arrangements are not publicly stated.

Kangiten's clinical capability list is more detailed than most facilities at this level. Documented nursing procedures include wound dressing, bed-sore management, catheter and tracheostomy care, NGT insertion, insulin administration, and medication monitoring — a range that suggests the centre is equipped to accept residents with moderate-to-high dependency. On the rehabilitation side, the facility's physiotherapists reportedly cover home visits across 14 service zones, and treat conditions including stroke, Parkinson's, arthritis, cerebral palsy, joint replacements, and spinal cord injuries. Day care and short-term stay options appear to exist alongside long-term residential places.

Pricing and total bed count are not published, so families should request these directly. The home-visit physiotherapy arm may operate somewhat independently of the residential unit — worth clarifying whether residents automatically access in-house physio or whether this is separately charged. Confirming the JKM licence number and the on-site medical cover arrangement (particularly overnight) are standard due-diligence steps before admission. The Batu Caves location has good road access but limited public transport, which matters for families visiting by bus or LRT.
address: 29, Jalan SP1, Taman Selayang Jaya, 68100 Batu Caves, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Nurul Jannah
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Nurul Jannah is a private elderly care centre established in 2018 and based at Jalan AU 5C/12 in Ampang Jaya, Selangor, on the KL border near Keramat and Wangsa Maju. It was founded with an explicitly Muslim-women-centred mission — the centre's stated tagline translates as "to care for, treat, educate, and show love to Muslim elderly women." This makes it a specialist placement for Malay-Muslim families seeking a faith-aligned residential setting for female relatives. The centre operates 24 hours, seven days a week, and is classified in directories as a home health care service.

The facility offers both monthly and daily placement, giving families flexibility for respite or long-term arrangements. Its website and Google Sites presence indicate that services include light medical treatment for unwell residents, religious classes for interested residents, and comprehensive emotional support. The dual contact model — separate numbers for male (En. Iqbal) and female (Pn. Yus) staff liaisons — reflects cultural sensitivity in communications. The Google Maps rating is 4.7 from seven reviews, suggesting consistent positive feedback from families who have used the service. Specific bed count, room types, and pricing are not publicly stated.

Key questions to raise on enquiry include current capacity and whether there is a waitlist, the level of nursing care available for residents with mobility limitations or medical devices, and how acute medical events are handled overnight. Given the female-only and Muslim-focused character, families should also confirm whether male caregivers are present and under what circumstances. The Jalan AU 5C/12 address in Ampang Jaya places it reasonably close to Hospital Ampang for emergencies.
address: 56, Jalan AU 5c/12, 54200 Ampang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua SRA
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua SRA was founded on 1 January 2020 by Sharil Hussein, a former Ministry of Health civil servant, as a protective shelter for elderly people discharged or abandoned from hospitals whose families cannot provide care. The home is located in Taman Malawati, Kuala Selangor — a suburban setting roughly an hour from central Kuala Lumpur. Residents are drawn predominantly from referrals through the hospital system rather than private placements, reflecting the founder's healthcare background and the NGO's welfare-first mandate. The facility is operated under the Selangor Relief Agency (SRA) umbrella.

As of early 2025, the home was caring for 34 elderly and frail residents. Funding has historically come from Selangor Zakat Board support, and the home has periodically run public appeals for donations to maintain its rented bungalow. Local welfare partners (including Mayda) have stepped in at various points to support premises and operating costs. The resident profile appears to include bedridden and uzur (severely debilitated) individuals, suggesting the home handles higher-dependency cases beyond basic assisted living.

The home runs a welfare-first model, so families considering a placement here should call ahead on +60 19-879 1926 to confirm current admission criteria, the latest fee structure for non-asnaf residents, and the current operating premises. Useful questions: the care team composition, whether nursing staff are available overnight, and how the home manages medical escalations given the Kuala Selangor location. SRA also welcomes volunteer and donor enquiries through the same number.
address: 771, Jalan Daeng Celak, Off, Jalan Klinik, Taman Malawati, 45000 Kuala Selangor, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Harian Warga Tua Cheras Baru
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Harian Warga Tua Cheras Baru is a community day care centre for older adults located at Lot 3716, Jalan 17, Off Jalan Kuari, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Kuala Lumpur. Unlike a residential nursing home, this is a daytime-only service: opening hours are Monday to Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, closed on weekends. The contact line is +60 3-4297 0316 (with a published mobile 012-643 7XXX). It is registered as a non-profit (Persatuan/association) rather than a commercial operator, and has been running long enough to host structured community programmes with university and teacher-training groups.

Operationally, the centre runs daytime activities for ambulant seniors rather than skilled nursing. Verified programmes documented in community-service reports include light aerobics, sports and recreation, and intergenerational activities run with visiting trainee teachers (one published report involved 63 student volunteers). The model fits seniors who live with family but need structured daytime supervision, social interaction, and basic supervised activity, rather than residents needing 24-hour nursing or post-stroke rehab.

This centre is most useful for KL families in the Cheras / Setapak / Wangsa Maju catchment whose elderly relative is mobile and continent but at risk of isolation, falls, or loneliness when the family is at work. Before enrolling, ask: daily/monthly fee and whether transport is provided; staff-to-senior ratio and whether anyone with nursing training is on site; whether the centre accepts mild dementia (and how they handle wandering); meal arrangements and halal status; what happens medically if a senior falls or becomes unwell during the day; and whether the centre is registered with JKM as a Pusat Jagaan Harian under Act 506.
address: Lot 3716, Jalan 17, Off Jalan Kuari, Cheras Baru, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Pusat Penjagaan Warga Emas Taman Kok Lian
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Taman Kok Lian is a small private elderly care home at No. C12A, Jalan Padang Rengas, Taman Kok Lian, 51200 Kuala Lumpur, in the Jalan Ipoh / Sentul area of north KL. The business is registered with SSM (registration number 002374615-U), and has a Facebook presence and a (now sparse) standalone website. The phone number on file is +60 11-5116 4919. Google ratings are positive but the review count is low (5/5 from 2 reviews), so the public signal is weak.

Directory entries position the centre as a residential nursing home / old folks home offering elderly residential care and rehabilitation, but no detailed information is published about staffing model, registered nurse coverage, bed count, fee structure, halal status, or specific clinical capabilities (tube feeds, catheter care, dementia behaviour management, palliative). The SSM registration confirms it is a legitimate registered business, but does not by itself confirm JKM registration under Act 506, which is the relevant licence for a care home.

If you are considering Taman Kok Lian, ask the operator directly for the JKM registration number and certificate; total beds and current occupancy; staffing pattern day and night (caregivers vs registered nurses); monthly fee and inclusions; whether they accept bedbound residents, NG/PEG feeds, urinary catheters, oxygen and dementia; halal kitchen status; visiting hours; and the language(s) used by staff with residents.
address: No.C12A, Jalan Padang Rengas, Taman Kok Lian, 51200 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Husna Arrashid
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Husna Arrashid is a Muslim-oriented private nursing home at No. 14B, Lorong Kijang, Kampung Kelang Gates Baharu, 53300 Kuala Lumpur (Setapak / Wangsa Maju area). The centre operates under an Islamic framework, has its own website (husnaarrashid.com), and was first registered with the Selangor Social Welfare Department in December 2016 after several years of operational development. The phone number on file is +60 3-4108 1811. Google ratings are mixed, averaging around 3.6 over 18 reviews, which is enough volume to take seriously rather than dismiss.

Verified operational details from the home's own website: capacity is approximately 28 residents; monthly fees are stated as RM1,200 to RM1,800; staff perform standard personal-care work including bathing, diaper changes, feeding, medication administration, and supervised exercise; the home runs an Islamic schedule, with halal meals and Iftar provision during Ramadan; the operating language is Malay. The home explicitly does not solicit public donations and funds itself from fees. Staff retention is highlighted, with some caregivers in post for three or more years, and the home encourages clinical staff to pursue further qualifications.

Visiting policy is restrictive and worth flagging: only family members are admitted, by appointment, and walk-in visits from non-relatives are not accepted. Families are also expected to provide personal items and to handle medical transport to clinic/hospital appointments. One credible online comment (cited in directory listings) alleges that after a change of ownership the centre's JKM registration and cleanliness standards were not maintained, so verify the current JKM certificate and visit the premises in person. Useful questions: current JKM registration number and date; presence of a registered nurse overnight (vs caregivers only); ability to manage NG/PEG feeds, catheters, wound care, oxygen, and dementia; transfer arrangements when a resident becomes acutely unwell.
address: No 14B, Lorong Kijang, Kampung Kelang Gates Baharu, 53300 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Elderly Welfare Association Chan Kuala Lumpur
editorial_summary:
Elderly Welfare Association Chan Kuala Lumpur (Persatuan Kebajikan Warga Emas Chan Kuala Lumpur) is a registered non-profit nursing home at 534, Lorong Ayer Lombong, off Jalan Air Panas, Setapak, 53200 Kuala Lumpur. It is listed on hati.my, the established Malaysian charity directory, which is the strongest public verification this batch produced. The contact number on file is +60 16-220 6247. Google rating is 4 out of 5 from 3 reviews — small sample, but consistent with a low-profile charity home rather than a commercial operator.

Verified details from the hati.my listing: the home cares for 25 elderly residents aged 60 to 90; it has been operating for around eight years (as of the listing); it primarily accepts seniors with mobility impairment or no family support; some residents pay a nominal fee where families are able, while those without family stay free of charge. Monthly operating costs are around RM16,000, including roughly RM3,000 in rent, and the home depends heavily on public donations, with bank details published (Alliance Bank, account in the name of Pusat Penjagaan Warga Emas Chan). It is not run as a profit-making business and should not be evaluated on the same fee-and-amenities basis as commercial homes.

This home suits low-income KL families needing a place for an elderly relative who is essentially homeless, mobility-impaired, or without family support, rather than families seeking high-acuity nursing for tube-fed or post-stroke residents. Practical questions before placement: confirm JKM registration under Act 506; ask about clinical limits (does the home accept bedbound, NG-fed, oxygen-dependent, or dementia-wandering residents, or refer them out); staffing pattern (caregivers only vs any registered nurse coverage); meal and religious arrangements; whether families are expected to contribute consumables (diapers, milk feeds); and how acute medical events are handled.
address: 534, Lorong Ayer Lombong, Taman Ayer Panas, 53200 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Cinta
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Cinta (Chinese: 愛心樂齡休養中心) is a small Chinese-oriented elderly care centre in the Sentul area of Kuala Lumpur, near Jalan Sentul Pasar (51100). The phone on file is +60 19-318 9406. Google rating is 4.3 from 10 reviews, which is a reasonable signal at this size. There is no public website, no active Facebook page, and no listing on the larger curated nursing home directories (hati.my, ielder.asia, agecope.org) that we could verify.

The Chinese name (樂齡休養中心, "senior rest centre") and the bilingual signage suggest the home primarily serves Chinese-speaking families, with Mandarin/Cantonese/Hokkien likely the everyday language inside the home, and Chinese-style meals. Beyond that, no operational detail is published: bed count, staff model (registered nurse vs caregivers), fee tiers, clinical capability for tube feeds, catheters, oxygen and dementia, and JKM Act 506 registration are all unconfirmed.

For families considering Cinta, the useful questions are practical and specific. Ask for the JKM registration number and date; total beds and current occupancy; whether any registered nurse is on duty (and at what times); the monthly fee and what it includes; ability to manage bedbound residents, NG/PEG feeds, urinary catheters, oxygen, dementia behaviours; meal style (Chinese vegetarian / non-vegetarian / mixed) and any cultural rituals supported; visiting hours; and the language used between staff and residents (especially relevant if the resident speaks Mandarin only or a specific dialect).
address: 2030-1A, Bangunan Dato Haji Suhaimi, Jalan Sentul Pasar Dalam, Sentul, 51100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Noble Care Nursing Home Klang Selangor
editorial_summary:
Noble Care Nursing Home Klang occupies a shophouse unit at 46-B, Lorong Baling, Kawasan 18, in the older industrial-residential fringe of Klang town. Established in 2012 according to the operator's website, it is one branch of a multi-site Noble Care group operating across the Klang Valley. The Klang branch is positioned as a full nursing home handling complex care, not just general aged accommodation, with round-the-clock nursing coverage and what the website describes as personalized health and wellness programmes within a safe, comfortable living environment.

The Noble Care website lists a notable range of clinical procedures: NG tube changing, Foley's catheter care, colostomy bag management, bedsore dressing, tracheostomy care, post-operative care, and cerebral palsy care — a breadth that indicates nursing staff are present and trained for dependency management beyond basic assistance. Dementia care and physiotherapy are also listed. Respite and palliative care are offered as distinct service categories. The shophouse format typical of Kawasan 18 means the physical footprint will be modest; families should inspect room sizes and outdoor access during any site visit. Bed count is not publicly stated.

Klang's Kawasan 18 is close to Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah (HTAR) — the main government referral hospital for the district — which is practical for specialist follow-ups. Pricing is not disclosed online; contact is via +60 16-278 6993. Ask about actual nurse-to-resident ratios, licence number, and whether any of the listed clinical procedures require prior arrangement or are routinely available on-site. The Google rating of 4.6 from 11 reviews is modest but positive.
address: 46-B, Lorong Baling, Kawasan 18, 41400 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Rumah Orang Tua Wesley
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Rumah Orang Tua Wesley is the residential care arm of the Wesley Outreach Society, which operates under Wesley Methodist Church. The home sits at No. 8, Jalan Meru, off Lorong Jugra, Jalan Klang Lama — an older residential road in the Kuala Lumpur–Selangor border corridor. Although the Google Maps record places it in Selangor, the address is in the Jalan Klang Lama corridor, and the administrator contact uses a 03- Kuala Lumpur prefix. The facility is a mission-driven, non-profit home rather than a commercial operator, which typically means tighter budgets but a strong ethos of pastoral care.

Admission criteria are clearly stated: applicants must be 60 or older, have no family able to care for them, and be both mobile and healthy at the time of entry. Required documentation includes a medical report with X-ray and blood test results. This is notably selective — the home is designed for independent-ish seniors who need companionship and basic support, not high-dependency nursing care. A supervisor (Mr Peter Lim) manages enquiries, and application forms are available at the church office or the home itself. Bed count and detailed services have not been publicly disclosed.

For families with a Methodist or broader Christian background seeking a modest, community-rooted option, this home is worth a direct enquiry. However, the mobile-and-healthy admission standard means it is unsuitable for residents with significant cognitive impairment, complex nursing needs, or limited mobility. Families should ask whether residents who decline in health can remain, or whether they would need to transfer to a higher-care facility — that transition plan matters a great deal for long-term peace of mind. Pricing is not published and likely involves a subsidy structure given the church affiliation.
address: 8, Jalan Meru, off, 31, 2 mile, Old Klang Rd, 58100 Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Attia Global Care Klang
editorial_summary:
Attia Global Care Klang is part of the Attia Nursing Care group, which the company states has been operating since 2011 and now runs six branches across the Klang Valley and Ipoh. The Klang branch is located at Jalan Raja Bot, 41400 Klang, placing it in the central commercial district of Klang town. The group's founding focus was on medically complex and rehabilitation cases — specifically stroke, coma, head injury, cancer, orthopaedic post-op, and amputations — and this clinical orientation distinguishes Attia from the majority of community nursing homes in the area.

The facility website indicates air-conditioned rooms with attached bathrooms, a nurse-calling system, elevator access, and a dedicated activity and physiotherapy room. Listed capabilities include speech therapy, occupational therapy, physiotherapy, and counselling — consistent with a rehabilitation-oriented care model. Assisted daily living and full-time residential care are also offered. The company describes its approach as 'medically skilled care in a respectful and self-sustaining community.' No public pricing is listed; the website advises calling directly for an assessment-based quotation, which suggests costs are individualised by dependency level. The Google rating of 4.7 from 77 reviews indicates consistent family satisfaction.

As a relatively newer branch in the Attia network, verify whether the Klang site carries the same clinical staffing depth as the more established PJ branches. Ask directly about doctor visit frequency, nurse-to-resident ratios on night shifts, and whether the physiotherapy room is staffed by a qualified physiotherapist daily or on a rotating schedule. Families with residents who need active stroke rehabilitation should also confirm whether the Klang branch runs formal rehabilitation programmes or provides maintenance physiotherapy only.
address: lot 83, 32-B, Lorong Raja Bot, Kawasan 18, 41400 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Golden Haven Nursing Care
editorial_summary:
Golden Haven Nursing Care (GHNC) is a small, deliberately boutique nursing home at 51, Jalan Bungor 3, Bandar Botanik, 41200 Klang — a modern, well-planned township in southern Klang with good road access and proximity to several private and public medical facilities. The home has its own website (goldenhavennursingcare.com.my) and markets itself as a personalised care environment. Contact is through Daniel Thomas at 017-583 7385, with a same-day response policy for enquiries.

GHNC operates at intentionally small scale: eight resident beds across four rooms (a 3-bed master, two 2-bed standard rooms, and one private single), plus day care services for up to four non-resident visitors per day. The home is staffed around the clock. Services explicitly described on the website include vital signs monitoring (blood pressure, temperature, pulse, oxygen), assistance with bathing, dressing, grooming and feeding, mobility support, and cognitive engagement activities. Pricing is not published and requires direct enquiry.

The small scale is both the facility's strength and its main constraint. Eight beds means genuinely high staff-to-resident ratios and a family-like setting, but it also means limited availability and potentially a waiting list. No specialised dementia unit or palliative care programme is described on the website. Families should ask about nursing qualifications of the on-site staff, what happens if a resident needs urgent hospital transfer, whether the single private room is available, and the monthly rate for their family member's specific care needs.
address: 51, Jalan Bungor 3, Bandar Botanik, 41200 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Genesis Life Care Centre Klang
editorial_summary:
Genesis Life Care Centre Klang is the founding branch of the Genesis Life Care group, established in 2018 at No. 3, Jalan Istana, Amverton Business Centre, 41000 Klang. The location puts it close to Tengku Ampuan Rahimah (TAR) Hospital — the main government referral hospital for Klang — a practical advantage for residents who need specialist follow-up or emergency access. The facility is listed as Ministry-approved and carries a Google rating of 4.3 from 71 reviews. Dr. Phang Sue Ling is identified as Chief of Operations, giving the centre a degree of medical leadership visibility that is uncommon among similarly sized private nursing homes.

The facility reports 120+ beds and a care team of 25+ professionals including a doctor who conducts weekly resident reviews, 24-hour nursing care, a senior therapist, and a dietician overseeing nutrition. Published care services include nursing care with personalised plans, dementia and memory care (described as psychologist-led), stroke rehabilitation with physiotherapy and occupational therapy, palliative and end-of-life care, post-operative recovery, and a senior daycare programme. Starting pricing is published at RM 2,500 per month — one of the more affordable entry points among Klang nursing homes with this breadth of clinical services. Halal dining is available.

With 120+ beds, Genesis Klang is a medium-to-large facility and its clinical scope is genuinely broad. Weekly doctor visits rather than daily presence is worth noting — ask whether a doctor is reachable within the facility on any given day or only scheduled once weekly. Clarify what 'psychologist-led' dementia care means operationally (is a psychologist physically on-site, or is the programme designed by a psychologist and delivered by caregivers?). The RM 2,500 starting price likely reflects the most basic shared-room arrangement; confirm what the full care package costs for residents with dementia or active rehabilitation needs.
address: No.3, Jalan Istana, Amverton Business Centre, 41000 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Happy Family Nursing Home (Taman Desa Jaya) (Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Desa Jaya)
editorial_summary:
Happy Family Nursing Home (Taman Desa Jaya), also branded Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Desa Jaya, is a branch of the Happy Family Nursing Home group operating in the Kepong area at 91, Jalan 43, Taman Desa Jaya, Kepong, 52100 Kuala Lumpur. Contact numbers on file are +60 19-777 7328 and +60 18-386 6228 (email: happyfamilynursinghome@gmail.com). The Happy Family group runs multiple branches across KL and Selangor (including Taman Kepong and Cheras locations) under a shared brand. As of writing, the group's main website (happyfamilynursinghome.com.my) is suspended, so detail has to be confirmed by phone.

Verified facts: Happy Family operates a multi-branch model rather than a single home, which means staff and operating procedures are likely shared across sites. The Taman Desa Jaya branch is positioned as a residential nursing home / pusat jagaan warga emas. Beyond the branch network and contact details, the published operational detail is thin: bed count, staffing pattern (registered nurses vs caregivers), fee tiers, halal kitchen, and specific clinical capabilities (tube feeds, catheters, oxygen, dementia, palliative) are not currently visible online and should be confirmed directly. Google review volume on this specific branch is very small (1 review).

This branch is likely to suit Kepong-area families who want a nursing home close to home and value being part of a small chain rather than a single-site operator. Before committing, ask: JKM Act 506 registration number specific to the Taman Desa Jaya branch (group registration is not enough); total beds and current occupancy on this site; staffing pattern day and night (RN vs caregivers); monthly fee and what is included (diapers, milk feeds, laundry, GP visits, transport); ability to take bedbound, NG/PEG, catheter, oxygen, and dementia residents; halal status of the kitchen; and visiting policy.
address: 91, Jalan 43, Taman Desa Jaya, 52100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Damai Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Damai Care Centre is a small private elderly care home in the Taman Mastiara / Jalan Ipoh corridor of Kuala Lumpur (north KL, between Sentul and Kepong). Contact is +60 16-516 1658. Google rating is strong (5/5) but the volume is modest at 11 reviews, which is enough to be a positive signal on resident experience but not enough to substitute for a site visit. There is no dedicated website and no public Facebook page surfacing in search; the larger 'Damai' brand in this area is the unrelated Damai Service Hospital on Jalan Ipoh, which families should not confuse with this care centre.

No operational detail is published online. Care intensity (ambulant only vs bedbound and tube-fed); staffing model (caregivers only vs registered nurse on duty day and night); bed count; fee structure; halal kitchen status; dementia, palliative and rehab capability; and JKM Act 506 registration are all unconfirmed. Reviews are positive on tone (cleanliness, attentiveness) but do not give specifics on clinical capability.

For families considering Damai, the consistent positive reviews are encouraging but warrant verification on the ground. Ask for the JKM registration number and certificate; total beds and current occupancy; staffing pattern across day, evening and night shifts; monthly fee and what it includes; ability to handle NG/PEG feeds, urinary catheters, oxygen, wound care, dementia wandering and end-of-life care; halal status; languages used by staff; and visiting hours. A short in-person tour to see the rooms, kitchen, bathrooms and how alert/oriented residents are will tell you more than the reviews.
address: 63, Jalan 14/18a, Taman Mastiara, 51200 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Mother's Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Mother's Nursing Home is a small private nursing home in Taman Jaya Baru, Kuala Lumpur. Phone is +60 11-1660 3636. There are no Google reviews on file, no website, no public Facebook page, and no entry in the major Malaysian nursing-home directories (hati.my, ielder.asia, agecope.org) at the time of writing. Effectively the only verified facts are the trading name, area, and contact number.

With no online footprint, every operational detail has to be confirmed by phone or site visit: care types (residential nursing vs lighter retirement-style care); JKM Act 506 registration; bed count; staffing pattern (registered nurse coverage vs caregivers only); fees; halal status; and clinical capability for bedbound residents, tube feeds, catheters, oxygen, dementia behaviours, and palliative care.

If you are considering Mother's, treat the visit as your primary diagnostic tool rather than online research. On the call, ask: the legal operator name and JKM registration number; total beds and current occupancy; staff-to-resident ratio day and night, and whether any registered nurse is on duty; monthly fee and what it includes (diapers, milk feeds, laundry, GP, transport); whether they accept bedbound, NG/PEG-fed, catheterised, oxygen-dependent or wandering dementia residents; meal arrangements and halal status; and visiting policy. On-site, look at the rooms, kitchen and bathrooms, and observe how alert and engaged the existing residents are.
address: 15, Jalan Jaya Baru 5, Taman Jaya Baru, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Happy Family Nursing Home (Jalan Ipoh) (Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Jalan Batu Batan)
editorial_summary:
Happy Family Nursing Home (Jalan Ipoh) operates at A5 and A6, Jalan Batu Batan, Taman Kok Lian, 51200 Kuala Lumpur, off Jalan Ipoh and a short drive from Sentul and Kepong.
