It is one branch of a small Klang Valley group trading as Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Happy Family, with related premises in Cheras (Taman Sri Bahtera), Kepong (Taman Desa Jaya) and Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang. The group markets itself as a residential nursing home for older adults with disability or chronic illness, contactable on 019-777 7328 / 010-244 2469.

Published material from the operator describes basic personal care (bathing, grooming, housekeeping), meal provision, and what it calls medical support using electronic medical equipment. It does not publish detailed clinical capabilities, registered-nurse coverage, doctor visit frequency, or bed numbers for the Jalan Batu Batan house specifically. The group's main website is currently offline (suspension notice). Rooms, prices and care types should therefore be confirmed by site visit. Google reviews are limited (3 ratings, 3.7 average) so there is little independent feedback to weigh.

The Jalan Ipoh location suits families anchored in the Sentul / Jalan Ipoh / Kepong belt who want a small, neighbourhood-scale nursing home rather than a large institutional one. Before committing, ask to see the JKM registration certificate, confirm whether a registered nurse is on duty overnight (not just a caregiver), check the actual ratio of staff to residents on each shift, ask which doctor visits and how often, and clarify how dementia behaviours, tube feeding, wound care and oxygen are handled. Get a written quotation that separates room rate from extras such as diapers, milk feeds, physiotherapy and hospital escort. Because this is one of several branches under the same brand, also confirm which entity holds the licence at this address.
address: A5 & A6, Jalan Batu Batan, Taman Kok Lian, 51200 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: St. Mary’s Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
St. Mary's Nursing Home is among the more established brands in the Klang Valley's private aged care sector, with five branches across KL and Petaling Jaya. The branch listed here at Taman Halimahton is at 2A, Jalan Halimahton, off the 3rd Mile Old Klang Road — a quiet residential pocket between the busy Old Klang Road and the Mid Valley/Bangsar corridor. The group was founded approximately 30 years ago by Madam Jagit Kour (known as Madam Sonia), who brought nursing training from Singapore and the United Kingdom. A second branch exists at 15C, Jalan Penghulu Mat, also in Taman Halimahton. The Google rating for the Taman Halimahton location is 4.4 from 10 reviews.

St. Mary's lists its services as elderly day programmes, private nursing care, assisted living, short-term residential care, and supplementary services tailored to individual needs. The group's emphasis on prayer sessions accommodating residents' own religious denominations, dietician-recommended meals, and daily wellness activities suggests an established routine rather than a bare-minimum operation. With five branches, the organisation has staffing depth and operational experience — reflected in the fact that the PJ branches reportedly operate with waiting lists. Bed count and pricing for the Taman Halimahton branch are not published.

The Old Klang Road location offers good access to Assunta Hospital, KPJ Damansara, and Pantai Hospital — all within 10–15 minutes. Families should clarify which specific St. Mary's entity operates this address (St. Mary's Aged Home Sdn Bhd and St. Mary's Elderly Home appear to be related but distinct registrations), and confirm the current contact person, as the group has expanded and management contacts have evolved. Ask about nurse qualification levels, how clinical escalation is handled at night, and whether the multi-faith prayer provision extends to the resident's specific religious background.
address: 2A, Jln Klang Lama, Taman Halimahton, 58000 Wilayah Persekutuan, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Little Sisters Of The Poor
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Little Sisters of the Poor, also known as St Francis Xavier's Home for the Elderly, is a Catholic charitable home for older adults at 3, Jalan 5/96 off Jalan Sekuci, Taman Sri Bahtera, Batu 5 Cheras, 56100 Kuala Lumpur. The Little Sisters of the Poor congregation arrived in Malaysia in 1965, ran homes on Klang Road then Weld Road, and moved into the present Cheras premises in November 1973. The congregation also runs a sister home in Penang; together the two Malaysian houses care for around 135 elderly residents who would otherwise have no one to look after them. The home welcomes elderly people of every race and religion.

The Cheras campus is unusually green and spacious for a city site, with hand rails through the corridors and lifts, attached bathrooms in resident rooms, multiple lounges, and natural lighting throughout. On-site facilities include a dining hall, physiotherapy room, hair salon, sewing room, reading room, tea room, a small shop selling items made by residents, and a main hall used for performances and gatherings. Daily activity programmes, religious services and visiting performers are part of the routine. The home is widely cited in Malaysian eldercare circles as a benchmark for cleanliness, dignity and standard of care, and admissions typically run with a waiting list.

This is a charity home rather than a commercial nursing home, and historically the model elsewhere in the congregation has been to admit low-income elderly people aged 65 and older regardless of nationality or religion, supported by donations rather than full fees. Families considering it should phone ahead to confirm current admission criteria, whether a means test applies, what donation or contribution is expected, current waiting list length, and the level of nursing care provided (the home is not pitched as a high-acuity medical facility). Ask how dementia, mobility loss and end-of-life care are handled, and whether residents transfer out if their needs exceed what the sisters can provide.
address: Jalan Sekuci, &, Jalan 5/96, Taman Sri Bahtera, 56100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Noble Care Nursing Home in Jalan IPOH, Kuala Lampur
editorial_summary:
Noble Care Nursing Home (Jalan Ipoh) is at 27, Jalan Cumarasami, off Jalan Ipoh, 51100 Kuala Lumpur. It is the Jalan Ipoh branch of Noble Care Malaysia, a family-run eldercare group established in 2005 that operates around 18 branches across the Klang Valley with extended services in Johor, Ipoh, Negeri Sembilan and Penang. The group's head office and a sister branch in Cheras / Taman Shanghai are the longer-running locations; the Jalan Ipoh house is one of the newer additions and is marketed as a residential nursing home with 24-hour staffing.

Published services at this location include long-term residential care, respite stays, end-of-life and palliative support, dementia and Alzheimer's care, and post-stroke and post-cancer care. Skilled-nursing tasks listed by the operator cover urinary catheter care, colostomy bag management, nasogastric (Ryle's) tube feeding, post-operative recovery, pressure ulcer dressing, tracheostomy care and on-site physiotherapy. Care for younger adults and children with cerebral palsy or other disabilities is also offered. Bed count, room types and pricing are not published online and have to be requested directly. Google reviews for this branch are limited (3 ratings, 5.0 average), so most independent feedback online concerns the wider Noble Care group rather than this specific address.

For families this branch may suit those who want a brand with multiple Klang Valley sites, a transferable care plan, and the option to move between branches if needs or family logistics change. Before signing, ask for the JKM licence specific to 27 Jalan Cumarasami (not just the group), the actual registered-nurse coverage day and night, ratio of carers to residents, monthly doctor visit arrangement, pricing for shared vs single rooms with a clear list of what is included (diapers, milk, laundry, physio, hospital escort), and whether tracheostomy / PEG / oxygen residents have ever been managed at this location specifically.
address: 27, Jalan Cumarasami, Jalan Ipoh, 51100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Bandar Damai Home Care
editorial_summary:
Bandar Damai Home Care is a small nursing home in the Bandar Damai Perdana neighbourhood of Cheras, Kuala Lumpur, at No. 24, Jalan Damai Perdana 2/6f. The facility is managed by a retired government hospital staff nurse and supported by trained nurses, with a website at bandardamaihomecare.wixsite.com.

The operator runs two homes with a combined capacity of 40 beds and provides 24-hour nursing care. Monthly doctor visits from KKM-registered physicians are included as a standard feature. Operating hours are daily 9am to 7pm. Phone: +60 3-9101 7930.

Two Google Maps reviews average 4.5 stars, with reviewers noting good service and attentive staff. Pricing is not published on the website. Request monthly fee details and ask to confirm the JKM licence number when you make contact.
address: No. 24, Jalan Damai Perdana 2/6f, Bandar Damai Perdana, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Ee Dian Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Ee Dian Nursing Home (伊甸园安老院) is a Cheras-area nursing home in Taman Bukit Hijau, Kuala Lumpur, contactable on 012-627 7345 and via eediannursinghome.com. The operator states more than 10 years of experience running the facility, and the location is convenient to several major hospitals - roughly 8 minutes by car to HUKM (Hospital Canselor Tuanku Muhriz UKM) and the UKM Specialist Centre, 7 minutes to Pantai Hospital Cheras, and around 12 minutes to Pantai Hospital Ampang - useful for families who anticipate frequent specialist follow-up.

Published services span three tiers: independent living for relatively well older adults, assisted living for those who need help with daily activities, and total nursing care for residents with significant medical needs including post-stroke recovery and other severe illness. Room options are listed as single rooms, shared double rooms, and open dormitory-style rooms, allowing families to match privacy and budget. Specific bed count, pricing per tier, registered-nurse coverage and dementia-care protocols are not published on the website, and at the time of writing parts of the site (the Home page) were returning a 404, so details should be verified by phone or site visit. Google review volume for this address is very low (1 rating, 5.0).

This home suits families based in the Cheras / Bandar Tun Razak / Bukit Hijau area who want proximity to UKM and Pantai Cheras and a graduated care model under one roof. Before committing, ask to see the JKM licence for the Bukit Hijau premises, confirm the registered-nurse-to-resident ratio on each shift (especially overnight), clarify which doctor visits and how often, ask about specific clinical capabilities (PEG / NG tube feeds, tracheostomy, oxygen, pressure ulcer dressing, syringe driver), and request the all-in monthly cost including diapers, milk feeds, laundry, physiotherapy and hospital escort. Confirm whether the open dormitory rooms have curtains or partitions and how dementia residents are accommodated.
address: 26, Jalan 3/119a, Taman Bukit Hijau, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Sahana Old Folks Home
care_types: Nursing home, Physical therapist, Retirement home
editorial_summary:
Sahana Old Folks Home sits at Jalan Selampit 23, Taman Klang Jaya, 41200 Klang — a residential neighbourhood in the heart of Klang town. The facility is positioned within roughly 2.5 km of two major hospitals: Bukit Tinggi Medical Centre (Manipal Hospitals Klang) and Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital Klang, which matters greatly for families managing residents with active medical conditions. Google Maps lists the care types as nursing home, physical therapy, and retirement home, suggesting a multi-level offering beyond basic residential care.

The facility is described as providing medical nursing and wound care alongside gentler services such as physiotherapy, games, arts and crafts, and gardening programmes. Premium rooms are listed as air-conditioned with television access and 24-hour nursing support. Daily homestyle meals are part of the package. Reviewer accounts on Google mention the facility successfully supporting residents with chronic kidney disease, prostate illness, and diabetes — noting improvements in mobility and glucose control — which points to active nursing management rather than purely custodial care. The facility holds a 5.0 Google rating across 21 reviews.

Bed capacity is not publicly disclosed, and detailed pricing is not available online — families should call +60 10-214 9701 to ask about current availability and monthly rates. The care types listed suggest physical therapy is offered in-house, but it is worth confirming whether a registered physiotherapist is on staff full-time or on a scheduled visiting basis. As with any small Klang nursing home, ask about staffing ratios at night and the protocol for medical emergencies given the proximity to nearby hospitals.
address: 63, Jalan Selampit 23, Taman Klang Jaya, 41200 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Sunstar Golden Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Sunstar Golden Care Centre is listed as a nursing home in Bandar Sunway, one of Selangor's most densely served eldercare corridors given its proximity to Sunway Medical Centre and a large residential population. The facility holds a 5-star Google rating across 24 reviews — a strong signal of resident and family satisfaction — and operates from a Bandar Sunway address. Beyond the Google Maps listing, the home does not maintain a published website or active social media page, so most service detail is best gathered on a call or visit.

Bandar Sunway's eldercare landscape ranges from premium retirement living products (Sunway Sanctuary is the most visible) down to smaller community-scale homes. Sunstar appears to sit toward the smaller, neighbourhood end of that spectrum based on its current footprint. The phone number (+60 14-660 1127) and its Google presence confirm it is operational, and the rating suggests a positively regarded environment.

When calling or visiting Sunstar Golden Care Centre, useful questions include the JKM registration and licence status, the range of dependency levels accepted (low, medium, high, bed-bound), nurse-to-resident ratios, visiting doctor arrangements, and current pricing. Bandar Sunway's hospital proximity is a real asset for high-acuity transfers — ask about the protocol for medical escalation and which hospital they typically liaise with.
address: 2, Jalan PJS 10/3, Bandar Sunway, 46150 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Noble Care | Retirement Home Jalan USJ 18
editorial_summary:
Noble Care's USJ 18 branch sits at 1 Jalan USJ 18/3 in Subang Jaya — a well-connected residential address in the USJ township, roughly equidistant between Sunway and the USJ commercial belt. Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd. operates around 18 branches nationally, with a concentration in Selangor and the Klang Valley, making it one of Malaysia's larger private nursing home chains. The USJ 18 location is marketed as a retirement and nursing home, offering care across the acuity spectrum from senior companionship and assisted daily living through to palliative, dementia, and complex clinical nursing.

The website documents a clinical capability set that goes well beyond basic elderly care: the team handles Foley catheter management, nasogastric tube feeding, colostomy bag changes, tracheostomy care, bedsore dressing, post-operative nursing, and physiotherapy. Stroke and cancer care, cerebral palsy management, and handicapped adult care are also listed. The facility positions itself as providing 24-hour medical supervision with a nursing team rather than caregiver-only staffing. Noble Care's group infrastructure means the USJ 18 home benefits from shared protocols and clinical governance across the network, which is a meaningful advantage over standalone homes.

Pricing is not published on the website — families should request the rate card directly at +60 16-778 6795, noting that Noble Care positions itself in the mid-to-upper market. It is worth confirming how many residents the USJ 18 branch specifically accommodates, since bed counts vary by branch across the network, and whether a JKM licence is held for this address. Questions about the nurse-to-resident ratio on night shifts, visiting hours, and meal arrangements will help families compare the USJ 18 branch accurately against Noble Care's other Selangor locations if they are also shortlisting Petaling Jaya or Klang branches.
address: 1 ,Jalan, Jalan USJ 18/3, Usj 18, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Sunway Home Healthcare
care_types: Home health care service, Health consultant, Hospice
editorial_summary:
Sunway Home Healthcare is a hospital-backed home care service operating under the Sunway Healthcare Group, one of Malaysia's larger private hospital networks. It is based at Sunway GEO Avenue in Bandar Sunway, Subang Jaya, and delivers care directly to patients' homes rather than admitting them to a residential facility. The service is positioned as an extension of Sunway Medical Centre's clinical standards into the community setting.

The team includes registered nurses with post-basic training in specialist areas. Services cover wound care, tube feeding (NGT), stoma care, oncology patient management, post-stroke and post-surgery rehabilitation, home physiotherapy, and palliative end-of-life nursing. Palliative patients are typically referred in by a Sunway Medical Centre physician. Pricing for individual sessions is listed on the website and ranges from around RM 160 to RM 380 or more depending on the service, though package costs vary. Day care and hourly respite sessions are also available.

This is not a residential nursing home — there are no beds, rooms, or 24-hour on-site staff. It is the right fit if your family member is living at home or with relatives and you need skilled clinical support on a scheduled basis: wound rounds, tube changes, post-hospitalisation monitoring, or caregiver respite. For families needing round-the-clock supervision or a residential placement, this service alone will not meet that need. Operating hours are Monday to Friday, 8:30am to 5:30pm; after-hours availability should be confirmed directly.
address: G-01-01, Sunway GEO Avenue, Jalan Lagoon Selatan, Bandar Sunway, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Elderly Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Elderly Care Centre is listed under a Subang Jaya address in the SS14 area with a phone number (+60 18-383 7704). The Google listing carries three 4.7-star reviews and an SS14 pin. The home keeps a low online profile and does not maintain a published website or active social media page, so most service detail is best gathered on a call or visit. SS14 is a mature residential neighbourhood in Subang Jaya with reasonable proximity to Subang Jaya Medical Centre.

Bed count, staffing, care scope, and JKM registration status are best confirmed directly with the home. Smaller community homes in established Subang Jaya neighbourhoods often operate quietly through word-of-mouth referrals rather than digital marketing, so the limited online footprint is not unusual. The SUMC proximity is a practical asset for families who anticipate needing hospital escalations.

Families considering this centre should call first to establish what care types are offered, whether it holds a current JKM licence, how many residents it accommodates, the nursing staff qualifications, and what medical complexity it can handle. A site visit during a mealtime or activity period is the most reliable way to assess day-to-day conditions before making a placement decision.
address: 1, Jalan SS 14/6k, Ss 14, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat jagaan warga emas murni kasih
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Murni Kasih is an aged care centre in Bandar Country Homes, Rawang — a township in northern Selangor roughly 30–40 minutes from central Kuala Lumpur, making it more relevant for families based in the Rawang, Serendah, or Kundang corridor than for those in the urban Klang Valley core. The facility has a Facebook presence (Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Murni Kaseh) with over 1,700 followers, which suggests an active community-facing operation, though the Facebook page was not accessible for detailed content extraction.

No website was found and no contact phone number is listed in the source data. The Google listing classifies it as aged care and gives a 5-star rating from three reviews — positive but too small a sample to draw firm conclusions. The name 'Murni Kasih' (Pure Love) and the Rawang location are consistent with a smaller, community-style care home, possibly with a Muslim orientation given common naming conventions, but this has not been confirmed.

Families should search for the facility on Facebook (Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Murni Kaseh) to obtain a current contact number, as no phone was available from any directory source. Ask about the bed count, care level provided (ambulant versus bedridden), nursing staff availability, daily routine, meal provisions, pricing, and JKM registration status. If Islamic dietary and prayer arrangements matter to your family, confirm these during the initial call.
address: 83, Jalan DM 3/3, Taman Desa Mas, Bandar Country Homes, 48000 Rawang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Ph Guardian Senior Care Centre
editorial_summary:
PH Guardian Senior Care Centre sits at No. 1, Jalan Putra Bistari 2/1T in Putra Heights, a residential township in the southern Subang Jaya corridor. It operates as a private nursing home serving the growing elderly population in this area, which sits roughly between Subang Jaya and Puchong. The centre's stated aim is compassionate, personalised care with dignity and respect at the centre of everything — standard positioning, but the Putra Heights address gives it good proximity to both Subang Jaya Medical Centre and Putrajaya Hospital.

PH Guardian advertises 24-hour care with qualified nurses and caregivers, fully furnished air-conditioned rooms, WiFi, Astro, hot-water showers, handrails, bath chairs, and round-the-clock CCTV monitoring. It is listed as handling mild dementia cases, suggesting nursing dependency is catered for, though the extent of medical complexity they accept is not publicly detailed. Pricing starts from RM2,800 per month with no registration fee, which is broadly in line with mid-tier Subang Jaya nursing homes.

The centre has only three Google reviews (5-star average), which makes independent assessment difficult. No Facebook activity, website content beyond the basic pages, or any JKM licence number was found publicly. Given the small footprint of online presence, families should visit in person, clarify what nursing qualifications are held by on-duty staff, confirm whether a doctor makes regular rounds, and ask specifically what level of medical dependency — PEG feeding, tracheostomy, oxygen — they can or cannot manage.
address: 1, Jalan Putra Bistari 2/1t, Putra Heights, 47650 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Ixora Senior Care
editorial_summary:
Ixora Senior Care is a growing private nursing home group with branches in Subang Jaya and a luxury outpost in Damansara Heights, Kuala Lumpur. The Subang Jaya facility most closely associated with this listing is located at No. 21 Jalan SS12/1F, Subang Jaya 47500 Selangor — a purpose-configured care home positioned as an affordable yet professionally run nursing facility. A sister branch operates in the SS14 part of Subang Jaya, and the group's flagship Care Suites is in Damansara Heights. The SS12 location specifically targets seniors needing long-term residential nursing, dementia care, post-surgery recovery, and assisted daily living, within a setting the brand describes as a blend of comfort and clinical rigour.

Packages start at RM3,400 per person per month — competitive for a staffed nursing home in the Subang Jaya area — and each package includes weekly doctor consultations, emergency doctor access, nursing procedures, pharmacy medication reviews, and physiotherapy sessions. Room options span single suites, two-bedded suites, three-bedded suites, and multi-sharing configurations. The facility runs 24-hour professional nursing care with CCTV coverage throughout. Ixora integrates technology into daily operations: fall detection sensors, electronic medical records, and a family portal giving relatives real-time access to health reports are features the group has highlighted in published materials, which is less common at this price point in the local market.

Families should ask on enquiry which specific services fall within the standard package versus incurring an additional charge, particularly for physiotherapy frequency and nursing procedures such as wound care. Confirming the exact bed count at the SS12 branch and current occupancy will help set expectations on room availability and the resident-to-staff ratio on night shifts. The Subang Jaya location is convenient for families based in USJ, Puchong, or Petaling Jaya, and proximity to Subang Jaya Medical Centre and Sunway Medical Centre is a practical plus for residents requiring specialist follow-up.
address: 21, Jalan SS 12/1f, SUBANG JAYA, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Bright Care Home (Pusat Jagaan Rumah Warga Tua Cahaya)
editorial_summary:
Bright Care Home — registered under the name Pusat Jagaan Rumah Warga Tua Cahaya — is a small residential care facility located at No. 2, Jalan Merpati 8, Bandar Puchong Jaya, Selangor. It has been listed as operating since 2010, making it one of the longer-standing community-run homes in the Puchong corridor. Google Maps classifies it as a retirement home and assisted living facility, and it carries a 4.5 rating from 15 reviews, suggesting a modest but reasonably well-regarded neighbourhood home.

Directory listings describe the facility as offering both day-care services on a daily-rate basis and full residential care on a monthly arrangement. This dual model is fairly common among older community homes in Puchong Jaya. No website or Facebook page is publicly active, which limits what can be verified independently about staffing levels, medical supervision, or the range of care dependencies managed. The facility is reachable by phone at +60 17-399 0362.

Because the facility has no active web presence, families should visit in person to assess room conditions, staff ratios, and whether the home can handle the specific dependency level of their relative — particularly for anything beyond light assisted living. Ask directly whether the staff includes qualified nurses, how medical emergencies are handled, and whether a doctor visits on any regular schedule. The lack of public information is not unusual for smaller neighbourhood homes in Selangor, but it does mean you cannot rely on anything other than an in-person assessment.
address: 40 Jalan 8 Taman Bukit Kuchai, Bandar Puchong Jaya, 47100 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Suria
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Suria is a nursing home located in Seksyen 7, Selangor. It holds a 3.5/5 rating on Google Maps from 6 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Suria is listed as a nursing home in Seksyen 7, Shah Alam — a mature residential township with reasonable access to major Shah Alam clinics and the broader Klang Valley hospital network. The home is contactable on +60 3-7958 5055.

The home keeps a low online profile and does not maintain a published website or active social media page, so most service detail is best gathered directly. Bed count, staffing model, the scope of medical care, religious or cultural orientation, and JKM registration are best confirmed on a call. Seksyen 7 is a mixed residential-commercial neighbourhood that supports a number of small community-scale homes.

Families considering this centre should call ahead to establish whether it is currently taking residents and what level of care is offered. Useful questions: how many beds, registered-nurse cover overnight, doctor-visit frequency, the dependency levels accepted (ambulant, partial-care, bedridden), the current monthly fee, and the JKM licence number. A short visit will give a clearer picture of day-to-day conditions than the listing alone.

title: Pusat Jagaan dan Rawatan Orang Tua Al-Ikhlas
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan dan Rawatan Orang Tua Al-Ikhlas is set inside a converted endowed mosque (masjid lama diwakafkan) at Lot 3530, Jalan Batu 18, Kampung Pulau Meranti, Puchong — a kampung enclave tucked between Puchong's commercial sprawl and the Putrajaya wetlands. The centre was founded in 1999 by Muji binti Hj Sulaiman, a former Chief Nurse at Hospital Serdang who brought home elderly patients discharged without family to receive them and has been running the home ever since. That clinical background — two decades of geriatric ward nursing before founding the home — is the most substantive credential in the public record. The facility currently cares for around 50 residents and employs approximately 12 staff.

The centre is Islamic in character, accepting donations of zakat and fitting its care philosophy within an Islamic welfare framework, though available sources do not state that admission is exclusively restricted to Muslims. Care provision includes daily personal assistance (bathing, dressing, feeding), basic medical monitoring, and social and recreational activities with a physical, mental, and spiritual dimension. Reviewer feedback on directory sites praises effective communication between staff and families and a holistic care approach.

The converted mosque setting is unconventional and worth seeing in person — for some families, the quiet kampung environment and community character will feel exactly right; others may want a more medically equipped facility. Pricing, bed availability, and the JKM licence number are best confirmed directly with the home. Given the 50-resident scale and 12-person staff, ask about the nursing cover arrangement — specifically whether trained nurses or caregivers manage clinical tasks overnight, and what the protocol is for medical emergencies given the rural access point.
address: Lot 3530, Jalan Batu 18, Kampung Pulau Meranti, 47120 Puchong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Noble Care Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Noble Care Nursing Home (Taman Shanghai) sits at Jalan Bukit Pisang, Taman Shanghai, 58100 Kuala Lumpur, in the Old Klang Road / Salak South corridor. It is one of the longer-running houses under the Noble Care Malaysia group, a family-run eldercare operator founded in 2005 with around 18 branches across the Klang Valley and outlying states. Office contact is 016-266 3901; visiting and admission enquiries are handled Monday to Saturday, 7am to 8pm. The Taman Shanghai site is presented as the brand's premium nursing home offering, organised into named wings or houses (Millow, Medley, Molly, Fair Haven, Eden House) covering both nursing-care and lower-acuity retirement-living tracks.

Published services include long-term residential nursing care, retirement-style living for more independent residents, respite stays, and a dementia / Alzheimer's pathway delivered by carers the group describes as having specific dementia training. The care plan is described as combining therapeutic, preventive and rehabilitative elements rather than a single fixed package. Bed count, room types, registered-nurse coverage and pricing are not published online for this address and need to be confirmed directly. Google reviews here are mixed - 13 ratings averaging 2.8 out of 5 - notably lower than the group's marketing tier elsewhere, so families should read the actual review text and ask the operator to address specific concerns.

This branch suits families wanting a multi-house campus rather than a small shophouse conversion, with the option to step a resident between an independent retirement room and full nursing care without changing operator. Before deciding, ask for the JKM licence specific to the Jalan Bukit Pisang premises, the named registered nurse on duty overnight, the actual carer-to-resident ratio on each shift, the doctor's visiting frequency, and a written quotation broken down by room category. Read the lower-rated Google reviews and ask the manager point by point how those issues have been addressed. Clarify what happens clinically (PEG, oxygen, tracheostomy, end-of-life care) and whether the dementia wing has secured exits.
address: No 9, Jalan Bukit Pisang, Taman Shanghai, 58100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Harvestars Care Centre (Klang, H-Residence)
editorial_summary:
Harvestars Care Centre's Klang branch operates from H-Residence, a stand-alone condominium block in Kawasan 6, Klang — part of what the group markets as Malaysia's first nursing care home built in condo style. The facility sits within a residential tower format and is designed to accommodate around 200 residents, serving seniors who need anything from independent or assisted living through to full nursing care for stroke recovery, dementia, and palliative cases. Harvestars runs a parallel branch in Puchong, making this a two-location chain with a consistent brand identity built around amenity-rich, hotel-adjacent living.

The website indicates a broad clinical scope: 24-hour care delivered by registered nurses, on-call doctors, physiotherapists, and traditional Chinese medicine practitioners. The care menu listed covers stroke rehabilitation, dementia support, palliative care, and assisted daily living. Recreational infrastructure is notably extensive for a nursing home — karaoke room, mahjong room, snooker room, library, massage room, and an in-house cafeteria, plus a natural-farming area. Four meals per day are provided by a dedicated chef. Wi-Fi, special mattresses, and a full-sized closet are listed as standard inclusions.

Because Harvestars operates inside a private residential high-rise, prospective families should ask specifically which floors or units are in use, how emergency evacuation works, and whether lift access is sufficient for bedridden residents. The group has a Facebook page for the Klang branch where recent activity can be checked, but no public pricing is published — call to discuss rates, which are likely to vary by room type and care level. Licence status and JKM certification should be confirmed before admission, as the condo-format model is novel in Selangor.
address: Blok A-GF-1, Jalan Dato Amar KS01, Teluk Gadung Kecil, Kawasan 6, 41100 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Al-Fattah
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Al-Fattah is a community-founded care centre operating from a rented residential property at No. 9, Jalan TTS 7/1, Taman Tasik Semenyih, 43500 Semenyih — a quiet lakeside housing estate about 45 minutes southeast of Kuala Lumpur. The centre was established in 2018 by Mohammad Azlan Abdul Rahman and grew from a grass-roots welfare initiative aimed at elderly individuals who lack family support or who are managing chronic conditions such as hypertension, diabetes, or mobility impairment. The Arabic name, its Facebook page identity as 'Villa Al-Fattah,' and its programming all point clearly to an Islamic-oriented facility.

The centre is also registered as Pertubuhan Kebajikan Warga Emas Al-Fattah — a welfare association (pertubuhan) rather than a private care home company — and has accepted public donations through sumbangan.com, suggesting a charitable funding model that likely keeps costs below market rates. Care activities listed include daily health monitoring, medication management, wound treatment, light exercise, religious programmes (congregational prayers and classes), nutritious meals adapted to dietary restrictions, and recreational activities. The residential setting means rooms and shared spaces are home-like rather than clinical, which suits ambulant or lightly dependent seniors but may be less suited to high-acuity cases requiring intensive nursing.

Families looking at Al-Fattah should be aware that this is a welfare-oriented, Malay-Muslim setting: residents should be comfortable with Islamic prayer schedules and communal religious observance. Bed count, staffing ratios, and whether a nurse or doctor provides formal medical oversight were not confirmed in available sources — these are essential questions to ask when contacting En. Azlan at 017-992 5503 (the contact listed in third-party sources, though the Google Maps listing shows +60 3-8726 0824). Confirm whether the facility holds a JKM licence, as its welfare-association structure may place it under a different regulatory framework than private care homes.
address: 9, Jalan TTS 7/1, Taman Tasik Semenyih, 43500 Semenyih, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Syurga Warga Emas
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Syurga Warga Emas is a nursing home situated in Kampung Baru Semenyih, a semi-rural township in the south-eastern reach of Selangor. The name — syurga means heaven — suggests a Muslim-run home with an emphasis on spiritual comfort alongside daily care. Semenyih sits roughly 40 km south-east of Kuala Lumpur, making it an accessible option for families based in Kajang, Beranang, or the Nilai corridor across the Negeri Sembilan border. The home is listed as a nursing home, indicating it accepts residents who require a degree of nursing supervision beyond simple companionship or assisted living.

The home keeps a low online profile, so most service detail is best gathered on a call to +60 16-675 8084. No website or Facebook page has been located. Rating and review data, total bed count, and staffing levels are not publicly stated. At the Semenyih price band, nursing homes in this area tend to cater to mid-range family budgets, but pricing here is unverified. Nearby healthcare infrastructure includes Klinik Kesihatan Semenyih and the larger hospital facilities in Kajang, roughly 20 minutes away.

For families visiting, key questions to raise on a first call include: whether the home holds a current JKM licence, what the nurse-to-resident ratio looks like day and night, whether the home accommodates bedridden or post-stroke residents, and what the visiting hours policy is. Ask also about the religious programme schedule, meal arrangements, and whether any subsidy or JKM fee assistance applies. A site visit is strongly recommended before any commitment.
address: Jalan Besar Kampung Baru, Kampung Baru Semenyih, 43500 Semenyih, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua is a retirement home located in Taman Klang Jaya, a residential neighbourhood within the Klang municipality in Selangor. Klang is a well-serviced town with good access to Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah — a major government hospital — and several private clinics, which makes the area a practical base for elderly residents who may need periodic medical follow-up. The home is listed as a retirement home rather than a nursing home, suggesting its primary orientation is residential living for ambulant or semi-independent seniors.

With 30 residents listed in the source data, the home operates at a modest, community-scale size. No website or Facebook page has been identified, and the home keeps a low online profile. Detailed service information — including care ratios, any dementia or post-acute capabilities, meal arrangements, and activity programming — is not publicly available. Contact is via +60 10-214 9701. Pricing, bed breakdown, and JKM registration status are all unverified at this stage.

Families considering this home should use the first phone call to establish the resident intake criteria — specifically whether the home accepts individuals who are fully or partially dependent — since the retirement home classification can span a wide spectrum. Ask about staffing overnight, the visiting policy, languages spoken by the care team, and whether the home is registered with the Jabatan Kebajikan Masyarakat. A personal visit to assess room sizes, cleanliness, and the atmosphere of the common areas is the most reliable next step.
address: 44, Lorong Serunai 3b, Taman Klang Jaya, 41200 Klang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Penjagaan Warga Emas Hati Mesra
editorial_summary:
Pusat Penjagaan Warga Emas Hati Mesra appears in Taman Nirwana, which falls within the broader Ampang corridor in Selangor — an area with access to Ampang Puteri Specialist Hospital and Hospital Ampang. The centre is categorised as a charity operation, consistent with donation-funded Islamic welfare homes (rumah kebajikan) that typically serve lower-income elderly residents who may have limited family support. The home carries two 5-star Google reviews.

The home keeps a very low online profile and is not currently reachable through a publicly listed phone number or website — most welfare homes in this category operate via JKM social-welfare referrals rather than direct private enquiries. A 'charity' care type in the Malaysian context typically implies means-tested admission, donor-funded operations, and care provided largely by volunteers or welfare officers, though some charity homes do employ qualified nursing staff.

Families interested in this type of welfare-oriented home are usually best served by contacting JKM Selangor directly to confirm the centre's registration, eligibility criteria, and current operational status. Placement in a charity home usually requires a social-welfare referral rather than a private admission process, and capacity is often limited. JKM caseworkers can also point families to other suitable welfare homes in the Ampang area if Hati Mesra is full.
address: Selangor, 34, Jalan Nirwana 36, Taman Nirwana, 68000 Ampang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Sri Tanjung (Muslim Women Only)
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Sri Tanjung is an exclusively Muslim women's care home located at 2990-A Lorong Melati, Kampung Damai, 48050 Kuang, Rawang, Selangor. The centre is registered with the Department of Social Welfare (JKM) under registration number B/PJBWT006/2019 and has been in operation for over 30 years, giving it among the longer operational histories of any community-based elderly care home in northern Selangor. Its single-gender, single-faith mandate is clearly stated — the home accepts Muslim women only — making it a specific resource for families seeking a religiously congruent environment for elderly female relatives.

Sri Tanjung offers flexible care arrangements including monthly residential placement, temporary or transit stays, and daily packages running from 7am to 7pm. Social media content shows residents engaging in group activities, and the TikTok and Facebook presence describes comprehensive care services (perkhidmatan jagaan yang menyeluruh). The JKM registration confirms a basic standard of government oversight. The home appears to operate on a community welfare footing, accepting enquiries via phone at +60 18-209 8986 and directing potential residents through a standard intake process.

The care level offered at Sri Tanjung should be discussed directly, as the home's positioning as a welfare-oriented community centre means its clinical nursing capabilities may be limited compared to a private nursing home. Families should ask what nursing qualifications the staff hold, how medical needs above a basic threshold are managed, and whether the daily rate and monthly rate structures differ significantly. The Kuang location is relatively rural — confirm access to the nearest hospital (Hospital Selayang, approximately 20 kilometres) and how medical emergencies are handled. For a Muslim family seeking a culturally appropriate, women-only residential option with a long track record, this home is worth investigating.
address: 2990-A, Lorong Melati, Kampung Damai, 48050 Sungai Buloh, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Barakah
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Barakah is based in Saujana Rawang, a growing township north of Rawang town proper in Selangor. It positions itself as an Islamic-oriented full-care centre for elderly Muslims, with a brand identity built around compassion and family-like care. The facility operates its own website (wargaemasbarakah.com, though the domain was redirecting at time of research) and has a Facebook presence, suggesting it is actively marketing. Monthly fees start from RM1,900, which is at the lower end for Selangor private nursing homes and likely reflects a shared-room model.

Based on available information, Barakah provides full-day and residential care, nutritious meals prepared according to halalan toyyiban standards, and spiritual programming including Quranic reading sessions, religious lectures, and group dhikr. A team of experienced doctors is cited as part of the medical support structure, though the frequency of doctor visits versus on-demand access is unclear. Activity programming includes gardening, art therapy, and light exercise. The Rawang location means the nearest major hospital is likely Hospital Rawang or facilities in Sg. Buloh, so families with medically complex residents should factor in transport time.

Pricing from RM1,900 is attractive, but families should clarify what that includes — whether meals, diapers, and medications are bundled or billed separately. Confirm nursing qualifications of day and night staff, the centre's JKM registration status, and whether they can handle higher-dependency residents (bedridden, PEG-fed, or needing wound care). The low Google review count (3 reviews at 3.7 stars) limits confidence without a direct visit.
address: Zara Fasa 14A No. 2, Jalan SR 8/2E, Taman Saujana Rawang Banglo, Saujana Rawang, 48000 Rawang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Semenyih
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Semenyih is listed in Bandar Teknologi Kajang — a township on the southern edge of the Klang Valley near Semenyih and Broga. The Google listing categorises it as a social welfare centre, which typically implies a charity or donation-funded operating model rather than a fully commercial nursing home. The home is contactable on +60 10-253 6750.

The home keeps a low online profile and does not maintain a published website or active social media page, so most service detail is best gathered directly. As a welfare-oriented home, admission may be means-tested or referral-based via JKM caseworkers rather than a private admission process. Bed count, staffing, and the scope of medical care are best confirmed on a call.

Families interested in welfare-oriented homes are usually best served by contacting JKM Selangor first to clarify eligibility and the referral pathway. Useful questions when calling the home directly: current admission availability, registered-nurse or caregiver coverage, dependency levels accepted, donation or fee arrangements, and the JKM licence number. JKM caseworkers can also point families to other suitable welfare homes in the Kajang/Semenyih area if Penyayang Semenyih is full.
address: Seksyen 1, No 15, Jalan 1/5, Bandar Teknologi Kajang, 43000 Semenyih, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Kaseh Bonda
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Kaseh Bonda is listed in Bandar Country Homes, Rawang — an outer northern Selangor township. The Google listing categorises it as a home health care service and lodging facility, which suggests it may operate as a smaller residential home rather than a fully clinical nursing home, though this is best confirmed directly. The home is contactable on +60 11-1111 7844.

The home keeps a low online profile and does not maintain a published website or active social media page, so most service detail is best gathered on a call. The 'home health care' classification can imply visiting nursing services, residential lodging, or both — clarifying which mode applies will help families understand if Kaseh Bonda matches their needs. The Bandar Country Homes location is on the outer edge of the Klang Valley, with the nearest major hospital being Hospital Selayang.

Families considering this home should call ahead to establish current operating hours, what care types are offered, and whether it accepts residential placements or only home-care visits. Useful questions: registered-nurse availability, dependency levels accepted, monthly fees, JKM licence status, and the protocol for medical escalations from the Rawang area.
address: 83, Jalan DM 3/3, Taman Desa Mas, Bandar Country Homes, 48000 Rawang, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Damai
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Damai — known in Chinese as 乐善社老人残障中心 (Lok Sin Society Elderly and Disabled Centre) — is a registered NGO care home located at No. 30, Jalan Damai Perdana 2/6B, Bandar Damai Perdana, Cheras, on the Selangor–Kuala Lumpur fringe. The Chinese name signals a community-welfare orientation rooted in traditional voluntary sector charity rather than private commercial operation. The home serves a mixed elderly and physically disabled population, with up to approximately 100 residents documented across available sources. Operating hours are listed as daily 10:30am–5:00pm for visits, though residential care is around the clock.

The home received support from the Sin Chew Daily Foundation's 300万送暖 (RM3 million warming) programme, indicating recognition from within the Chinese-medium philanthropic community. The facility appears to accommodate residents at varying dependency levels — both ambulatory elderly and more dependent individuals with physical disabilities — suggesting a broader intake than most nursing homes. No clinical specialisations such as dementia units or formal rehabilitation programmes have been confirmed from available public sources.

As an NGO home, pricing is likely means-tested or donation-supplemented rather than fixed at commercial rates, but this should be confirmed directly (contact: +60 17-370 0403; Facebook: wargatuadamai). The Chinese-language community orientation means Mandarin and likely Cantonese are working languages, though the name Damai and the Cheras location suggest some Malay-speaking capacity too. Families should ask about the ratio of nursing-trained staff to caregivers, whether residents with dementia or complex medical needs are accepted, and how fee structures work for residents without family financial support.
address: Bandar Damai Perdana, 43000 Balakong, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Bait Al Mawaddah, Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Lembaga Zakat Selangor
editorial_summary:
Bait Al-Mawaddah is an elderly care complex established in 2014 by Lembaga Zakat Selangor (LZS) and Majlis Agama Islam Selangor (MAIS), built on three acres of waqf land in Kampung Jalan Kebun, Section 30, Shah Alam. It was formally inaugurated by the Sultan of Selangor in September 2016. The centre operates under an explicitly Islamic welfare mandate — its name means 'House of Compassion' — and is designed to shelter elderly Muslim asnaf (zakat-eligible poor) in Selangor who are abandoned or lack family support capable of caring for them. The construction cost RM3 million and the facility has been recognised with a Selangor State Outstanding Senior Care Centre Award from JKM.

The centre has 26 rooms accommodating up to 52 residents. Facilities on site include a clinic room, physiotherapy room, prayer hall, lecture hall, dining hall, and landscaped gardens. Programmes include morning exercise classes, handicraft sessions, cooking activities, and regular Islamic education — fardhu ain classes and religious lectures. The health programme integrates disease prevention, continuous monitoring, psychosocial support, and healthy lifestyle practices as a formal framework.

Admission eligibility is strict and non-negotiable: applicants must be Muslim, registered as fakir or miskin asnaf with LZS, aged 60 or above, and — importantly — must be physically capable of self-care at the point of entry. This means Bait Al-Mawaddah does not accept bedridden residents or those with significant nursing dependency at admission. There is no fee structure published because the facility is funded through zakat and waqf. Families with a non-Muslim relative or a Muslim relative who does not qualify as asnaf will need to look elsewhere; this centre is not open to the general public.
address: Kampung Jln Kebun, 40594 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Serene Skies Care Centre, Nursing Home (Bandar Sunway)
editorial_summary:
Serene Skies Care Centre is described in its own website copy as a nursing home serving the Petaling Jaya area, with proximity to Subang Jaya, Puchong, and Kelana Jaya cited as part of its catchment. The Google Maps listing places it in Taman Seri Subang — a residential enclave adjacent to the Bandar Sunway township — which would make it genuinely accessible for families based anywhere in the Sunway-Subang-PJ triangle. The facility holds a perfect 5-star Google rating from 30 reviews, a score that reviewers attribute to attentive, compassionate staff.

The operator's website describes 24/7 care with a team of skilled nurses and caregivers, and explicitly lists dementia care (described as a memory care programme for Alzheimer's and other dementias), post-surgery recovery, and personalised medical support including medication management and physical therapy. Recreational activities and social events are noted as part of the daily programme. These are credible service claims for a residential nursing home in this price bracket and area, though no independent clinical accreditation or JKM licence number appears in the public record.

A significant limitation in researching this facility is that its official website is hosted on an Odoo platform and returned a 404 error during research, which makes it difficult to verify details such as bed count, room configurations, pricing, and staffing qualifications. Families should request a full written service schedule, pricing breakdown, and JKM registration details when making an enquiry. The Bandar Sunway location is commercially well-connected and near Sunway Medical Centre, which is a practical advantage if hospitalisation becomes necessary — ask whether the home has an existing referral relationship with nearby hospitals.
address: 2, Lorong PJS 10/26c, Taman Seri Subang, 46150 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Mintygreen Care Centre @ USJ21, Subang Jaya
editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Centre @ USJ21 is one branch in Mintygreen's growing Klang Valley network, operating from 1, Jalan USJ 21/10 in UEP Subang Jaya — a mature township with excellent road access and proximity to Subang Jaya Medical Centre (SJMC) and several public clinics. Mintygreen Healthcare Group, established in 2017, is known as an early mover in integrating Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) into its nursing home model, and this positioning runs across all branches. The USJ21 unit is a smaller, neighbourhood-scale branch rather than a flagship; the main Kajang–Balakong site serves as the group's headquarters.

Across the Mintygreen network, the group's documented capabilities include 24-hour nursing care, post-surgery recovery, regular doctor visits, physiotherapy, rehabilitation, psychological counselling, TCM (acupuncture and Tui Na), dietetic services, day care, and home care through its '24 Angels' arm. Whether all of these services are available at the USJ21 branch specifically — or concentrated at larger sites — is not publicly stated and should be confirmed directly. The contact person listed for USJ21 is Ms. Xiao Tong at +60 11-5587 9788, distinct from the group's main line, which suggests branch-level management.

For families in Subang Jaya or USJ who want a local option without travelling to Kajang, this branch is a reasonable first call — but it warrants a site visit to assess the physical space, bed count, and what share of Mintygreen's full capability is actually on-site. Pricing is not published for this branch; the group's rates at other locations tend toward the mid-market range for Klang Valley. Ask specifically about doctor visit frequency, physio session costs, and whether TCM is integrated or a pay-per-session extra.
address: 1, Jalan USJ 21/10, Usj 2, 47630 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Synergy Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Synergy Care Centre is a 24-hour nursing home at 15, Jalan USJ Sentral 3, in the USJ Sentral commercial precinct of Subang Jaya, Selangor. The facility is operated under Nursing Synergy Sdn Bhd, which also maintains a separate contact line at a second location. The centre is led by Dr. Pathma, who is referenced in multiple reviews as being personally involved in resident care and responsive to family enquiries.

The centre provides trained registered nurse coverage around the clock, with a senior physiotherapist on-site delivering daily physiotherapy seven days a week. Clinical capabilities include feeding tube management, tracheostomy care and suctioning, oxygen therapy, wound care, and medication administration. Room types include single rooms, twin rooms, and dormitory beds, with third-party pricing sources listing rates in the RM 2,001–RM 3,000 per month range. Day care for seniors who return home each evening is also offered. Social activities — described as yoga, gardening, drawing, and board games — are organised regularly.

This is a competent option for medically complex residents within the USJ area, particularly those requiring tube care or respiratory support who cannot afford acute hospital costs long-term. The seven-days-a-week physiotherapy is a meaningful differentiator at this price band. Confirm on-site nurse staffing ratios, whether the doctor visit is a dedicated medical director or an on-call arrangement, and current JKM registration status before committing.
address: 15, Jalan Usj Sentral 3, USJ Sentral, 47640 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Impresif
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Impresif sits in a residential pocket of PJS 7, Petaling Jaya, at Jalan Bukit Menteri Selatan 7/2. It is classified as a non-profit organisation on Google, which suggests it may operate on a welfare or charitable footing rather than as a fully commercial nursing home — though this has not been independently confirmed. The location gives reasonable access to the Asia Jaya area and several bus routes serving the PJ Selatan corridor.

The home keeps a low online profile — no website or Facebook page — and is best contacted directly via the listed phone number (03-7954 9008). Bed count, staffing, the scope of medical care provided, and JKM registration status are best confirmed on a call. The non-profit classification could mean it operates as a charity home with subsidised placements, or it could reflect an outdated business-type tag on the listing.

Families considering this centre should call first to establish whether it is taking new residents, what level of care is provided, and whether there is a waiting list as is common with welfare-based homes. Useful questions when calling: the JKM licensing status, whether a registered nurse is on staff, and whether the home accepts bedridden residents. A short visit will give a much clearer picture than the listing alone.

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Muhammad Ibraahim (PJMI)
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Muhammad Ibraahim (PJMI) is located at Jalan TTS 5/2, Taman Tasik Semenyih, 43500 Semenyih, in the Semenyih sub-district of Selangor. The facility carries a Google rating of 4.8 from 75 reviews, which is notably high and suggests strong satisfaction among residents and families, though the volume of publicly available operational information is limited. From what is available, PJMI appears to operate as an Islamic-oriented elderly care home serving warga emas — senior citizens — with a stated mission of supporting those who lack family members to care for them.

Registered on the charitable platform Sumbangan.com, PJMI is positioned as a community welfare centre that relies partly on donations to sustain its operations and to assist elderly residents without heirs. This model — part residential home, part charitable welfare centre — is common among community-based care homes in semi-rural Selangor townships. The name and mission suggest a Muslim-managed environment, likely incorporating religious observance such as daily prayers into the daily routine. Specific clinical services, bed capacity, staffing ratios, and pricing have not been publicly disclosed by the facility.

Semenyih is approximately 35–40 km southeast of Kuala Lumpur, making it relatively accessible from Kajang and Beranang but distant from major tertiary hospitals — Hospital Kajang is the nearest government facility. Families considering PJMI should visit in person to assess the physical condition of the premises, staffing levels, clinical capabilities, and nursing care arrangements. Given the charitable funding model, confirm whether the facility holds a valid JKM licence, what medical oversight is in place, and whether the home can manage residents with complex nursing needs or is better suited to ambulatory, lower-dependency elderly individuals.
address: 2, Jalan TTS 5/2, Taman Tasik Semenyih, 43500 Semenyih, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Noble Care Malaysia Sdn Bhd
editorial_summary:
Noble Care (Noble Care (M) Sdn Bhd, reg. 903876-P) is a Malaysian nursing home chain with over 15 residential branches across the country, including Cheras, Rawang, Klang, Petaling Jaya, USJ-9 Subang Jaya, USJ-18, Jalan IPOH, Ampang, Semenyih, Kajang, Mid Valley, Seremban, and Serendah in the central region, plus branches in Johor Bahru, Ipoh, and Penang. The head office is at USJ 9 Subang Business Centre, Subang Jaya. Noble Care has been operating for 16+ years and positions itself as a full-service nursing home group.

Care offerings at residential branches include nursing care, dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative care, stroke and cancer recovery, respite admissions, handicapped adult and children care, and physiotherapy. The group also offers home nursing as a secondary service. Each residential branch maintains doctors and nurses available 24/7. The chain provides private and shared living spaces, social and recreational activities, nutritious meals, and outdoor areas across its branches.

This entry covers the Noble Care brand and head office. Individual branch profiles are listed separately in this directory for Rawang, Klang, Subang Jaya (USJ-9 and USJ-18), Jalan IPOH, and other locations. For specific pricing and availability, contact the relevant branch directly. Head office: +60-167786795 or +60-162786993.
address: No.24, Bussiness Centre, 1, Jalan USJ 9/5t, Usj 9, 47620 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: My Peaceful Home Care Centre
editorial_summary:
My Peaceful Home Care Centre occupies a 670-square-metre property at 14, Jalan SS 19/3 in the SS 19 neighbourhood of Subang Jaya, Selangor. The operator expanded with a second centre in 2022 and serves residents from the Subang Jaya, Petaling Jaya and Damansara corridor. The home offers residential elderly care, assisted living and dedicated dementia nursing care.

Two in-house physicians conduct weekly medical examinations; 24/7 trained caretakers are on duty and all rooms are fitted with call buttons. Room configurations include multi-sharing, twin-sharing and single private options, all with outdoor garden access. Operating enquiry hours are 9am to 10pm; visiting hours run 9am to 6pm.

Six Google Maps reviews average 4.8 stars, with reviewers noting well-maintained facilities and experienced, approachable staff. Pricing and bed count are not posted on the website at mypeacefulhome.com.my — request a written quote during your visit and confirm the JKM licence number at the same time.
address: 14, Jalan SS 19/3, Ss 19, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: My Joyful Home Care Centre
editorial_summary:
My Joyful Home Care Center operates from 12, Jalan SS 19/3 in the SS 19 neighbourhood of Subang Jaya, Selangor — immediately adjacent to a similarly-named home at number 14. The centre launched a professional website in early 2025 at myjoyfulhome.com.my and lists 24/7 nursing staff as a core feature.

Services span nursing home care, assisted living, dementia care, palliative care, diabetes management, respite stays and general senior residential care. Reviewers specifically mention daily doctor visits and describe a clean, homelike environment. Two contact numbers are listed: 019-329 3855 and 011-1281 2292.

Eight Google Maps reviews average 5.0 stars. Reviewers describe professional caregivers, a comfortable atmosphere and attentive day-to-day support. Pricing and bed count are not posted on the operator website — contact the centre directly for a written fee breakdown and ask to confirm the JKM licence number during your site visit.
address: 12, Jalan SS 19/3, Ss 19, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Noble Care Nursing Home | Nursing Home Subang Jaya Selangor
editorial_summary:
Noble Care at USJ 18 is one branch within Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd., a national operator running 18 locations across Malaysia. The USJ 18 facility is at 1, Jalan USJ 18/3, 47630 Subang Jaya — a mature residential area with good road access off the Federal Highway and LDP. As part of an established network, Noble Care operates under a standardised care model with corporate oversight, distinguishing it from standalone family-run homes in the same price bracket.

The website indicates the USJ 18 branch provides a broad scope of care including general senior residential care, dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative and cancer care, stroke care, respite care, and post-operative recovery. Clinical capabilities listed include catheter management, nasogastric tube changes, colostomy care, tracheostomy care, and wound dressing — a meaningful range suggesting the branch can handle medically complex residents who might otherwise require a hospital ward. Physiotherapy and cerebral palsy treatment are also listed. The facility emphasises 24/7 medical supervision and a home-like environment.

The Google rating of 3.8 across 21 reviews is the lowest among Noble Care's Klang Valley branches and is worth noting — families should read recent reviews carefully before visiting. Bed capacity is not disclosed on the website, and pricing is not published, so a consultation call (+60 16-278 6993) is needed to assess costs and availability. Because Noble Care runs multiple sites, clarify during the visit whether the USJ 18 branch retains its own permanent clinical team or whether staff rotate across branches.
address: 1, Jalan USJ 18/3, Usj 18, 47630 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Attia Global Care Centre ( Subang Jaya)
editorial_summary:
Attia Global Care Centre Subang Jaya is part of the Attia Nursing Care network, which has grown since 2011 from a single nursing home into six branches across the Klang Valley and Ipoh. The Subang Jaya branch sits at No. 7, Jalan SS 19/2 in the established SS19 neighbourhood, providing long-term assisted living care in a facility described as homely rather than clinical in character — air-conditioned rooms with attached bathrooms, a large living area, elevators for accessibility, and nurse call systems. Weekly doctor visits, hospital transfer support, and daily monitoring of blood pressure, glucose, and diabetes parameters are part of the standard offering.

The medical services at this branch are notably broad for a community nursing home: medication management, speech therapy, physiotherapy, acupuncture, wound dressing, tracheostomy management, and two-hourly repositioning to prevent pressure sores. Day care is available alongside full residential care. Recreational outings and an in-house activity programme are listed as resident engagement features. The network's longevity and multi-branch presence suggest stable operational management and the economies of scale needed to maintain consistent staffing. The SS19 location is contactable at 03-5611 0548 or WhatsApp 016-647 1674.

Visits to the centre are by appointment only — call ahead to arrange. Families should ask specifically about current capacity at this branch (not network-wide), the on-site nurse staffing ratio, whether the weekly doctor is a GP or specialist, and how complex dementia or palliative cases are managed. Attia's multi-site model means quality can vary between branches, so an in-person assessment of the SS19 premises is worthwhile before commitment. Pricing is not published; request a personalised quote based on dependency level.
address: 7, Jalan SS 19/2, Ss 19, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: My Precious Home Care Elder Care
editorial_summary:
My Precious Home Care Elder Care is a nursing home in Subang Jaya, Selangor (Taman Subang Mewah), founded by medical professionals and positioned as a clinical yet homely residential care provider. It serves residents requiring nursing care, dementia support, palliative care, and rehabilitation, and also offers home care services as a separate offering.

**Care services**
- 24-hour residential nursing care
- Dementia care
- Palliative care
- Rehabilitation support
- Home care (separate team available)
- Four meals daily (home-cooked)
- Monitoring visits by healthcare personnel

**Staffing & support**
- Founded and guided by medical professionals
- 24-hour care services confirmed
- Wheelchair accessible entrance, parking, restroom, and seating
- Assistive hearing loop available

**Capacity & pricing**
- Pricing not published on operator website — request a written quote during your visit

**License & verification**
- JKM Registered — licence number: B/PJB WT 011/2025.

**What to ask on visit**
- What is the monthly fee structure for dementia or palliative care residents?
- How are families notified in the event of a change in a resident's condition?
- What is the staff-to-resident ratio during night hours?
address: 27 Jalan USJ 1/3B, Taman Subang Mewah, Grandville, 47600 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Emerald Nursing Care Home (USJ & Subang Jaya Elderly Home)
care_types: Nursing home, Rehabilitation center, Wellness center
editorial_summary:
Emerald Nursing Care Home (USJ & Subang Jaya Elderly Home, 梳邦再也疗养院) is listed in Putra Heights — a planned township in southern Subang Jaya served by the LRT and within reasonable distance of Sunway Medical Centre and Subang Jaya Medical Centre. The Google listing categorises it as a nursing home, rehabilitation centre, and wellness centre, suggesting a broader rehabilitation-and-recovery focus alongside residential care. The home is contactable on +60 18-987 3862.

The home keeps a low online profile and does not maintain a published website or active social media page, so most service detail is best gathered directly. Care scope, bed count, staffing, and JKM registration are best confirmed on a call.

Families considering this home should call ahead to establish current admission availability and what rehabilitation programmes are run on-site. Useful questions: physiotherapy and occupational-therapy capability, registered-nurse cover, doctor-visit frequency, languages used by staff, dependency levels accepted, monthly fees, and the JKM licence number. The Putra Heights location is a practical asset for families anticipating hospital escalations.
address: Kingsley Hill, Putra Heights, 47650 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Mega Senior Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Mega Senior Care Centre is located at 29 Jalan Pantai 9/7, Seksyen 9, 46000 Petaling Jaya — a shophouse unit in a quiet residential street in PJ's older, well-established Section 9 neighbourhood. Despite the Google Maps area tag of 'Sek 9', the address firmly places it in Petaling Jaya rather than Shah Alam. The centre holds a perfect 5-star Google rating from 35 reviews, a signal worth noting — though a small review base means individual experiences carry significant weight in that average. The website positions the centre across three service categories: retirement living, dementia and palliative care, and home nursing.

The centre's website describes 24-hour care by certified caregivers, four daily home-cooked meals, senior-friendly facilities, and a daily activity programme of gentle exercise and interactive games. Cultural celebrations — Chinese New Year, Mother's Day — and intergenerational visits from local university students suggest an active social calendar. The dementia and palliative care streams appear to be genuine offerings rather than marketing labels, though the clinical depth of each is not independently documented beyond what the website states.

Key practical gaps: bed capacity, pricing, staffing qualifications, and JKM licence details are all absent from the public record. The website names individual staff members (Emie, Siri, Kay, Momo, Pearl) and visitor reviews speak warmly of their attentiveness — but families should ask for written confirmation of nurse-to-resident ratios, whether a GP visits on a fixed schedule, and what the escalation pathway is if a resident deteriorates overnight. The home nursing arm is worth enquiring about separately if your family member is not yet ready for full residential admission.
address: 29, Jalan Pantai 9/7, Sek 9, 46000 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: PJ Care Centre
editorial_summary:
PJ Care Centre's Seksyen 14 branch is a deliberately low-density nursing home at 20 Jalan 14/30, in the mature Seksyen 14 neighbourhood of Petaling Jaya. The centre caps its occupancy at 13 residents across a 4,000-plus sq ft property — a choice that prioritises privacy and one-to-one attention over throughput. Room configurations range from 3-bed sharing to single en-suite options, giving families a meaningful range of privacy levels within a single facility. The Google Maps listing carries a 4.4 rating from seven reviews. PJ Care Centre also operates a second branch, meaning it has institutional continuity beyond a single-owner setup.

Care at Seksyen 14 includes 24-hour monitoring by qualified caregivers covering bathing, grooming, feeding, and mobility assistance. Daily home-cooked meals run from breakfast through dessert with fresh fruit, and dietary consultation is available. Medication management is handled by what the facility describes as a licensed geriatric pharmacist — an unusual and noteworthy capability for a home of this size. The outdoor garden produces organic fruit and vegetables and doubles as an activity space, with residents participating in group exercise, dancing, and mahjong. On-site resident nurses are mentioned, though exact nurse-to-resident ratios are not published.

With a 13-person cap, prospective families should expect a waitlist and should enquire early. Pricing is not advertised; given the upscale positioning and low density, fees are likely above mid-market. Clarify whether in-house nursing extends to residents requiring IV medication, wound care, or nasogastric tube management, as the published scope focuses more on quality-of-life programming than clinical complexity. The Seksyen 14 central location places it within a few minutes of Pantai Hospital Kuala Lumpur and PJ city amenities.
address: 20, Jalan 14/30, Seksyen 14, 46100 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ @ My Manor
care_types: Retirement home, Home health care service
editorial_summary:
My Aged Care's My Manor is the flagship property of My Aged Care Sdn Bhd, a licensed elder care operator in Petaling Jaya described on its own website as established in 2008. My Manor sits behind the MBPJ City Council building in PJS 7, occupies roughly 10,000 square feet, and is built around a low-density concept — fewer residents per floor than typical multi-storey nursing homes. The company now runs five Petaling Jaya locations in total, with My Manor being the original.

Care tiers at My Manor span general assisted living, bedridden care, post-stroke rehabilitation, post-operative recovery, and palliative care. The website states 24-hour care with hourly rounds and a doctor on-call arrangement. Six meals daily and laundry are included. The facility also offers home nursing and ambulance services as an extension of its main care offering. An in-house physiotherapy programme supports residents recovering from stroke or surgery.

Pricing was not published on the facility's own site, but third-party sources list room options across the My Aged Care network ranging from RM 3,001 to RM 5,000 per month depending on room type and care level. Families considering My Manor should confirm current bed availability directly — the low-density model means capacity is limited. Ask specifically about the doctor on-call arrangement (response times, whether a GP or specialist), nurse-to-resident ratios at night, and JKM registration status.
address: 27, Jalan Taman 7/6, Pjs 7, 46050 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Mega Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Mega Nursing Home is located at No. 1, Jalan 14/47A in Taman Aman, Petaling Jaya — a mature residential neighbourhood close to the Federal Highway, about 3 km from Pantai Hospital and accessible to SJMC. The facility is classified as both a nursing home and assisted living facility, indicating it caters to a spectrum from ambulant residents needing support through to fully dependent cases. Its description emphasises a home-like environment with a serene courtyard, wet-room ensuite bedrooms, and a large multi-functional room — language that prioritises comfort without claiming a resort finish.

Mega Nursing Home provides 24-hour specialist care, with access to trained staff covering nursing, pharmacy, and physician services along with on-site diagnostic capabilities. Residents include individuals living with dementia, diabetes, hypertension, and depression, and the facility is reported to care for residents aged over 100 years — suggesting comfort with long-term, complex chronic care. Religious, laundry, and housekeeping services are included, and a daily activities calendar covers exercise and recreational programming. A second phone number (+60 12-777 5582) appears in some directories alongside the number in our data.

Only two Google reviews (5 stars) are available, which limits independent verification. Pricing is not publicly listed. Families should clarify what "physician services" means in practice — whether a doctor is on-site daily or visits periodically — and confirm the extent of dementia-specific capabilities, including whether they have a designated dementia unit or manage it in a mixed-ward setting. Asking about night nursing ratios and escalation protocols will help gauge how they handle medical emergencies.
address: 1, Lorong 14/47a, Taman Aman, 46100 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Unique elderly care centre
editorial_summary:
Unique Elderly Care Centre is based at No. 22, Jalan 17/1 in Seksyen 17, Petaling Jaya — a well-connected residential address close to both the LDP and Federal Highway, with Assunta Hospital and Pantai Medical Centre within 10–15 minutes. The centre operates 24 hours daily and explicitly lists a broad clinical capability set on its website, making it one of the more transparent facilities in the Petaling Jaya market.
