title: Homage Malaysia
editorial_summary:
Homage is Malaysia's largest app-based home care platform, connecting families with trained caregivers, licensed nurses, and certified therapists for care delivered in the client's own home. The service operates in Kuala Lumpur, Selangor, Johor, Penang, Perak (Ipoh), and Kedah. Booking is done through the Homage app (iOS and Android), which shows each Care Pro's qualifications and experience. Care can start within five working days of confirmed booking; 24-hour care is available using two Care Pros in rotating 12-hour shifts. The Care Pro pool is described as 3,000+ Malaysians, with a less-than-5% acceptance rate and a 4.8-star professional rating.

Services cover three categories: Home Personal Care (ADLs — bathing, dressing, toileting, transfers, meal preparation, companionship), Home Nursing by licensed nurses (wound care, NGT feeding, catheter, stoma, medication administration, post-surgery procedures), and Home Therapy by certified therapists (physiotherapy, occupational therapy, speech therapy). Specialised programmes are available for dementia, stroke rehabilitation, cancer care, Parkinson's disease, palliative care, and end-of-life support. Hospital partners include Qualitas, SunMed, and CVSKL.

Published floor rates are: daily living care from RM30/hour (certified caregivers), home nursing from RM36/hour (licensed nurses), and home therapy from RM135/hour (therapists). Multi-session packages reduce the per-hour cost — speak with a Care Advisor for package pricing. Homage is best suited to families who want flexible, on-demand care — from a single hour to full 24-hour cover — without committing to a residential facility.

title: Care Concierge Malaysia
pricing_display: From RM235/day (live-in caregiver); from RM200 for 4h visit; RM10,800/mo (stroke/dementia bundle)
editorial_summary:
Care Concierge is a Malaysian senior care specialist offering home-based caregiving, assisted living residences, and senior day care under one brand. The company focuses on specialised care for Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, dementia, cancer, and post-stroke and post-surgery recovery. At-home services are available in Penang, Perak (Ipoh), KL, Selangor, Melaka, Negeri Sembilan, and Johor. Assisted living residences are located in KL, Petaling Jaya, Penang, and Kuching, Sarawak. A senior day care programme and the company's own care-tracking app round out the offering.

Care Concierge publishes its full price list online, which is unusual in the Malaysian home care market. The Care Plus live-in package starts from RM235/day on a 20-day monthly plan (RM300/day on a daily basis). Flexible Flexi Care visits start from RM200 for a 4-hour plan. Comprehensive specialist bundles for stroke and dementia are priced at RM10,800/month — this includes a nurse/care manager, a neurologist consultation, physiotherapy three times weekly, and a full month of professional caregiving. A Doctor House Call service is available at RM300/visit (Medical Officer) or RM500/visit (Specialist). Physiotherapy sessions are RM200 each, occupational therapy RM180, and speech therapy RM300 for 45 minutes.

Care Concierge is one of the few Malaysian operators that bridges home care and residential care under one roof, which is useful for families not yet certain which route to take. The published price schedule makes it straightforward to compare costs against nursing home placement. Reach them at +603 2724 3828 or via WhatsApp at 60189815128.
address: A-1-5, Block A, Jaya One, 72A, Jln Profesor Diraja Ungku Aziz, Pjs 13, 46200 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: Noble Care Malaysia
editorial_summary:
Noble Care Malaysia is one of the country's larger managed eldercare networks, operating more than 16 nursing homes across West Malaysia and positioning its home nursing arm as a complement to its residential portfolio. For families in Kuala Lumpur who need professional care delivered at home rather than a bed in a facility, the home nursing service sends trained nurses and caregivers to the client's residence — covering everything from post-surgical wound care and medication management to chronic disease monitoring, physiotherapy, and palliative support. The company's main website is mynoblecare.com; the number in our dataset (03-7728 2886) should be verified on first contact as Noble Care's published lines are 016-778 6795 and 016-278 6993.

The home nursing service is clinically broad. Published capabilities include IV therapy, Foley's catheter care, nasogastric tube management, tracheostomy care, colostomy bag changes, bedsore dressing, and occupational and speech therapy — a list that extends meaningfully into complex medical territory beyond basic companionship. Caregivers and nurses are described as certified, and the organisation emphasises personalised care plans adjusted to the client's specific medical condition. Arrangements are available on a full-time, part-time, or hourly basis, which gives families flexibility in building a care package around existing family capacity.

Pricing is not published on the website and is quoted after a free consultation — a standard approach in Malaysia's home care sector where needs vary significantly. Families should ask at consultation about the qualifications and registration status of nurses assigned to complex cases (particularly for tracheostomy or PEG tube management), the process for replacing a carer who is absent or not a good fit, and how medical escalations are handled when the carer is on-site. Noble Care's scale means it has an established operational framework, but families should still request references or Google Maps reviews for the specific service area relevant to their home.

title: Pillar Care
pricing_display: RM4,600 / 20 working days (home care or nursing support)
editorial_summary:
Pillar Care (Pillar Health Sdn Bhd) is a Petaling Jaya–based home care agency delivering in-home personal care and nursing services for elderly Malaysians. Operating from PJ21 Commercial Centre in SS3, the company places trained caregivers and private duty nurses who visit clients at home. Services cover companion care, personal care assistance (ADLs), senior transportation, medication management, and full home nursing procedures including wound dressing, nasogastric tube care, catheter management, and stoma care. Private duty nurses are available around the clock, 24 hours a day, seven days a week, for both scheduled visits and hourly care.

Specialised programmes are offered for dementia, stroke, Parkinson's, diabetes, cancer, multiple sclerosis, palliative care, and end-of-life care. The company states that 8,582 Malaysian families have used their service, and all Care Specialists go through training reviewed by external auditors. Published pricing is RM4,600 for a 20-working-day package — equivalent to approximately RM230/day — applicable to both home care and home nursing support. The company notes that hourly rates from other providers typically run RM30–RM50/hour by comparison.

Pillar suits Klang Valley families who want a committed package-based arrangement rather than ad-hoc app bookings. The standard billing unit is a 20-day care block. Contact at +6018 2046966 or hello@pillarcare.com; the active website is pillarcare.com (the .com.my variant redirects).

title: WhereWeCare
pricing_display: From RM3,700/28 days (live-in caregiver); RM1,200/week; RM200/day
editorial_summary:
WhereWeCare is a Malaysian home care agency placing certified caregivers and registered nurses for in-home elderly care across major Malaysian cities — Kuala Lumpur, Penang, Johor Bahru, Ipoh, Melaka, and Perak, with nationwide coverage on request. The agency operates on a placement model: it assesses care needs, matches a suitable caregiver, and manages the ongoing arrangement. Key features include no long-term contracts, free carer replacement if needed, and transparent published pricing with no hidden costs. The company cites 500+ satisfied clients and over 10 years of experience in elderly care.

Services focus on live-in caregiving arrangements. All plans cover nutritious meal preparation, mobility support, physiotherapy exercises, bedsore prevention, medical appointment accompaniment, and companionship. Specialised programmes are available for dementia, palliative care, post-surgery recovery, and customised wellness plans including nutrition guidance and mental health support. Registered nurses are also available for nursing-level procedures at home.

Published pricing: the 28-day live-in package starts from RM3,700 per four weeks (approximately RM132/day), the 7-day package from RM1,200/week, and daily bookings from RM200/day. The monthly package price is competitive against shared-room nursing home rates in the Klang Valley, making it a genuine option for seniors who can manage at home with round-the-clock support. Enquire via WhatsApp at +60 11-6196 3941 or visit wherewecare.com.

title: Komune Care Senior Day Club
editorial_summary:
Komune Care Senior Day Club operates within Komune Living & Wellness, an integrated multi-generational development built by UOA Group in Bandar Tun Razak, Cheras — one of the most distinctive senior living concepts in Malaysia. The address is Unit M-03A, 21 Jalan Tasik Permaisuri 2, 56000 Cheras, Kuala Lumpur. Komune Care itself is a joint venture between UOA and Care Concierge, an established home care operator, which means the day club and assisted living suites are backed by a specialist clinical partner rather than managed purely as a property amenity. The day club occupies 6,600 sq ft and is marketed as the largest senior daycare facility in Malaysia.

The service offer across the Komune Care platform is substantial. For day club members, structured programming includes occupational therapist-designed activities, music therapy, daily morning and evening exercises, weekly excursions, and shuttle transfers to a nearby mall. The adjacent Komune Living building houses medical infrastructure through UMH Healthcare — covering general practice, dental, ambulatory health screening, and medical aesthetics — as well as Traditional Chinese Medicine consultation and treatment through Tong Xin Tang, giving day club members access to a layered wellness ecosystem. For residents choosing assisted living suites, 24-hour nursing and caregiver support is available, and the facility has stated capacity for residents managing dementia, stroke recovery, post-hospitalisation care, cancer care, and complex comorbidities. Published pricing: assisted living suites from RM 6,800 per month; day club membership from RM 3,000–3,500 per month.

Komune Care is best suited to seniors who will benefit from a structured, socially active day programme or who need assisted living with a lifestyle component rather than a purely clinical environment. Families should ask on enquiry about clinical escalation protocols — specifically how a medical emergency during a day club session is managed, whether in-house nursing is present during day club hours, and how waitlists for assisted living suites are structured. The RM 500,000 Senior Care Fund established by UOA to subsidise eligible lower-income families is also worth raising if cost is a consideration.

title: Merry Care Kepong Baru 3
editorial_summary:
Merry Care Kepong Baru 3 is one of six branches in the Merry Care chain, located at No.1, Jalan 9, Taman Kepong, 52100 Kuala Lumpur. Merry Care is a six-branch nursing home chain operating across Kepong Baru, Desa Jaya, and Selayang Baru in the Klang Valley. The group markets itself at elderlycare.my and positions on the affordable end of the residential care market — a deliberate point of difference against private retirement homes that typically start north of RM 4,000 per month. With multiple homes clustered within walking distance of each other in Kepong Baru, families touring the chain often visit two or three branches in a single afternoon.

The operator publishes the same service bundle across all branches: medication management, daily items purchasing, hospital visitation, daily fitness activities, a 24-hour assistant on duty, and five meals a day with unlimited servings on request. Pricing is published on the operator website (elderlycare.my) starting from RM 2,500 per month for a shared room — a starting figure that, in practice, varies with the resident's actual care needs and dependency level. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

Kepong Baru is a dense residential pocket in north-Kuala Lumpur with good access to Hospital Sungai Buloh and the Damansara hospital cluster (KPJ Damansara, Sunway, ParkCity Medical Centre). The neighbourhood is tightly built — terrace-house lots, narrow back lanes — so families should ask on a visit how natural light, communal space, and any outdoor compound are handled, how many residents share a room, how many caregivers cover each shift, whether a registered nurse is on-site overnight or on-call, and what specific clinical procedures the home performs in-house versus refers out (PEG feeds, oxygen, wound dressings, urinary catheter changes). Confirm the RM 2,500 starting rate matches the actual care plan since add-ons can shift the monthly figure.
address: 42, Jalan Antoi 2, Kepong Baru, 52100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

title: Sunway Sanctuary
editorial_summary:
Sunway Sanctuary is the senior-living arm of Sunway Group's integrated township in Bandar Sunway, Selangor - a purpose-built community offering hotel-grade apartments and suites for older adults who want to remain independent while having medical backup nearby. The community is annexed to Sunway Medical Centre, which is the practical reason most families consider it: residents have priority access to one of the country's largest private hospitals without leaving the compound. Suites are configured for ageing-in-place - wide doorways, level thresholds, grab rails as standard - and the campus shares amenities with the wider Sunway township, putting the pyramid mall, monorail, and parks within a covered walkway. 

Daily life leans active rather than clinical. Residents have a calendar of group exercise, art and music programmes, and outings; meals are served in a communal dining room with dietary options. The community is licensed and openly published as part of Sunway's healthcare arm, which is unusual transparency for the segment and helpful for families used to opaque smaller operators. 

Pricing sits at the premium end of the Klang Valley AL market - expect five-figure monthly fees with the model evolving (some suites have been offered as memberships rather than month-to-month rentals), so the financial model is the question to ask first on a tour. Other call-time questions: what happens if a resident's care needs escalate beyond AL (does the community have a nursing wing, or is the transfer path to Sunway Medical and then out), what is and isn't included in the fee (utilities, meal tiers, additional carer hours), and minimum tenancy.
address: 5, Jalan Lagoon Selatan, Bandar Sunway, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

title: ReU Living
editorial_summary:
ReU Living is a hybrid senior-living and post-hospitalisation residence on the edge of KLCC, positioned by its operators as a 'hotel-in-a-hospital' concept. It serves two adjacent populations under one roof: older adults who want serviced apartment living with concierge medical support, and patients in the post-acute window who need structured recovery — typically post-stroke, post-surgical, or after a chemotherapy cycle — without staying on a hospital ward. The dual-track model is deliberate, and worth understanding before booking, because it shapes everything from the staffing pattern to the daily rhythm. 

Suites are hotel-style with private bathrooms and city views; residents have access to in-house Traditional Chinese Medicine, physiotherapy, and a doctor on call. Meals follow tiered dietary plans (Western, Asian, therapeutic) and the concierge layer covers the small things — laundry, transport to appointments, guest hosting — that families end up doing themselves at less integrated communities. The KLCC location is genuinely useful: most major specialists and private hospitals are within ten minutes, which matters more for the post-hospitalisation track than the lifestyle one. 

Pricing is premium, billed monthly, with shorter post-acute stays priced differently from long-stay residency — confirm both rates and what's bundled. Useful call-time questions: how is care escalation handled (is there an in-house nursing capability for higher acuity or is transfer to a partnered hospital the expected path), what's the actual mix of long-stay vs. short-stay residents on a typical floor, and how are meal/care additions billed.

title: 'Love And Care' Care Centre
editorial_summary:

It operates from Laluan Menglembu Barat 4 and is governed as a persatuan kebajikan (welfare association), indicating community or non-profit management rather than a private commercial model. Wheelchair-accessible entrance and parking are confirmed.

The centre does not maintain a public website, and key operational details including bed count, care levels, nursing staffing, and pricing have not been published through any verified channel. Google Maps lists office hours of 9 AM to 5 PM daily. Families should speak directly with staff to understand the level of care provided, whether medical supervision is available, and how the home handles higher-dependency residents.

Google reviews stand at 4.7★ across 6 reviews — too few to identify recurring trends, though the consistent ratings suggest families who have used the centre have been satisfied.

**What to ask on visit:**
- What care levels does the home accept — independent seniors, assisted daily living, or higher-dependency residents?
- Is a nurse or qualified caregiver on site at all times, including overnight?
- What are the visiting hours beyond the 9 AM–5 PM office hours listed on Google?
- What is the monthly fee and what does it include?
- Is the home registered with JKM or MOH, and can I see the registration certificate?
- How many residents does the home currently accommodate?
- What happens if a resident’s condition deteriorates and hospital care is needed?
address: 112, Laluan Menglembu Barat 4, 31450 Menglembu, Perak, Malaysia

The centre has been operating since 1984, founded by Mrs Yap as a small home for seniors in a rented bungalow on Jalan Woodward. Today it operates under the Swiftcare Integrated Group with a licensed capacity of 140 residents — one of the larger private nursing facilities in the Kinta Valley. JKM licence A/PJB WT 054/2022.

**Services (from myplace.com.my):**

- Day care
- Outsourced nursing

Residents have access to tailored exercise programmes and gardening activities; meals are adjusted to health and dietary needs. Nursing staff are registered with the Malaysia Ministry of Health. A visiting doctor attends monthly.

**What reviewers say (Google, 25 reviews, 4.5★):**

- Staff described as well-trained and caring, with good continuity (named individually in several reviews)
- Spacious rooms and garden highlighted as a draw
- Balanced, nutritious meals noted; accepted for stroke and bedridden patients

Pricing is not published — contact the admissions team for a quote based on care level. Visiting hours are not published on the website; confirm when booking a viewing. Full details at **[myplace.com.my](https://www.myplace.com.my/)** and on Facebook at **[My Place Care Centre](https://www.facebook.com/myplacecarecentre/)**.

The home is notable for its spacious, airy layout — reviewers frequently describe the environment as well-kept and serene, with adequate ventilation and room for residents to move around. Staff have shown capacity to manage post-hospitalisation recovery; one family noted their father returned to the home after two separate hospital admissions for COPD and pneumonia, with the team coordinating his care during the transition back from ward to residential setting.

Teluk Intan sits in the Hilir Perak district, with Hospital Teluk Intan as the main public referral centre for acute and specialist needs. Families based in Bidor, Sungkai, or further south often consider Anson Home because it is one of the longer-established centres in the town.

**What reviewers say** (Google, 5 reviews — small written sample): The written feedback skews very positive across multiple reviewers. One family member describes being grateful to the director, caregivers and nurses for looking after their aunt for around six years, citing kind nursing staff. Another reports their family member has lived there for two years and gets along well with both staff and other residents. A third notes their mother's spirits and health improved over a two-month stay and praises the team's attentive care.

Pricing, visiting hours, room options, and bed availability are not publicly published. Call +60 12-592 6058 to enquire. Connect on Facebook at facebook.com/p/Anson-Home-Care-Centre-100067177830193.

**What to ask on the visit:**
- Monthly fees for shared vs single rooms, and what is included (diapers, milk, laundry, consumables)
- Nurse-to-resident ratio by shift, and whether a registered nurse is on duty overnight
- Doctor visit frequency, and which clinic or hospital handles emergencies and routine reviews
- Current bed availability and typical waiting time
- Daily activity schedule and whether residents are taken outdoors
- Languages spoken by frontline caregivers
- How complaints or concerns are documented and escalated

De Home Care Centre is a JKM-registered residential elderly care home at 5, Lorong Taman Ipoh 2, Taman Ipoh Selatan, 31400 Ipoh, Perak, Malaysia. It holds JKM licence A/PJB WT 022/2020 (registered in 2020). The home operates in the Lorong Taman Ipoh 2 area of Perak.

**Services (from JKM registration):**
- JKM-licensed residential elderly care (Pusat Jagaan Berdaftar, Warga Tua category)

The location is served by Hospital Raja Permaisuri Bainun, Hospital Fatimah, and KPJ Ipoh Specialist Hospital — useful for families managing residents with ongoing medical needs requiring outpatient follow-up or unplanned hospital admissions. Detailed information about staffing, capacity, room layout, and activities is best obtained by visiting the centre and speaking with management directly.

**What reviewers say** (Google: 4.4★, 7 reviews): One reviewer notes: "Good and caring services, clean environment and helpful staffs over there. I am very satisfy and feel peace of mind let ". Reviews are largely positive across the sample.

Pricing, visiting hours, room options, and bed availability are not publicly published. Call +60165900291 to enquire. Confirm JKM licence and current registration status on visit.

**What to ask on visit:**
- What are the monthly fees, and what is included for nursing care versus daily living support?
- What is the nurse-to-resident ratio across day and night shifts, and is a registered nurse on duty overnight?
- How are residents' medical needs managed — visiting doctor, hospital referrals, overnight emergencies?
- What is current bed availability, room options, and the admission process?
- What activities and social programmes are offered to residents day-to-day?

title: Dr. Thomas Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Dr Thomas Care Centre is a purpose-built nursing home at 24 Jalan Labrooy, Taman Pari, about five minutes from central Ipoh. Opened in July 2020 as Ipoh's first multi-purpose built care centre, it was established by Dr Thomas Arul — a general practitioner with over 40 years of experience who attends on-site daily. The centre holds JKM licence A/PJB WT 045/2021.

**Services (from drthomascarecentre.com):**
- 24-hour nursing and elderly care
- Post-hospitalisation recovery
- Palliative care
- Rehabilitation (stroke and recovery)
- Physiotherapy
- Specialised care
- Medical procedures
- Day care

The facility has a capacity of 50 residents across single, double, and triple-bedded air-conditioned rooms, each with an attached bathroom. A medical consultation room is on-site. The presence of a founding GP attending daily means residents can receive medical review without repeated clinic trips — a meaningful advantage for post-operative and complex cases.

**What reviewers say (Google, 13 reviews, 4.7★):**
- Wound care management singled out by multiple reviewers as outstanding — Dr Thomas praised for clear treatment explanations and consistent follow-up
- Dr Thomas personally described as attentive and compassionate by families of long-stay residents
- Spacious, quiet, hotel-like environment consistently noted
- Post-surgery recovery stays positively described; one reviewer worked remotely during recovery

Pricing was from RM1,900/month at the 2020 launch; current rates were not published at time of this review. Visiting hours are not published — confirm when booking a visit. Full details at **[drthomascarecentre.com](https://drthomascarecentre.com/)** and on Facebook at **[Dr Thomas Care Centre](https://www.facebook.com/drthomas.carecentre/)**.

**What to ask on visit:**
- What are the current monthly fees, and what care level is covered in the base rate?
- How often does Dr Thomas attend, and who covers when he is unavailable?
- What is current bed availability for residential and day care?
- Are physiotherapy sessions included or billed separately?
- What are the visiting hours?
address: 24, Jalan Labrooy, Taman Pari, 30100 Ipoh, Perak, Malaysia

title: Edelweiss Elderly Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Edelweiss Elderly Care Centre is a residential elderly care home in Taman Muhibbah, Melaka — part of the Elders Home Care group, sharing the Taman Muhibbah cluster with its sister homes Elders Home and Elders Home Plus. The Elders Home group is run by medical professionals and describes itself as an exclusive low-occupancy retirement and nursing home. Edelweiss specifically is registered under JKM/MOH and operates from the same Taman Muhibbah complex at Jalan Taman Muhibbah, 75200 Melaka.

**Services (from eldershome.com.my):**
- Residential elderly care
- Medical and nursing supervision
- Daily physiotherapy and exercise
- Visiting doctor (weekly rounds)
- Personal care and meals

The group's published profile emphasises a low-resident-to-staff ratio and clinical oversight rather than a high-volume care model.

Google reviews for the Elders Home group at Taman Muhibbah stand at 5★ across 21 reviews — too few to identify trends specific to Edelweiss as a separate brand, but reviewers consistently describe the cluster as clean, professionally staffed, with a visiting doctor doing weekly rounds, daily physio, and a homely atmosphere.

Pricing is not published — contact for a quote. Visiting hours are not published — confirm when booking a viewing. Full details at **[eldershome.com.my](http://www.eldershome.com.my/)**.

**What to ask on visit:**
- How is Edelweiss differentiated from the other Elders Home brands at the same Taman Muhibbah address?
- What is the current occupancy and resident-to-staff ratio?
- How often does the visiting doctor attend, and what is the protocol for after-hours medical issues?
- Is a registered nurse on site overnight, and what is the day/night staffing pattern?
- What clinical conditions are accepted — dementia, bedridden, post-stroke, palliative?
- What languages are spoken by staff (Mandarin, Hokkien, BM, English)?
- Are religious or dietary preferences accommodated?

title: Eden on The Park Care Residence
editorial_summary:
Eden-on-the-Park Nursing Care Residence (喜登园养老与护理院舍） is a purpose-built nursing home on the Kuching–Samarahan Expressway at Lot 4989, Block 26, MTLD, Kota Samarahan, Sarawak. It sits alongside a sister property — Eden on The Park Active Retirement Living Resort — making it one of the most comprehensive elderly care developments in the Kuching area. The nursing residence is MOH-licensed and accepts dependent residents needing rehabilitation, dementia care, post-stroke recovery, and long-term nursing.

**Services (from eden-on-the-park.com.my):**
- 24-hour nursing care
- Post-hospital recovery and rehabilitation
- Physiotherapy
- Dementia care
- Respite care
- Palliative care

A registered nurse is on duty, and the residence operates with a structured rehabilitation and physiotherapy programme.

**What reviewers say (Google, 81 reviews, 4.4★):**
- Comfortable purpose-built environment, modern facility design
- Good physiotherapy and recovery programme reported
- Staff and nurses described as responsible and caring
- Considered a leading option in the Kuching elderly care market
- New concept in old-age care for the region

Pricing is not published in detail online — contact for a quote. Visiting hours are not published — confirm when booking a viewing. Full details at **[eden-on-the-park.com.my](https://eden-on-the-park.com.my/nursing-care-residence-kuching-samarahan/)**.

**What to ask on visit:**
- What is the monthly fee tier, and what is included versus extra (physio sessions, diapers, meals)?
- What is the resident-to-staff ratio overnight, and how many registered nurses are on each shift?
- What is the rehabilitation programme like, and who supervises it (physiotherapist, doctor)?
- What is the protocol for medical emergencies and hospital transfers from Kota Samarahan?
- What clinical conditions are accepted — bedridden, advanced dementia, dialysis, tube-feeding?
- How are family visits and overnight stays handled, especially for end-of-life care?
- Are religious or dietary preferences accommodated?

title: Elders Home Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Elders Home Care Centre is a nursing home located in Taman Muhibbah, Klebang Besar, Melaka, operated by the registered entity MA 0185845-P. The operator also runs a second Melaka branch — Elders Home Plus — in Taman Puncak Bertam, giving families two options under the same management umbrella. The Klebang Besar location serves the western corridor of Melaka Tengah, within reach of the Hospital Melaka referral network.

Residential nursing care is the primary offering, with staff accommodating elderly residents across a range of dependency levels. The facility has maintained a consistent Google presence since at least 2017, the year indicated by the operator's registered copyright. Hospital Melaka is the nearest public tertiary facility for medical escalations, and its proximity makes this location practical for families who want a nursing home close to the main referral hospital.

**What reviewers say (Google, 21 reviews, 5★):**
- Families describe the facility as comfortable and clean
- Reviewers with parents in residence report satisfaction with daily care
- Staff noted as friendly and attentive

Pricing is not published — contact the home for a quote. Visiting hours are not stated publicly — confirm when booking a visit. Full details at **[eldershome.com.my](http://www.eldershome.com.my/)**.

**What to ask on visit:**

title: Evergreen Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Evergreen Care Centre is a JKM-registered elderly care home at 107, Jalan Kompleks Sukan, Taman Bandaraya, 31400 Ipoh, Perak. It holds JKM licence A/PJB WT 047/2022. The home sits in a residential pocket close to the Ipoh sports complex on the eastern side of the city.

**Services (from JKM registration):**
- JKM-licensed residential elderly care (Pusat Jagaan Berdaftar, Warga Tua category)

Taman Bandaraya is well-connected to central Ipoh, putting families within easy reach of Hospital Raja Permaisuri Bainun (the main public hospital), Hospital Fatimah, KPJ Ipoh Specialist Hospital, and Pantai Hospital Ipoh. The neighbourhood is residential, which makes for a quieter setting than the city centre.

**What reviewers say** (Google, 4 reviews, 2 with written text — very small sample): The two written notes are brief and positive (one short comment of approval, one saying simply "I like it"), but they don't offer detail on care quality, food, or activities. With this little written commentary, families will learn far more from a visit and a frank conversation with the person in charge than from the public review history.

Pricing, visiting hours, room options, and bed availability are not publicly published. Call +60 16-526 2817 to enquire.

**What to ask on the visit:**
- Monthly fees for shared vs single rooms, and what is included
- Nurse-to-resident ratio by shift, and overnight nursing cover
- Doctor visit schedule and emergency hospital referral arrangements
- Current bed availability and typical waiting time
- Daily routine, activities, and outdoor access
- Languages spoken by frontline caregivers
- How families are kept updated on a resident's condition and care

title: Labuan Aged Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Labuan Aged Care Centre is the first dedicated elderly day-care facility in the Federal Territory of Labuan, the small island territory off the northern coast of Sabah. It holds JKM licence L/PJH WE 001/2023 — denoting Labuan's first licensed elderly care service. The centre operates from 9869, Jalan Mohammed Salleh, in Labuan town. As a small territory with an ageing local population, Labuan previously had no dedicated elder-care option, so this facility fills a real gap.

The centre's primary public presence is its Facebook page rather than a website. The model appears to be daytime activities and rehabilitation rather than 24-hour residential care — families looking for full residential nursing will likely need to consider options on the Sabah mainland.

Google reviews stand at 5★ across 1 review — too few to identify recurring trends. The single review describes it as the "first day care center in Labuan", consistent with the JKM-001/2023 licence number.

Pricing is not published — contact for a quote. Visiting hours are not published — confirm when booking a viewing. Full details on the operator Facebook page: **[Labuan Aged Care Centre on Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=100090267521584)**.

**What to ask on visit:**
- Is this a day-care service, or is overnight residential placement available?
- What is the daily activity programme — exercise, social activities, cognitive stimulation?
- What conditions are best supported — early dementia, post-stroke, general companionship?
- What is the staff-to-attendee ratio, and how many qualified caregivers are on each shift?
- Are meals included, and how are dietary needs (halal, dietary restrictions) handled?
- What is the fee structure — daily, monthly, or subsidised?

The day-care model is designed to give caregivers respite during work hours while keeping residents in a familiar home environment overnight.

**What reviewers say** (Google: 4.9★, 16 reviews): Reviews are consistently positive, with families praising the supportive environment and structured programming. The 4.9-star average across a respectable 16-review sample is unusually strong for a specialist non-profit programme in this category.

Visiting hours, fees, and admission criteria are not all listed publicly. Call +60 13-520 1220 or visit the **[Dementia Society Perak website](http://thedementiasocietyperak.wordpress.com/)** for current programmes and family support resources.

**What to ask on visit:**

title: Pusat Jagaan Kindhara Sdn Bhd
editorial_summary:
Tucked into a quiet residential street in Taman Johor Jaya, Pusat Jagaan Kindhara Sdn Bhd — branded as KindHara Senior Care Home (康和苑养老院) — offers a deliberately home-like alternative to institutional elder care. The facility operates from a terrace-house premises at Jalan Teratai 38, which keeps it embedded in a regular neighbourhood rather than set apart in a clinical complex. That setting reflects its core philosophy: "We care for every senior as family" and "Compassionate Living for Seniors" — phrases that show up consistently in their materials and that families report feeling in the day-to-day rhythm of the place.

What distinguishes KindHara from many Johor Bahru competitors is the breadth of its care tiers under one roof. Day Care is available for seniors who still live at home but benefit from structured supervision during the day. Short-term and respite stays let families take a break without a long-term commitment. For those who need a permanent home, long-stay residential placement is offered. Most notably, the team explicitly handles Specialised Care for stroke survivors and post-hospital recovery — a meaningful gap-filler for families whose relative has been discharged from Hospital Sultanah Aminah or other Johor Bahru facilities but still needs more support than home alone can provide. Staff are multilingual (English, Mandarin, and Malay), which matters in a Chinese-heritage-heavy area like Taman Johor Jaya where families often feel more at ease discussing care in their mother tongue.

KindHara holds a valid JKM licence (J/PJBWE025/2026), confirming it meets the government's minimum standards for residential elder care. The home has produced a virtual tour video — accessible via their YouTube channel — which is a practical and transparent gesture that saves families an unnecessary first visit. Enquiries can be made via WhatsApp (016-612 2927) or email (KindHara25@gmail.com). When you do visit, ask about the nurse-to-resident ratio on night shifts, what the escalation protocol is if a stroke resident deteriorates, and how therapy sessions for post-hospital recovery are structured — whether in-house or via outpatient referral.

**What to ask on visit**
- What is the carer-to-resident ratio during the day and at night?
- For stroke patients, is there an in-house physiotherapist or is therapy arranged externally?
- What does the post-hospital recovery programme look like week by week?
- How are medical emergencies handled — is there a doctor on call?
- Can we trial a day care placement before committing to a long stay?
- What is included in the monthly fee and what is charged separately?
address: 30, Jalan Teratai 38, Taman Johor Jaya, 81100 Johor Bahru, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

title: Pusat Jagaan PAWE Benoni
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Pawe Benoni is a registered nursing home in Kampung Kuala Repas, Benoni, in the Papar district of Sabah -- approximately 40 kilometres south of Kota Kinabalu. It holds JKM licence S/PJH WE/01-2026, one of the most recently issued licences in this batch, suggesting the facility is newly registered or recently renewed under the current JKM framework. The Papar district is a quieter rural area with a mix of Sabahan communities, and placements here suit families based in or near Papar who prefer a smaller, community-based setting over a city home.

Benoni is a village in the rural Papar area, so families should factor access to medical facilities into their decision -- nearby town facilities in Papar are available, but specialist care would require travel to Kota Kinabalu. Pusat Jagaan Pawe Benoni and its sister facility Pusat Jagaan Pawe Papar appear to operate under the same operator in the same district.

No operator website or public social media presence is available. All enquiries need to be made directly by phone. No Google reviews were available at the time of writing.

Pricing is not published -- contact for a quote. Visiting hours are not published -- confirm when booking a viewing.

**What to ask on visit:**
- What is the current monthly fee and what does it include?
- What nursing or personal care qualifications do the staff hold?
- What is the resident capacity, and how many residents are currently admitted?
- How are medical emergencies handled -- which hospital or clinic is used?
- What activities or social programmes are available for residents?
- Is the JKM licence (S/PJH WE/01-2026) displayed on-site?

editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Persatuan Kebajikan Buddhist Than Hsiang Kedah is listed in the JKM elderly care register as a Kedah-state branch of the Than Hsiang Buddhist welfare network, operating under licence K/PJB WE004/2025. The Than Hsiang umbrella covers a Buddhist temple, community welfare society and several elderly care projects, the best known being Wan Ching Yuan, the Buddhist-run care home in Sungai Ara, Bayan Lepas, Pulau Pinang. The Kedah branch is not separately mapped on Google at the time of writing, so the exact address, capacity, and operating arrangement for this Kedah facility should be confirmed directly before any visit.

Than Hsiang as an organisation publishes information about its Penang services at thanhsiang.org. The Kedah branch is not described on that site, which is a gap families should be aware of: a JKM licence confirms registration but not the on-the-ground service profile. As a Buddhist welfare-society home, expect a community-funded fee structure rather than commercial pricing, and a culturally Chinese-Buddhist environment.

Google reviews for the Kedah branch are not available — too few or none recorded under this name at present.

Pricing is not published — contact for a quote. Visiting hours are not published — confirm when booking a viewing. For a comparison with the network's Penang home, see Wan Ching Yuan in Sungai Ara.

**What to ask on visit:**
- What is the exact address and contact in Kedah, and is the facility currently accepting new residents?
- Is this a sister-home of Wan Ching Yuan in Penang, or a separate operation?

editorial_summary:

The association was founded in 1994 under the guidance of Venerable Wei Wu, with the Sungai Ara branch located at 5 Jalan Sungai Ara, Bayan Lepas, Penang. This facility is JKM Registered under licence number K/PJB WE004/2025 and is linked to the Than Hsiang Temple complex.

The facility accommodates up to 27 residents at its Sungai Ara location. Care is provided by qualified staff nurses and workers, assisted by volunteers. The facility offers residential care for elderly residents alongside day care and respite care options.

The association operates a subsidised fee model: residents without means are accepted at no charge, while others contribute a monthly donation of RM 450. This makes it one of the most affordable registered elder care options associated with an established charitable organisation.

title: Pusat Jagaan Rumah Orang-Orang Tua (PKK) Simee
visiting_hours: Mon–Sun & Public Holidays, 8:30am–5:30pm
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Rumah Orang-Orang Tua (PKK) Simee — known locally as the Kampung Simee Home for the Aged — is a charitable residential home at Lot 157862 Block A, Jalan Kompleks Sukan, Ipoh. Founded in 1952 by Rev. Fr. Anthony Khaw as a modest wooden structure, the home has grown over seven decades into two modern three-storey buildings accommodating residents of all races and religions. It holds JKM licence A/PJB WT 046/2023.

**Services (from kgsimeehome.com and operator-published facts):**
- Charitable residential care for elderly without family support
- Basic on-site medical support with hospital referrals as needed
- Daily meals, personal care, and a community hall for social activities
- Programmes run with partner organisations through CSR visits and events

The home currently cares for around 39 elderly residents with a staff team of 12 supported by three regular volunteers. Admissions are reviewed by a selection committee that assesses eligibility on a needs basis. The 2024 annual operating budget is approximately RM618,000, funded entirely through public donations — no residential fee is charged for eligible residents, which makes the home a meaningful safety-net option for low-income families with no alternative.

**What reviewers say** (Google: 3.8★, 5 reviews): Reviews are mostly star-only without substantive written content, and the small sample makes it hard to identify recurring themes. A visit will tell families more than the public review history; the home's seven-decade track record and committee-based admissions process speak more clearly than the numerical score.

Visiting hours are Monday to Sunday and public holidays, 8:30am–5:30pm. Full details at **[kgsimeehome.com](https://www.kgsimeehome.com/)** and on Facebook at **[Pusat Jagaan PKK Simee](https://www.facebook.com/p/Pusat-Jagaan-Rumah-Orang-Orang-Tua-PKK-Simee-100064488590548/)**.

**What to ask on visit:**
- What are the eligibility criteria — must an applicant demonstrate no family support, and what documentation is needed?
- How long is the typical waiting list, and how does the selection committee work?
- What on-site medical care is available, and how are hospital referrals and emergencies handled?
- What activities and social programmes are offered to residents day-to-day?
- How can families or organisations contribute through donations or CSR partnerships?
address: Lot. 157862, Block A, Jln Kompleks Sukan, Kampung Simee, 31400 Ipoh, Perak, Malaysia

Bohor Mali sits in the Simpang Ampat area of Perlis, a quiet rural region with limited commercial elderly care options, where welfare-society homes fill a real gap.

- Visitors describe a hands-on community-supported environment

title: Pusat Jagaan Trinity Eldercare Berhad
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Trinity Eldercare Berhad is an elderly day-care and respite centre at Lot 59, Jalan Ellis, 93300 Kuching, Sarawak. It is operated by Trinity Methodist Church Kuching as a community service — the day centre is run by trained staff with volunteer support, and the operator publishes its own programme at trinity-eldercare.com. The centre is structured to serve seniors who live at home but need daytime supervision, social interaction, and rehabilitation rather than overnight residential placement.

**Services (from trinity-eldercare.com):**
- Day care for seniors
- Cognitive stimulation and activities (group programmes weekday mornings)
- Support for caregivers of dementia and stroke recovery patients
- Volunteer-supported community programme
- Methodist church-run charitable service

Families and caregivers of seniors with Alzheimer's, mild dementia, or post-stroke rehabilitation needs are the primary user group reflected in published reviews.

**What reviewers say (Google, 22 reviews, 5★):**
- Families report significant relief from caregiver burden — particularly for dementia and stroke recovery
- Residents become more cheerful and socially engaged after enrolling
- Staff described as caring, passionate, and patient with elders
- Mornings include group activities; volunteers actively involved
- Long-term placements common — multi-year users reported

Pricing is not published in detail — contact for a quote. Visiting hours are not published — confirm when booking. Full details at **[trinity-eldercare.com](https://trinity-eldercare.com/)**.

editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Yayasan Teratai is an aged-care nursing home operated by Yayasan Teratai, a charitable foundation incorporated in Ipoh on 14 April 2001 as a Public Company Limited by Guarantee. Beyond running its own care centre, the foundation donates over RM700,000 annually to 13 aged-care organisations, orphanages, and 20 healthcare bodies across the Kinta Valley. The centre holds JKM licence A/PJB WT 041/2022 and is located near KPJ Ipoh Specialist Hospital. Main office address: 16 Jalan Raja Dihilir, 30350 Ipoh, Perak Darul Ridzuan.

**Services (from yayasanteratai.org.my and operator-published facts):**
- 24-hour residential aged care

- Annual dinners and festive celebrations for residents
- Health & Aged Care, Education Care, and broader community programmes run by the foundation

The home accepts residents aged 50 and above who are semi-mobile, with day-to-day care provided by a team of retired nurses and professional caregivers. Much of the foundation's website is login-gated for members and donors, so granular detail on rooms, fees, and admission criteria is best obtained by contacting the centre directly.

**What reviewers say** (Google: 4.5★, 2 reviews): Both reviews are star-only without written commentary, so there is no usable narrative from the public record. The foundation's incorporated status, JKM registration, and visible charitable activity across Perak speak more strongly than the small review sample.

Visiting hours are reported as 10:00am–4:00pm daily. Pricing and detailed services are not published publicly — contact the centre on +60 16-534 5679 or via **[yayasanteratai.org.my](https://www.yayasanteratai.org.my/)** for current availability.

**What to ask on visit:**

title: Starshine Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Starshine Care Centre is a nursing home in Taman Sin Lok, central Ipoh, at No 65, Jalan Sin Lok (also published as Jalan Kuala Kangsar), 30010 Ipoh. The centre serves both short-stay rehabilitation and longer-term residential care for seniors with chronic conditions. All rooms are air-conditioned and furnished; the building has 24-hour CCTV, a nurse call system, WiFi, and Astro TV throughout. The centre holds JKM licences A/PJB WT 025/2024 and A/PJB WT 057/2024.

**Services (from starshinecare.com):**
- 24-hour nursing care
- Doctor visits (regular, not just on-call)
- Physiotherapy
- Day care
- Assisted living
- Rehabilitation
- Medical nursing care for chronic conditions
- Retirement care

The presence of a regular doctor visit service — rather than only nursing cover — means residents can receive medical review without repeated hospital trips, which matters for families managing chronic conditions from a distance. Residents also have access to organised activities including outdoor walks, board games (chess, mahjong, cards, bingo), and social gatherings. Meals are personalised to individual health needs and dietary restrictions.

**What reviewers say** (Google: 5★, 9 reviews): The home maintains a perfect five-star average across a small but consistent reviewer base. Most reviews are star-only without written narrative, so families will learn more from a visit and direct conversation with the team than from the public review trail.

Pricing is not published. Visiting hours are not published — confirm when booking a viewing. Full details at **[starshinecare.com](https://www.starshinecare.com/)** and on Facebook at **[facebook.com/StarshineCareCentre](https://www.facebook.com/61558588282494)**.

**What to ask on visit:**
- What is the monthly fee for residential care, day care, and short-stay rehabilitation, and what is included?
- How frequently does the visiting doctor attend, and what triggers an emergency hospital referral?
- What is current bed availability and the typical waiting time?
- When was the last JKM inspection and can you share the result?
- What rehabilitation programmes are delivered in-house versus referred out?
- How are dietary and religious meal needs managed across residents?

title: Young Heart Senior Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Young Heart Senior Care Centre (童心乐龄日托休闲中心) sits inside Horizon Hills, one of Iskandar Puteri's most established gated townships, at Jalan Horizon Perdana 3. The name says it plainly: 日托 (day care) and 休闲中心 (leisure centre). This is not a residential nursing home — it is a weekday day care programme running Monday to Friday, 8 AM to 6 PM, designed for families where working adults leave for the office and do not want an elderly parent spending the day alone at home, disconnected and sedentary. The centre operates with an explicit Chinese-community orientation, with staff communicating comfortably in Mandarin and family reviews written in both English and Chinese.

What families describe in the reviews makes the value proposition clear. One reviewer's mother had stopped wearing her hearing aids at home and was deteriorating quietly — after three days at Young Heart she put them back on, her communication improved, and her energy returned. Another notes the shift from isolated scrolling at home to eating together, joining activities, and being part of what multiple reviewers call a "big family" atmosphere. The centre provides lunch and afternoon tea daily, cooked in-house, with multiple reviewers calling out the food as healthy and delicious. Group activities form the backbone of the daily schedule — the specifics vary, but the consistent theme across ten five-star reviews is that residents come home more alert and engaged than when they left. The facility itself is described as clean, bright, and well-maintained, set within the well-resourced Horizon Hills township.

Young Heart carries a Google rating of 5.0 across 10 reviews — an unusually clean score for a centre of its size. It sits in a part of Iskandar Puteri that has relatively few senior day care options relative to its large upper-middle-class population of working families with ageing parents. The operating hours of 8 AM to 6 PM cover a standard working day neatly, though it is worth confirming whether there is any flexibility at the edges or transport arrangements if you need them. When you visit, bring specific questions about how the day is structured for different ability levels, what happens when a resident is unwell, and whether the centre closes on public holidays.

**What to ask on visit**
- What does a typical day look like, hour by hour, and how is it adapted for different mobility or cognitive levels?
- Can you accommodate residents with mild dementia or early cognitive decline?
- Is there a nurse or trained first-aider on site at all times?
- Are there transport arrangements, or is it drop-off and pick-up only?
- Does the centre close on public holidays or during school holidays?
- Can we arrange a trial day before committing to a regular schedule?
address: 22, 22-01, Jalan Horizon Perdana 3, Horizon Hills Iskandar Puteri, 79100 Johor Bahru, Johor Darul Ta'zim, Malaysia

editorial_summary:
The Synod of the Diocese of West Malaysia Care Centre is a JKM-registered care home in the Bukit Bintang area of Kuala Terengganu, operated under the Anglican Diocese of West Malaysia. The Diocese runs Anglican Care as one of its pastoral ministries, and this centre represents the Anglican Church's elderly welfare work in Terengganu. JKM licence number T/PJB WE 005/2024 is on file — a recent registration.

Anglican Diocese care homes in Malaysia are typically open to residents of all faiths, not restricted to members of the Anglican community. The Synod of the Diocese of West Malaysia administers the Church of England-affiliated congregations across Peninsular Malaysia and Singapore, making this one of the few Anglican-run care facilities outside the Klang Valley.

No dedicated facility website or public Maps listing was confirmed for this centre during research. The Synod's main website (anglicanwestmalaysia.org.my) references a Homes & Centres directory under Anglican Care but does not publish operational details for individual locations. Pricing is not published — contact for a quote. Visiting hours are not published — confirm when booking a viewing. JKM Registered — licence number: T/PJB WE 005/2024.

title: Harmopeace Oldfolks Home
editorial_summary:
Harmopeace Oldfolks Home (平和養老院) is a non-profit residential care facility set on five acres in Gopeng, Perak, operated under the auspices of the True Buddha School community. The two-storey home accommodates up to 100 residents and provides both long-term elderly care and end-of-life support within a Buddhist-values framework. It is managed by a volunteer committee chaired by Lecturer Lianhua Ximei (蓮花細妹).

The home provides residential elderly care and end-of-life support (臨終關懷) in a quiet, low-density setting that stands apart from urban nursing homes. Daily life integrates True Buddha School teachings and spiritual practice, making it particularly suited to residents and families who share that faith tradition or are comfortable in a Buddhist environment. The generous five-acre grounds offer a calm, unhurried pace of care that families visiting from urban centres often find reassuring.

Pricing and visiting hours are not listed on the website; families should call +605-359 5353 or email tbspinghe@gmail.com to make enquiries. As a community-based non-profit, the home operates with a volunteer governance structure and a donation-supported model — ask directly about monthly fees and whether financial assistance is available. No JKM or MOH licence information is publicly listed; confirm registration status on your visit.

**What to ask on your visit:**
- What are the monthly fees, and is there a sliding scale or subsidy for families in financial need?
- Is the facility registered with JKM or MOH, and can we see the licence and last inspection report?
- What are the visiting hours, and are there any restrictions during religious observances?
- How are residents' medical needs managed — is there a visiting doctor, and how are emergencies handled overnight?
- How is end-of-life care delivered, and what spiritual support is offered to residents and families?
- Are residents of other faiths or no faith accepted, and how is the daily religious environment managed?
address: Lot 69252, Jalan Kota Bharu, 31600 Gopeng, Perak
